STORMCAST ETERNALS Tis Spearhead army consists of the following units: GENERAL Г‚ВЌ Yndrasta, the Celestial Spear UNITS Г‚ВЌ 1 Knight-Vexillor Г‚ВЌ 3 Annihilators Г‚ВЌ 5 Vanquishers Г‚ВЌ 5 Vanquishers Г‚ВЌ Stormstrike Chariot Te warriors under YndrastaГўв‚¬s command ensure that the enemy overextend themselves before the killing strike is dealt. Tat deadly blow is almost always struck by SigmarГўв‚¬s Huntress herself. It is her spear that plunges into the heart of the enemyГўв‚¬s most monstrous champion, and it is her hand that raises their decapitated head to the skies to the thunder of SigmarГўв‚¬s approval. Yndrasta is one of SigmarГўв‚¬s mightiest champions, her role to hunt down the deadliest and most evil of foes. Her Spearhead formation works in two distinct elements. Te ГЇВ¬ВЃrst is based around a small force of Vanquishers that strike from the heavens in a blinding column of lightning. At their head, a Knight-Vexillor raises a magical Banner of Apotheosis to heal any wounds they suГЇВ¬в‚¬er, while a Stormstrike Chariot adds some serious punch to their charge. When the foe musters a counter-attack, its commanders and warbeasts seeking to overrun the Stormcast Eternals infantry, the second part of this dual assault descends in a blaze of force. Here the Annihilators are in their element, their meteoric charge crushing all but the most formidable foes. Yndrasta, waiting for the foe to reveal their hand, is ofen the last to strike Гўв‚¬" and when she does so, she is quite capable of slaying even a rampaging gargant with a series of pinpoint spear-thrusts. YNDRASTAГўв‚¬S SPEARHEAD Гўв‚¬ЛњLet the vengeance of the God-King blast these fools from ourГ‚ sight. Attack!Гўв‚¬ Гўв‚¬" Yndrasta, the Celestial Spear

STORMCAST ETERNALS Tis Spearhead army consists of the following units: GENERAL Г‚ВЌ Lord-Vigilant on Gryph-stalker UNITS Г‚ВЌ 1 Lord-Veritant Г‚ВЌ 3 Prosecutors Г‚ВЌ 5 Liberators Only when the hordes of Chaos threaten to choke all life from the realms does the God-King call upon the Vigilant Brotherhoods. Tese are elite formations led by Stormcasts of the Ruination chambers, warriors who have seen battle so many times and whose souls have been so eroded that they are close to pure vessels of SigmarГўв‚¬s will. Tough their lack of humanity renders them ruthless, they are nigh incorruptible, their sense of self too diminished for Chaos to ГЇВ¬ВЃnd true purchase. Tis allows them to traverse lands that are so twisted that other warriors would soon wither or mutate upon entering them. Each Vigilant Brotherhood is led by a Lord-Vigilant. Tese masterful commanders are hewn from centuries of service, and they tread the thin line between preserving humanity and achieving victory at all costs. Each is chosen by their Gryph-stalker mount, creatures born under the waxing of the fell moon Dharroth and that can detect which Stormcast souls are most resistant to entropy. Swooping above them come Prosecutors wielding ГЇВ¬вЂљaming sigmarite spears. Tough each is an exemplar of SigmarГўв‚¬s creed, their exposure to the Cursed Skies has transformed them into shells of their former selves, leading them to pursue the eradication of Chaos at the expense of all else. A Lord-Veritant always accompanies each Vigilant Brotherhood, keeping watch over the state of their comradesГўв‚¬ fading identities whilst adding their considerable might to the force. Te ГЇВ¬ВЃnal element of the Brotherhood is a core of Liberators, unyielding champions of Order who have perished again and again in defence of the Mortal Realms. VIGILANT BROTHERHOOD

SPEARHEAD REFERENCE DEPLOYMENT Attacker sets up their army first, followed by the defender. Each unit must be set up wholly within friendly territory and more than 6" from enemy territory. SEIZING THE INITIATIVE If the player who went second in the previous battle round wins the priority roll and chooses to go first in the current battle round, they do not draw any battle tactic cards for that battle round unless they are the underdog and the difference in victory points between the players is 5 or more. VICTORY POINTS At the end of each of your turns: Гўв‚¬Вў	 Score 1 victory point if you control at least one objective. Гўв‚¬Вў	 Score 1 victory point if you control two or more objectives. Гўв‚¬Вў	 Score 1 victory point if you control more objectives than your opponent. Гўв‚¬Вў	 Score 1 victory point for each battle tactic you completed this turn. BATTLE TACTICS CARDS Гўв‚¬Вў	 Start of first battle round: draw 3 battle tactic cards. Гўв‚¬Вў	 Start of second or later battle round: discard any battle tactic cards in your hand if you wish. Then if you have less than 3 battle tactic cards in your hand, keep drawing them until you have 3. START OF BATTLE ROUND SEQUENCE Гўв‚¬Вў	 First battle round: attacker chooses who takes the first turn. Later battle rounds: players make a priority roll (CoreГ‚ Rules, 12.0). Гўв‚¬Вў	 Determine underdog (Core Rules, 12.0). Гўв‚¬Вў	 Draw twist card. Гўв‚¬Вў	 Players draw battle tactic cards (see below). Гўв‚¬Вў	  Start of Battle Round  abilities are used. CALL FOR REINFORCEMENTS Declare: Pick a friendly Reinforcements unit that has been destroyed. Effect: Set up an identical replacement unit on the battlefield, wholly within friendly territory, wholly within 6" of the battlefield edge and notГ‚ in combat. Each Reinforcements unit can only be replaced once. Replacement units cannot themselves be replaced. Keywords Core Once Per Turn, Your Movement Phase TERRAIN Large Terrain Feature: Cover, Obscuring, Unstable Small Terrain Feature: Cover, Unstable Cover: Subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that target a unit that is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless that unit charged or has the Fly keyword. Obscuring: A unit cannot be targeted by shooting attacks if it is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless it has the Fly keyword. Unstable: Models can move across but cannot be set up on or end any type of move on any part of this terrain feature that is more than 1" tall. PRE-BATTLE SEQUENCE Гўв‚¬Вў	 Roll off. Winner chooses who is attacker and who is defender. Гўв‚¬Вў	 Attacker picks regiment ability and enhancement, then defender does so. Гўв‚¬Вў	 Defender chooses Aqshy or Ghyran side of realm battlefield. Гўв‚¬Вў	 Defender picks deployment map (horizontal or diagonal) and chooses which territory is theirs. Гўв‚¬Вў	 Defender sets up 1 large and 1 small terrain feature. Then attacker does the same. Each terrain feature must be set up wholly within friendly territory, more than 6" from all other terrain features and more than 3" from both long battlefield edges and enemy territory. Terrain features cannot be set up on objectives. Г‚В© Copyright Games Workshop Limited 2024

UNIVERSAL CORE ABILITIES NORMAL MOVE Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not inГ‚ combat to use this ability. Effect: That unit can move a distance up to its Move characteristic. That unit cannot move into combat during any part of that move. Keywords Core, Move RUN Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not inГ‚ combat to use this ability. Effect: Make a run roll of D6. That unit can move a distance up to its Move characteristic added to the run roll. That unit cannot move into combat during any part of that move. Keywords Core, Move, Run RETREAT Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is inГ‚ combat to use this ability. Effect: Inflict D3 mortal damage on that unit. That unit can move a distance up to its Move characteristic. That unit can move through the combat ranges of any enemy units but cannot end that move within an enemy unitГўв‚¬s combat range. Keywords Core, Move, Retreat Your Movement Phase Your Movement Phase Your Movement Phase CHARGE Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not inГ‚ combat and has not used a Run or Retreat ability this turn to use this ability. Then, make a charge roll of 2D6. Effect: That unit can move a distance up to the value of the charge roll. That unit can move through the combat ranges of any enemy units and must end that move within Г‚ВЅ" of an enemy unit. If it does so, the unit using this ability has charged. Keywords Core, Move, Charge FIGHT Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is inГ‚ combat or that charged this turn to use this ability. That unit can make a pileГўв‚¬'in move. Then, if that unit is in combat, you must pick one or more enemy units as the target(s) of that unitГўв‚¬s attacks. Effect: Resolve combat attacks against the target unit(s). Keywords Core, Attack, Fight SHOOT Declare: Pick a friendly unit that has not used a Run or Retreat ability this turn to use this ability. Then, pick one or more enemy units as the target(s) of that unitГўв‚¬s attacks. Effect: Resolve shooting attacks against the target unit(s). Keywords Core, Attack, Shoot Your Shooting Phase Your Charge Phase Any Combat Phase UNIVERSAL WEAPON ABILITIES Anti-X (+1 Rend): Add 1 to this weaponГўв‚¬s Rend characteristic if the target has the keyword after Гўв‚¬ЛњAnti-Гўв‚¬ or fulfils the condition after Гўв‚¬ЛњAnti-Гўв‚¬. Multiples of this ability are cumulative. For example, if a weapon has both Anti-charge (+1 Rend) and Anti-Hero (+1 Rend), then add 2 to the Rend characteristic of the weapon for attacks that target a Hero that charged in the same turn. Charge (+1 Damage): Add 1 to this weaponГўв‚¬s Damage characteristic if the attacking unit charged this turn. Companion: This weapon is not affected by abilities used by a friendly unit that affect the Attacks characteristic or the attack sequence. Crit (2 Hits): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack scores 2 hits on the target unit instead of 1. Make a wound roll for each hit. Crit (Auto-wound): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack automatically wounds the target. Make a save roll as normal. Crit (Mortal): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack inflicts mortal damage on the target unit equal to the Damage characteristic of that weapon and the attack sequence ends. Shoot in Combat: This weapon can be used to make shooting attacks even if the attacking unit is in combat. End of Turn Combat Phase Charge Phase Shooting Phase Hero Phase Movement Phase Start of Turn PHASES Г‚В© Copyright Games Workshop Limited 2024

Tis download explains how to play games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar with your collection of Citadel Miniatures. It is divided into 4 sections: THE RULES: DIGITAL DOWNLOAD CORE RULES (pg 5-27) Te Core Rules explain the basic concepts of the game, including how your units move, shoot, charge and ГЇВ¬ВЃght. Tese rules are used in all games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar. ADVANCED RULES (pg 28-40) Te Advanced Rules explain how to use special unit types like Wizards and Priests, how to ГЇВ¬ВЃll your army roster, how to score battle tactics, and more. Tis section also covers expanded rules for terrain. GLOSSARY (pg 59-64) Te Glossary is an alphabetised list of important rules terms along with summarised deГЇВ¬ВЃnitions. It is a handy reference tool if you need a quick reminder of what a particular rule does. CITADEL TERRIN LIST (pg 65-66) Te Citadel Terrain List shows you which terrain types apply to which sets of Citadel Terrain as well as clarifying how many scenery pieces each terrain feature comprises. Te rules are numbered to make it easy to look up a rule referenced elsewhere. For example, if a rule told you to see Гўв‚¬ЛњCore Rules, 1.0Гўв‚¬, you would look up the rule numbered 1.0 in the Core Rules, which happens to be Гўв‚¬ЛњCore ConceptsГўв‚¬. 3 e Core Concepts . to b

## Page 5

While some battlepacks focus on balance and competitive play, and others put the emphasis on the story, every Warhammer Age of Sigmar battle is an awe-inspiring spectacle with fully painted armies and terrain. BATTLEPACKS While the rules explain how to play Warhammer Age of Sigmar, battlepacks provide a unique experience for each of your games. Te battlepack you choose will tell you which Advanced Rules to use, and it may include additional special rules. In addition, each battlepack contains a collection of scenarios called battleplans that explain things like where the armies deploy and how to win the battle. WeГўв‚¬d recommend reading through the Core Rules ГЇВ¬ВЃrst to understand the mechanics of the game and then picking a battlepack to play. SPEARHEAD: FIREГ‚ AND JADE (pg 43-58) Spearhead is the simplest way to play Warhammer Age of Sigmar, yet it has amazing tactical depth and re-playability. Te armies used in games of Spearhead are pre-made, so you can walk straight into your nearest Warhammer store and grab a Spearhead army oГЇВ¬в‚¬ the shelf! Te Spearhead battlepack Fire and Jade includes rules for you and your opponent to ГЇВ¬ВЃght battles in the lush, overgrown jungles of Ghyran and on the ГЇВ¬ВЃery lava plains of Aqshy. MATCHED PLAY: BORDER WAR (pg 41-42) Matched Play is a gaming mode designed for balance and equal victory conditions for both players. Te Border War battlepack (a truncated version of the First Blood battlepack in the Core Book) introduces you to Matched Play, providing conditions that give no particular advantage to either side in order to see which player is the canniest commander. 4 CORE RULES

CORE RULES 1.0 CORE CONCEPTS Battles in Warhammer Age of Sigmar are fought on a surface that is referred to as the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld. Each battle uses a scenario called a battleplan, which will explain how to set up the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld and how to determine the winner. Each player is the commander of an army. Each army belongs to a y.y faction and is made up of groups of models called units. Models and units in your army are referred to as friendly models and units, and models and units in y y your opponentГўв‚¬s army are referred to as enemy models and units. y y 2.0 TOOLS OF WAR To ГЇВ¬ВЃ ght a battle, you will need: Гўв‚¬Вў A tape measure  Гўв‚¬Вў  A surface to play on (the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld) Гўв‚¬Вў Some dice Гўв‚¬Вў 2 armies of Citadel Miniatures 2.1 MEASURING DISTANCES Distances in Warhammer Age of Sigmar are measured in inches ("), between the closest points on the bases of the models youГўв‚¬re measuring to and from. You can measure distances whenever you wish. When measuring the distance between units, always measure the distance between the closest points on the bases of the closest models in each unit. If a model does not have a base, measure to and from the closest point on the model instead. 2.1.1 WITHIN AND WHOLLY WITHIN A model is within a certain distance of something (e.g. another unit, a territory or an objective) if any part of its base is within that distance. A model is wholly within a certain distance of something if every part of its base is within that distance. A unit is within a certain distance of something if any part of the base of any model in the unit is within that distance. A unit is wholly within a certain distance of something if every part of the bases of all of the models in the unit is within that distance. 2.2 DICE Warhammer Age of Sigmar uses six-sided dice, of en abbreviated to D6. Гўв‚¬Вў  Some rules refer to Гўв‚¬Лњ2D6Гўв‚¬, Гўв‚¬Лњ3D6Гўв‚¬ and so on Гўв‚¬" in such cases, roll a number of dice equal to the number before Гўв‚¬ЛњD6Гўв‚¬ and add the results together. Гўв‚¬Вў  A roll of Гўв‚¬Лњ2+Гўв‚¬ means a roll of 2 or more, a roll of Гўв‚¬Лњ3+Гўв‚¬ means a roll of 3 or more, and so on. Гўв‚¬Вў  If a rule requires you to roll a D3, roll a dice and halve the total, rounding up. For D3 rolls, a roll of Гўв‚¬Лњ2+Гўв‚¬ means a result of 2 or more af er halving the total and rounding up. Гўв‚¬Вў  EГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects that allow you to modify a dice roll are called modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers. Гўв‚¬Вў  Some rules allow you to re-roll a dice roll, which means you get to roll some or all of the dice again. You cannot re-roll a dice more than once, and re-rolls happen before modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers to the roll (if any) are applied. Гўв‚¬Вў  If you are instructed to roll oГЇВ¬в‚¬ , each player should roll a dice. ГЇВ¬в‚¬, ГЇВ¬в‚¬ Whichever player has a higher roll wins the roll-oГЇВ¬в‚¬ . If the result is a tie, each player should roll oГЇВ¬в‚¬  again until there is a clear winner, unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed. THE MOST T IMPORTANTГ‚ RULE I While youГўв‚¬re playing W Warhammer Age of Sigmar, W you might encounter a rules y situation that you arenГўв‚¬t sure s how to resolve. If youГўв‚¬re new h o the game, we recommend t discussing with your d opponent and agreeing on a o fair and reasonable solution f so you can get on with the s game as quickly as possible. g Otherwise, the O Special Rules section includes detailed s nstructions on how to resolve in hese situations. t RULES UPDATES R We are committed to W supporting Warhammer s Age of Sigmar rules via A regular free updates based r on community feedback. o Please check P warhammer- community.com c to download he latest updates and FAQs. t T is book also includes the T corrections from the oГЇВ¬Ж’  cial c errata for the rules at the time e of going to print. o RULES SUMMARIES Г‚ВЌ Many pages in the rules have red boxes in the sidebar, such as this one. 5.0 ABILITIES Te vast majority of things that units can do in Warhammer Age of Sigmar are called abilities. Tis is an example of an ability: Timing Name and description Declare instructions EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect Keywords 5.1 KEYWORDS Some abilities have one or more keywords listed at the bottom of the ability on their keywords bar, e.g. the Гўв‚¬ЛњShootГўв‚¬ ability shown above has the Core, Attack and Shoot keywords. Units also have a keywords bar on their warscroll. Keywords let you know which abilities can be used or which units can be picked as targets for an ability. For example, the Гўв‚¬ЛњChargeГўв‚¬ ability can only be used by a unit if it did not use an ability with the Run or Retreat keyword earlier in the turn. Te singular and plural forms of a keyword are synonymous for rules purposes. 4 1 2 3 Ability icons give you an idea what kind of eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect the ability has at a glance. Movement Ability OГЇВ¬в‚¬ensive Ability Defensive Ability Shooting Ability Rallying Ability Special Ability Control Ability SHOOT: Te warriors take aim and unleash a deadly volley. Declare: Pick a friendly unit that has not used a Run or Retreat ability this turn to use this ability. Ten, pick one or more enemy units as the target(s) of that unitГўв‚¬s attacks (see 16.0). EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Resolve shooting attacks against the target unit(s). Keywords Core, Attack, Shoot Your Shooting Phase 5.2 USING ABILITIES Te timing of an ability tells you when it can be used. When using an ability, follow these steps: 1. Declare the Ability: Tell your opponent which ability is being used. If the ability has Declare instructions, resolve them at this step. 2. Use Reactions: Starting with the player using the ability, the players alternate using any abilities with an appropriate Reaction timing. Players can choose to pass instead of using a reaction, but once both players consecutively pass, no further reactions to that ability can be used. 3. Resolve the EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Follow the instructions in the EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect part of the ability. 5.3 THE RULES OF ONE Гўв‚¬Вў A unit cannot use more than 1 Core ability per phase (see 14.0). Гўв‚¬Вў A unit cannot use the same ability more than once per phase, unless speciГЇВ¬ВЃed otherwise. Гўв‚¬Вў A unit cannot be aГЇВ¬в‚¬ected by the same passive ability more than once at the same time. For example, if a unit is within range of two diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent terrain features that have the Гўв‚¬ЛњCoverГўв‚¬ passive ability, the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect only applies to it once. 5.4 PASSIVE ABILITIES Abilities that have the Passive timing are called passive abilities. Passive abilities are not declared. Te eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects of passive abilities always apply if the conditions of the ability are met, and they must be applied if it is possible to do so. 5 2 3 4 1 5 CORE RULES

6.0 VISIBILITY A target model is visible to another model (which weГўв‚¬ll call the Гўв‚¬Лњobserving modelГўв‚¬) if you can draw a straight line through the air (whether horizontal, diagonal or vertical) from any point on the observing model to any point on the target model that does not intersect any objects except for other models in the observing modelГўв‚¬s unit. A target unit is visible to an observing unit if at least 1 model in the target unit is visible to at least 1 model in the observing unit. A model is always considered to be visible to itself. Г‚ВЌ If any part of another model can be seen by an observing model, both the target model and its unit are visible to that observing model. T e Liberator at the back of this unit can see through their fellows. Models donГўв‚¬t block the visibility of other models in their unit Гўв‚¬" it is assumed that they ГЇВ¬ВЃ ght in such a way as to not obstruct one anotherГўв‚¬s attacks. ГЇВ¬ВЃ An observing modelГўв‚¬s visibility can be blocked by enemy models. T e A Plaguebearer in this shot is not visible to the Liberator because it is hidden by the Great Unclean One. Even though only half of this Liberator can be seen, he counts as being visible. A model is visible no matter how much of the model is blocked from sight or A how little of it is visible; if any part of the model can be seen, it is visible. n some cases, it might not be I mmediately clear whether a im model is visible. If so, stoop m down to get a look from d behind the observing model. b f any part of the other model I s visible, even if it is just the is ip of a spear, then that model t s visible for rules purposes. is CORE RULES 9

## Page 11

7.0 COMBAT R NGE Each model has a combat range that extends 3" horizontally from its base and any distance vertically from that circle to form a cylinder. T e combat range of a unit extends 3" horizontally and any distance vertically from every model in that unit. Units from opposing armies that are within each otherГўв‚¬s combat range and that are visible to each other are inГ‚ combat with each other. When a unit that is not in combat enters the combat range of a visible enemy unit, it movesГ‚ into combat. A model is considered to be in combat with an enemy unit if that unit is within the modelГўв‚¬s combat range and visible to it. A modelГўв‚¬s combat range extends 3" horizontally outwards from it in all directions and an inГЇВ¬ВЃ nite distance vertically. Note that this is measured from the edge of its base, not the centre. Г‚ВЌ A unitГўв‚¬s combat range extends 3" out from every model in that unit. Г‚ВЌ If any enemy models are within a unitГўв‚¬s combat range and visible to it, that unit is in combat. A unitГўв‚¬s combat range extends 3" horizontally and any distance vertically from all models in the unit. As you can see, this forms a sort of Гўв‚¬ЛњcloudГўв‚¬. Even though both Rat Ogors are within the combat range of the Liberator, the Rat Ogor behind the wall is not visible to the Liberator and is therefore not in combat. A unit is in combat with a visible enemy unit if any of its nit if models are within the combat range of that enemy unit. Even though 1 Rat Ogor is not within the LiberatorsГўв‚¬ combat range, its unit is still in combat. ГЇЖ’Вј ГЇЖ’В» 3" 3" 3" 3" 3" CORE RULES 10

10.0 THE DEPLOYMENT PHASE Once the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld is ready, the players must deploy their armies. T is is y y done in a special phase called the deployment phase. T e deployment phase happens once per battle, before the start of the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst battle round (see 12.0). STEP 1 Гўв‚¬" DEPLOY FACTION TERR IN FEATURES Some factions have faction terrain features, which have their own warscrolls and are set up using Deploy Terrain abilities. T e player who begins deployment must use Deploy Terrain abilities ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst, followed by their opponent. STEP 2 Гўв‚¬" DEPLOY ARMIES Unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed in the battleplan, the players alternate using Deploy abilities to deploy their units, starting with the player who begins deployment. Once one player has no more Deploy abilities to use, their opponent must continue to use Deploy abilities until they also have no more to use. STEP 3 Гўв‚¬" USE DEPLOYMENT PHASE ABILITIES Af er both players have ГЇВ¬ВЃ nished deploying their units, the player who begins deployment can use any Г‚ DeploymentГ‚ PhaseГ‚  abilities that are not Deploy or Deploy Terrain abilities, in the order of their choosing, then their opponent can do the same. 10.1 UNIVERSAL DEPLOYMENT PHASE ABILITIES DEPLOY FACTION TERR IN: T e army has taken to the ГЇВ¬ВЃ eld surrounding an important and powerful landmark. Declare: Pick a friendly faction terrain feature that has not been deployed to be the target. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect: Set up the target faction terrain feature wholly within friendly territory, more than 3" from all objectives and other terrain features. Af er you have done so, it has been deployed. Keywords Deploy Terrain Deployment Phase DEPLOY REGIMENT: A mighty hero leads their retinue into battle. Declare: Pick a regiment from your army roster to be the target. No units in that regiment can have already been deployed. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect: Keep using Deploy abilities without alternating until all units in that regiment have been deployed. You cannot pick units that are not in that regiment as the target of any of those Deploy abilities. Keywords Deploy Deployment Phase DEPLOY UNIT: Additional forces take to the ГЇВ¬ВЃ eld. Declare: Pick a unit from your army roster that has not been deployed to be the target. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect: Set up the target unit wholly within friendly territory and more than 9" from enemy territory. Af er you have done so, it has been deployed. Keywords Deploy Deployment Phase A regiment is a collection of units. It is one of the main building blocks of an army (seeГ‚ Гўв‚¬ЛњArmy CompositionГўв‚¬ in the Advanced Rules). Some factions have special Deploy abilities, such as the Гўв‚¬ЛњT e Celestial RealmГўв‚¬ ability of the Stormcast Eternals. Г‚ВЌ Player who begins deployment can use any Deploy Terrain abilities, followed by their opponent. Г‚ВЌ Players alternate using Deploy abilities. Г‚ВЌ Player who begins deployment must use any other Deployment Phase abilities, then their opponent can do so. CORE RULES 12

11.0 BATTLE SEQUENCE Each battle lasts a number of battle rounds, speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed in the battleplan. During each battle round, each player will take a turn. Each turn is broken down into multiple phases (see 13.0). 12.0 START OF BATTLE ROUND At the start of each battle round, follow these steps: 1.  Determine the Active Player: y If it is the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst battle round, the player who ГЇВ¬ВЃ nished setting up their army ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst decides who will take the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst turn (unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed in the battlepack or battleplan). If it is not the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst battle round, the players make a roll-oГЇВ¬в‚¬  called the priorityГ‚ roll and the winner decides who will take the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst turn. If the roll-oГЇВ¬в‚¬  is a tie, the player who took the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst turn in the previous battle round decides who will take the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst turn in the current battle round. When it is a playerГўв‚¬s turn, regardless of whether they take the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst or second turn, they are referred to as the active player. 2. Determine the Underdog:g Whichever player has the fewest victory points is the underdog for the battle round. If the players are tied (e.g. in the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst g battle round), then there is no underdog, unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed. 3. Start of Battle Round Abilities: T e active player can use any Г‚ StartГ‚ ofГ‚ BattleГ‚ RoundГ‚  abilities ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst, then their opponent can do the same. 13.0 TURN PHASES Each playerГўв‚¬s turn is broken down into 7 phases, as shown on the lef . In each phase, the active player can use any abilities with the Гўв‚¬ЛњYourГ‚ (Гўв‚¬В¦)Г‚ PhaseГўв‚¬ or Гўв‚¬ЛњAny (Гўв‚¬В¦) Phase Гўв‚¬ЛњA Гўв‚¬Лњ Гўв‚¬ timing in the order of their choosing. For example, in the Movement Phase, the active player could use abilities with the Г‚ YourГ‚ MovementГ‚ PhaseГ‚  or Г‚ AnyГ‚ MovementГ‚ PhaseГ‚  timing. Once the active player has ГЇВ¬ВЃ nished using abilities, their opponent can then use any abilities with an Гўв‚¬ЛњEnemy (Гўв‚¬В¦) PhaseГўв‚¬ or Гўв‚¬ЛњAny (Гўв‚¬В¦) Phase Гўв‚¬ЛњA Гўв‚¬Лњ Гўв‚¬ timing, in the order of their choosing. 13.1 FIGHT ABILITIES Abilities with the Fight keyword follow a diГЇВ¬в‚¬ erent sequence to other abilities. When the players are using  CombatГ‚ Phase  abilities as described in 13.0, they cannot use Fight abilities. Af er the players have ГЇВ¬ВЃ nished using  CombatГ‚ Phase abilities that are not Fight abilities, they must alternate picking 1 eligible unit to use a Fight ability, starting with the active player. Each unit in combat must use a Fight ability if it is able to. Once a player has no more units that are eligible to use a Fight ability, the other player continues to pick units that are eligible to use a Fight ability, one af er another, until there are no more units that are eligible to use a Fight ability. THE BATTLE Г‚ВЌ T e battle lasts a ГЇВ¬ВЃ xed number of battle rounds. Г‚ВЌ Each battle round contains 2 turns. Г‚ВЌ T e player whose turn is taking place is the active player. Г‚ВЌ Each turn contains 7 phases. Г‚ВЌ In each phase, the active player uses abilities ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst, then their opponent does the same. Г‚ВЌ T e active player uses Combat Phase abilities that are not Fight abilities, then the opponent does the same. Г‚ВЌ Players alternate picking a unit to use a Fight ability, starting with the active player. Г‚ВЌ Each unit must use a Fight ability if it is able to. Active playerГўв‚¬s CombatГ‚ Phase abilities (not Fight) OpponentГўв‚¬s CombatГ‚ Phase abilities (not Fight) Players alternate using Fight abilities, starting with the active player Start of Turn Hero Phase Movement Phase Shooting Phase Charge Phase Combat Phase End of Turn CORE RULES 13

## Page 16

MOVEMENT 15.0 MOVEMENT Some abilities allow a unit to move. When a unit moves, each model in that unit can move, one at a time, in the order chosen by that unitГўв‚¬s commander. When a model moves, it can pivot and change direction as of en as you like, but no part of that modelГўв‚¬s base can move a greater total distance than the maximum allowed by the ability used. Models cannot move beyond the edge of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld or through other models, and they can never end a move standing on top of another model (the base counts as part of the model). A modelГўв‚¬s base is assumed to remain parallel with the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld as it moves. If a model ends its move on an uneven surface, it must be able to stand up by itself with its base as close to parallel with the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld as possible (in other words, models cannot lie ГЇВ¬вЂљ at on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld or lean against terrain to stop them from falling over). Г‚ВЌ When moving a unit, move any number of models in that unit. Г‚ВЌ Each model can turn and pivot, but no part of its base can move farther than the maximum distance. Г‚ВЌ Units can move any distance up to the maximum speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed in the ability. Г‚ВЌ Units must end their move in coherency. Г‚ВЌ Af er ГЇВ¬ВЃ nishing a move, a unit must be in a single group. Г‚ВЌ Coherency range is Г‚ВЅ" horizontally. Г‚ВЌ Each model must be within coherency range of a diГЇВ¬в‚¬ erent model from the same unit. Г‚ВЌ While a unit has 7+ models, each model must be in coherency with 2 other models in the unit. 15.1 COHERENCY Any time a unit is set up or ends a move, it must be in a single group. A unit is considered to be in a coherent group if each model in that unit is within coherency range, measured horizontally, of at least 1 other model in that unit (ignore diГЇВ¬в‚¬ erences in height between the two models). For the majority of units, coherency range is Г‚ВЅ", though some units (particularly those with large models with overhanging parts) have a longer coherency range noted on their warscroll for ease of play. While there are 7 or more models in a unit, that unit is considered to be in a coherent group if each model in that unit is within coherency range of atГ‚ least 2 other models in that unit. If it is not possible for a unit to end a move in a single coherent group, that move cannot be made. T is Grey Seer is using the Гўв‚¬ЛњNormal MoveГўв‚¬ ability to change its position on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld. No part of that move can be within the combat range of the Liberators, so the Grey Seer has to stay outside the area marked in blue. 6" CORE RULES 15

As a model moves across terrain, its base is assumed to stay in contact with the terrain feature and parallel to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld. Models can climb up or down terrain. T ey can jump down too, but this ends their move. 15.2 MOVING ACROSS TERR IN When a model moves, it can move over terrain features but not through them. A model can be moved over terrain features that are 1" or less in height as if they were not there. A model can be moved vertically in order to climb up or down any terrain features that are taller than 1", counting the vertical distance as part of its move. Models cannot end a move mid-climb. 15.2.1 JUMPING DOWN When a model that is on a terrain feature moves, it can jump down from the edge of the terrain feature to land on a lower part of the same or a diГЇВ¬в‚¬ erent terrain feature or to land on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld. To do so, you must trace the path of the modelГўв‚¬s move to the edge of the terrain feature. T en, that model can jump down any distance, but doing so immediately ends their move. Models cannot end any kind of move mid-jump Гўв‚¬" if it is not possible to end the move as a result, that move cannot be made. FLY: Soaring above the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld, this unit can reach destinations that are inaccessible to other troops. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect: As this unit moves, it ignores other models, terrain features and the combat ranges of enemy units. It cannot end its move in combat unless speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed in the ability that allowed it to move. Ignore any vertical distance moved for this unit. Passive Г‚ВЌ Models can move freely over terrain features 1" or less in height. Г‚ВЌ Models can climb up and down terrain features, and they can jump down from a higher ledge. Г‚ВЌ Models cannot end a move mid-climb or mid-jump. Г‚ВЌ Pile in: move up to 3". Г‚ВЌ If in combat, the unit must end the move closer, or at least as close, to the target enemy unit. 15.3 PILE-IN MOVES Some abilities, such as Fight abilities, allow a unit to make a short move called a pile-in move to get into a better position for combat. To do so: If your unit is in combat: y Pick an enemy unit your unit is in combat with to be the target of the pile-in move. Each model in your unit can move up to 3". T at move can pass through the combat ranges of any enemy units, but each model must end that move no further from the target unit. At the end of the move, your unit must still be in combat with all units that it was in combat with at the start of the move. If your unit is not in combat: y Each model in your unit can move 3" in any direction. T at move can pass through and end within the combat ranges of any enemy units. 15.4 FLYING Units with the Fly keyword have the following passive ability: When moving ГЇВ¬вЂљ ying units, move them horizontally in any direction, ignoring intervening models and terrain, and place them where you wish, so long as they are allowed to end their move on that spot. Note that some units have the Fly keyword even if that unit canГўв‚¬t really ГЇВ¬вЂљ y. T is of en represents units that bounce, bound or skitter across the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld so adeptly that they might as well be ГЇВ¬вЂљ ying! CORE RULES 16

16.0 PICKING TARGETS When declaring an Attack ability for a unit, you must pick the target unit(s) for its attacks. T e number of attacks each model can make is equal to the Attacks characteristic of the weapons it is using. In most cases, models attack with every weapon on their warscroll (melee weapons for combat attacks, ranged weapons for shooting attacks), though some warscrolls specify that certain models in the unit are armed with special weapons or that the unit must pick between multiple weapons when it attacks. If the unit is in combat, it can only target units that are in combat with it. If a model has more than one attack, you can split the attacks between eligible targets as you wish. Гўв‚¬Вў Combat attacks are made with melee weapons. T e target unit(s) must be within the combat range of the attacking model and visible to it. T e model must attack with all of the melee weapons it is armed with. Гўв‚¬Вў Shooting attacks g are made with ranged weapons. T e target unit(s) must be within a distance equal to the Range characteristic of the weapon being used and visible to the attacking model. Models cannot make shooting attacks if their unit is in combat, unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed (see 20.0 Weapon Abilities). 17.0 THE ATTACK SEQUENCE Resolve steps 1-4 below for each attack made against a target unit, one attack at a time. If you picked more than one target unit for the Attack ability, resolve all the attacks made against one unit before moving on to the next, in an order of your choosing. Once you are familiar with the attack sequence, see Гўв‚¬ЛњFast Dice RollingГўв‚¬ (see 17.3) if you want to speed up play. 1.  Hit Roll: Roll a dice. If the roll equals or exceeds the attacking weaponГўв‚¬s Hit characteristic, the attack scores a successful hit: move on to the next step. If not, the attack fails and the attack sequence ends. UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed hit rolls of 1 always fail. If an unmodiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed hit roll for an attack made with a weapon is a 6, that attack is a critical hit. 2.  Wound Roll: Roll a dice. If the roll equals or exceeds the attacking weaponГўв‚¬s Wound characteristic, the attack successfully wounds: move on to the next step. If not, the attack fails and the attack sequence ends. UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed wound rolls of 1 always fail. 3.  Save Roll: T e commander of the target unit rolls a dice, subtracting the attacking weaponГўв‚¬s Rend characteristic from the roll. UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed save rolls of 1 always fail. If the roll equals or exceeds the Save characteristic of the target unit, the attack fails and the attack sequence ends. If not, it is a successful attack: move on to the next step. 4. Determine Damage: g  T e attack inГЇВ¬вЂљ icts a number of damageГ‚ points on the unit equal to the Damage characteristic of the weapon. InГЇВ¬вЂљ icted damage points are added to a temporary damage pool for the target unit. Af er completing these steps for all of the attacks made as part of that Attack ability, the attacks for that ability are resolved and you can move on to the damage sequence (see 18.0). ATTACKING A Critical hits have no eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect on C heir own but of en trigger t additional eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects (see 20.0 a Weapon Abilities). W Keep track of the number of K damage points in the damage d pools of target units. Placing p dice next to those units is a d handy way of doing this. h Г‚ВЌ Pick targets for all attacks when declaring an Attack ability. Г‚ВЌ If a unit is in combat, it can only attack units it is inГ‚ combat with and that are visible to it. Г‚ВЌ If making a combat attack, the target must be within the attacking modelГўв‚¬s combat range. Г‚ВЌ If making a shooting attack, the target must be visible and within the weaponГўв‚¬s Range. Г‚ВЌ A unit cannot make shooting attacks if it is in combat, unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed. CORE RULES 17

## Page 19

17.1 ATTACK MODIFIERS AND CAPS ModiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers to hit rolls, wound rolls and save rolls are capped to prevent overwhelming combinations of abilities. When making a hit roll or a wound roll, add up all positive and negative modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers that apply to the roll, then cap the result at a maximum of +1 (if positive) or a minimum of -1 (if negative). When making a save roll, add up all the positive and negative modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers that apply to the roll, then cap the result at a maximum of +1. Note that, unlike hit rolls and wound rolls, there is no cap on the amount that can be subtracted from save rolls. 17.2 MORTAL DAMAGE Some abilities inГЇВ¬вЂљ ict mortal damage. If an ability inГЇВ¬вЂљ icts mortal damage on a unit, add that number of damage points to the unitГўв‚¬s damage pool for that ability (see 18.2 Allocating Damage). 17.3 FAST DICE ROLLING In order to speed up play, it is of en possible to make several attacks at once. If you choose to do so, all of the attacks must have the same Hit, Wound, Rend and Damage characteristics, the same weapon abilities (if any Гўв‚¬" see 20.0), and they must target the same enemy unit. If this is the case, make all of the hit rolls at the same time, then all of the wound rolls and ГЇВ¬ВЃ nally all of the save rolls. T en, add up all the damage points inГЇВ¬вЂљ icted on the target unit and move on to the damage sequence (see 18.0). If the attack sequence ends for an attack made while fast dice rolling, it doesnГўв‚¬t stop the attack sequence for all the attacks, just that speciГЇВ¬ВЃ c attack.k. T e bold advance of the Stormcast Eternals enrages a pack of Rat Ogors, triggering a mad charge. T ese hulking Skaven aim to tear apart their foes with brute strength and ferocity before a counter-attack can be launched. CORE RULES 18

18.0 THE DAMAGE SEQUENCE Af er resolving the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect of any ability that inГЇВ¬вЂљ icts damage points on a unit, follow the steps below. If the ability inГЇВ¬вЂљ icted damage points on more than one unit, follow the steps below for each of those units, one at a time. 19.0 STRIKE-FIRST AND STRIKE-LAST If there are any Strike-first units in combat after any non-Fight combat phase abilities have been used, other units cannot be picked to use a Fight ability until those units have been picked to use a Fight ability. Af er all those Strike-first units have fought, the active player picks the next unit to ГЇВ¬ВЃ ght. If there are any Strike-last units in combat, they cannot be picked to use a Fight ability if there are any units in combat that do not have Strike-last and have not yet used a Fight ability. If a unit has Strike-first and Strike-last, treat it as if it had neither. 20.0 WEAPON ABILITIES Some weapons have one or more of the passive weapon abilities below. If a weapon has multiple weapon abilities that apply on a criticalГ‚ hit, before the attack sequence starts, the commander of the attacking model must pick 1 of those abilities to use. 21.0 HEALING Some abilities allow you to heal a unit. To heal a unit, remove a number of damage points from the unit equal to the number in brackets af er Гўв‚¬ЛњHealГўв‚¬, e.g. Heal (2). SPECIAL RULES UNIVERSAL WEAPON ABILITIES Anti-X (+1 Rend): Add 1 to this weaponГўв‚¬s Rend characteristic if the target has the keyword af er Гўв‚¬ЛњAnti- Гўв‚¬ЛњA Гўв‚¬Лњ Гўв‚¬ or fulГЇВ¬ВЃ ls the condition af er Гўв‚¬ЛњAnti- Гўв‚¬ЛњA Гўв‚¬Лњ Гўв‚¬. Multiples of this ability are cumulative. For example, if a weapon has both Anti-charge (+1 Rend) and Anti-Hero (+1 Rend), then add 2 to the Rend characteristic of the weapon for attacks that target a Hero that charged in the same turn. Charge (+1 Damage): Add 1 to this weaponГўв‚¬s Damage characteristic if the attacking unit charged this turn. Companion: Unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed, this weapon is not aГЇВ¬в‚¬ ected by friendly abilities that aГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect weapon characteristics or the attack sequence, except for those that apply negative modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers to it (e.g. Гўв‚¬ЛњCovering FireГўв‚¬). Crit (2 Hits): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack scores 2 hits on the target unit instead of 1. Make a wound roll for each hit. Crit (Auto-wound): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack automatically wounds the target. Make a save roll as normal. Crit (Mortal): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack inГЇВ¬вЂљ icts mortal damage on the target unit equal to the Damage characteristic of that weapon and the attack sequence ends. Shoot in Combat: T is weapon can be used to make shooting attacks even if the attacking unit is in combat. T ere may be situations when T a unit that has a Strike-first s not in combat at the start is of the phase, but because of o moves such as pile-in moves, m t is Гўв‚¬Лњpulled into combatГўв‚¬ later it n the phase. In such cases, in Strike-first S has no eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect on that unit because it was o not in combat at the start of n he phase. t Abilities that allow a unit A o use a t Fight ability mmediately af er another im unit do not override u he t Strike-first or Strike-last S constraints, so you could not pick a unit s with w Strike-last to ГЇВ¬ВЃ ght mmediately af er a unit im with w Strike-first. T e T Companion weapon ability restricts things like a mounts from beneГЇВ¬ВЃ ting from m most eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects that augment a m unitГўв‚¬s capabilities. CORE RULES

## Page 25

Г‚ВЌ Returned models must be set up in coherency with the models in their unit that were not returned as part of that ability. Г‚ВЌ Returned models can only be set up in combat with an enemy unit if their unit is already in combat with it. Tis Gryph-crow is a token. 22.0 RETURNING AND ADDINGГ‚ MODELS Some abilities allow you to return slain models to a unit or add new models to a unit. In either case, set up those models, one at a time (see 15.1) within coherency range of the model(s) in that unit that were not returned or added this turn. If that unit has 7 or more models (including the model being set up), it must be set up within coherency range of at least 2 other models in that unit that were not returned or added this turn. Te new models can only be set up in combat with an enemy unit if their unit is already in combat with that enemy unit. 23.0 TOKENS Tokens are not considered to be models for rules purposes and can be ignored or moved out of the way for purposes of visibility, coherency or abilities. Tey cannot be picked as the target of abilities. Teir purpose is to indicate persisting eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects on units they are placed next to. Each time a unit with a token ГЇВ¬ВЃnishes a move or is set up, place the token next to the unit. 24.0 SETTING UP UNITS Some abilities allow you to set up a unit on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. When doing so, you must set up all models in that unit. If this is impossible, you cannot use that ability. A unit set up on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld in a phase other than the deployment phase cannot use Move abilities in the movement phase of the same turn. 24.1 RESERVE UNITS Some abilities allow you to set up units in reserve. Tese units are placed to one side instead of being set up on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. At the start of the fourth battle round, units that were set up in reserve using a Deploy ability and that are still in reserve are destroyed. 24.2 REPLACEMENT UNITS Some abilities allow you to set up a replacement unit. When setting up that unit, it should have the same warscroll type, weapon options and number of models as the original unit, unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃed in the ability. Many of these abilities specify the proportion of models in the replacement unit (e.g. half the number of models from the original unit). In these cases, you can pick which models from the original unit are set up. Te replacement unit is otherwise treated as a new unit; any keywords or abilities the original unit gained during the battle, and any persisting eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects that applied to it, do not apply to the replacement unit. Each unit can only be replaced once, and you cannot replace replacement units. 25.0 GUARDED HEROES All Heroes that are not Monsters or War Machines have the Гўв‚¬ЛњGuardedГ‚ HeroГўв‚¬ passive ability: GUARDED HERO: Te leader is shielded by their warriors, who close ranks around them to protect them from harm. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: If this Hero is within the combat range of a friendly unit that is not a Hero: Гўв‚¬Вў Subtract 1 from hit rolls for shooting attacks that target this Hero. Гўв‚¬Вў If this Hero is Infantry, they cannot be picked as the target of shooting attacks made by models more than 12" from them. Passive Understandably, Monsters and War Machines donГўв‚¬t beneГЇВ¬ВЃt from the Гўв‚¬ЛњGuarded HeroГўв‚¬ ability. Tese units are very conspicuous targets even if they are surrounded by their fellows! Because a replacement unit is treated as a completely new unit, it could, for instance, use a Once Per Battle ability on its warscroll even if the unit it replaced used that ability earlier in the battle. CORE RULES

## Page 26

26.0 R NDOM CHAR CTERISTICS Some warscrolls show a random characteristic roll instead of a ГЇВ¬ВЃ xed value. When this is the case, the value of the characteristic is generated v by the commander of the unit in question each time an ability requires that characteristic. In the case of random weapon characteristics, generate a random Attacks characteristic for each model in the attacking unit each time you declare an Attack ability, and generate a random Damage characteristic each time you inГЇВ¬вЂљ ict damage with that weapon (roll once for each attack). 27.0 MODIFIER ORDER T e eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of some abilities modify a characteristic or roll. Unless stated otherwise, a characteristic or roll cannot be modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed to less than 1. T e exception to this is Rend, which can be modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed to a minimum of 0 (Гўв‚¬Лњ-Гўв‚¬). If a characteristic uses a random characteristic roll (see above), apply characteristic modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers af er that characteristic has been generated. If a characteristic or roll is halved or would be modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed to a value that is not a whole number, round it down to the nearest whole number. Apply characteristic modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers in the following order: 1.  ModiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers that set a characteristic to a ГЇВ¬ВЃ xed value. 2.  ModiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers that multiply or y y divide a characteristic. 3.  ModiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers that add to or subtract from a characteristic. If there are multiple modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers of the same type (e.g. that set a characteristic), apply them in the Order of EГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects (see 30.0). The effects of some abilities (e.g. Гўв‚¬ЛњAn Excess of DepravityГўв‚¬) allow you to replace a roll with a fixed value. When doing so, you must replace the roll before rolling the dice for it. Г‚ВЌ When using a random characteristic, generate it each time it is needed for an ability. Г‚ВЌ When using random Damage, generate it for each attack made. Skaven technology is infamously unpredictable. T eir warpstone-powered weapons are just as likely to belch out a S thunderous volley as they are a single forlorn shot Гўв‚¬" or they might just explode without warning! CORE RULES 25

## Page 27

28.0 ADVANCED ABILITY RULES Гўв‚¬Вў  Unless stated otherwise, units using or picked as part of an ability must be onГ‚ the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld. Гўв‚¬Вў  If an eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect states that a unit can do something, its commander can choose whether to resolve that part of the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect or not. Гўв‚¬Вў  If an eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect states that a unit must do something, its commander has no choice and must resolve that part of the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect. If this is impossible, no part of the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect is applied but the ability is still considered to have been used. Гўв‚¬Вў  When Гўв‚¬Лњthis unitГўв‚¬ appears in ability text (most of en on warscrolls), it means the unit that is using the ability. Гўв‚¬Вў  If an ability aГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects more than one unit, the player who used the ability can choose the order in which units are aГЇВ¬в‚¬ ected by it. Гўв‚¬Вў  When resolving an eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect, if you need to roll a dice for multiple aГЇВ¬в‚¬ ected units, roll and resolve the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect for one unit before moving on to the next. Гўв‚¬Вў  If an ability instructs you to pick more than 1 unit, each unit you pick must be a diГЇВ¬в‚¬ erent unit unless stated otherwise. 28.1 PERSISTING EFFECTS Some abilities have eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects that arenГўв‚¬t immediately resolved (e.g. Гўв‚¬Лњadd 1 to save rolls for this unit for the rest of the turnГўв‚¬ or Гўв‚¬Лњthis unit has WardГ‚ (5+) for the rest of the turnГўв‚¬). T ese eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects count as the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of passive abilities (see 5.4) for their duration. 28.2 Гўв‚¬ЛњONCE PERГўв‚¬ TIMINGS T e timing part of some abilities says Once Per Phase, Once Per Turn or Once Per Battle. If the ability is used by a unit, it can be used a maximum of one time in that phase, turn or battle by each unit that can use the ability. If the ability is used by a player, it can be used a maximum of one time in that timing window by that player. Some abilities that are used by units say Once Per Phase (Army), Once Per Turn (Army) or Once Per Battle (Army). T ese abilities can be used a maximum of one time in that phase, turn or battle regardless of the number of units in the army that can use the ability. 29.0 CONTR DICTORY RULES If two or more rules contradict, if one of those rules states that something cannot do something, this takes precedence over rules that state it can or must do that thing, unless the second rule speciГЇВ¬ВЃ cally overrides the restriction of the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst. For example, while the Гўв‚¬ЛњNormal MoveГўв‚¬ ability states you cannot move into combat during that move, the Гўв‚¬ЛњFlyГўв‚¬ ability speciГЇВ¬ВЃ es to ignore the combat ranges of enemy models during a move. Excepting the above, the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect of the most recently used ability takes precedence. 30.0 ORDER OF EFFECTS T e eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of passive abilities are considered to be applied more recently than the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of other abilities and rules. T e eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of the active playerГўв‚¬s passive abilities are considered to be applied more recently than the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of their opponentГўв‚¬s passive abilities, whose passiveГ‚ abilities are considered to be applied more recently than the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of neutral passive abilities (e.g. passive abilities on a terrain feature that is in neither playerГўв‚¬s army). T e active player chooses the order in which neutral passive eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects are applied. Some abilities have a delayed eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect (e.g.Г‚ Гўв‚¬Лњeach time a friendly unit uses a Fight ability, af er that ability has been resolved, Heal (D3) that unitГўв‚¬). If more than one of these eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects would be resolved at the same time, the active player resolves the delayed eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of their abilities ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst, in an order of their choosing, then their opponent does the same. WHO IS USING THEГ‚ ABILITY? Most abilities are found on warscrolls, but many appear elsewhere Гўв‚¬" the best examples being the Universal Core Abilities (see 14.0). While abilities on warscrolls are always used by the unit whose warscroll it is, some abilities that do not appear on warscrolls will tell you explicitly to pick a unit to use the ability. In both cases, it should be clear which unit is using the ability. Some abilities that do not appear on warscrolls, most commonly enhancements (see Army Composition, 4.1), are given to certain units in your army. In such cases, the unit to which the enhancement was given is the one using the ability. Abilities that neither appear on a warscroll nor tell you to pick a unit to use the ability are used by you, the player. Finally, for the purposes of the rules in this section, when a rule refers to a player using an ability, this includes abilities used by units in that playerГўв‚¬s army. CORE RULES 26

## Page 28

31.0 END OF TURN OVERVIEW At the end of each turn, follow these steps: 1.  T e active player can use any abilities with the Г‚ EndГ‚ ofГ‚ YourГ‚ TurnГ‚ or Г‚ EndГ‚ ofГ‚ AnyГ‚ TurnГ‚  timing, in the order of their choosing, then their opponent can use any abilities with the Г‚ EndГ‚ ofГ‚ EnemyГ‚ TurnГ‚ or Г‚ EndГ‚ ofГ‚ AnyГ‚ TurnГ‚  timing, in the order of their choosing. 2.  Determine which player controls each objective (if any). 3.  T e active player scores victory points as described in the battleplan. 32.0 OBJECTIVES Many battleplans award victory points for controlling objectives, which are represented by objective markers. Unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed, objective markers are round and 40mm wide. Models can move over and end their moves on objective markers. If an objective marker is on the border between territories, it is within all those territories but wholly within none of them. Objective markers donГўв‚¬t block visibility. 32.1 CONTESTING OBJECTIVES Unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed, if an objective is within a modelГўв‚¬s combat range, that model is contesting that objective. If any models in a unit are g g contesting an objective, that unit is contesting that objective. Each unit can only count as contesting a single objective for the purposes of determining objective control (see 32.2). Before determining objective control, for each of their units contesting two or more objectives, the active player must pick one of those objectives for it to contest. T en, their opponent does the same. DesignerГўв‚¬s Note: For purposes other than determining objective control, a unit can contest more than one objective. 32.2 OBJECTIVE CONTROL At the start of the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst battle round and at the end of each turn, follow this sequence for each objective in an order chosen by the active player: 1.  Starting with the active player, each player determines the control score of each of their units that is contesting that objective. A unitГўв‚¬s control score is the combined Control characteristics of all the models in that unit that are contesting the objective. Some abilities modify a unitГўв‚¬s control score, but it cannot be reduced to less than 1. 2.  Each player adds up the control scores of all of their units contesting that objective. T is is their army control score for that objective. 3.  T e players compare their army control scores for that objective. If one playerГўв‚¬s score is higher, that player gains control of that objective. Once a player gains control of an objective, it remains under their control until their opponent gains control of it. 32.3 TERR IN CONTROL Some battleplans require you to gain control of a terrain feature. Terrain features are controlled in the same way as objectives, but terrain features do not remain in your control if your units are no longer contesting them. Each unit can contest 1 objective and 1 terrain feature at the same time. 33.0 END OF BATTLE ROUND At the end of each battle round, the active player can use any Г‚ EndГ‚ ofГ‚ BattleГ‚ RoundГ‚  abilities, then their opponent can do the same. Each battleplan will specify the number of battle rounds that should be fought. If you have completed the number of battle rounds speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed in your battleplan, the battle has ended, and you should follow the rules included in that battleplan to determine the winner. END OF TURN Г‚ВЌ An objective marker is a 40mm round marker. Г‚ВЌ A model contests an objective if the objective marker is within its combat range. Г‚ВЌ A player gains control of an objective if the sum of the Control characteristics of friendly models contesting that objective is higher than that of enemy models. Г‚ВЌ Check if you gain control of objectives at the start of the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst battle round and at the end of each turn. Г‚ВЌ An objective remains in your control until your opponent gains controlГ‚ of it. Г‚ВЌ Terrain features are controlled in the same way as objective markers but do not remain in your control if no friendly models are contesting them. Sometimes objective markers S get accidentally nudged g while you are moving models w around. T is is perfectly a ГЇВ¬ВЃne Гўв‚¬" just remember to put ГЇВ¬ВЃ hem back in their proper t positions when determining p objective control. o CORE RULES 27

## Page 29

ADVANCED RULES Each Advanced Rules section is designed to be modular, allowing each battlepack to specify which Advanced Rules to use with that battlepack. Tis book contains the following Advanced Rules sections: COMMANDS (pg 29-30) Tese rules explain how you can earn command points to use powerful commands that allow you to react to abilities and interact in your opponentГўв‚¬s turn. If you are not using the Commands rules, ignore any abilities that have the command point symbol in the corner. TERRIN (pg 31-32) Tese rules go into more detail about how your models interact with terrain features, such as how they can take cover or even draw power from arcane nexuses. MAGIC (pg 33-34) If youГўв‚¬d like to wield unlimited mystical power by including Wizards, Priests and manifestations in your battles, see Magic. ARMY COMPOSITION (pg 35-38) If youГўв‚¬re ready to build your own army roster and customise your army with regiments, battle formations and enhancements, see Army Composition. COMMAND MODELS (pg 39) Here you will ГЇВ¬ВЃnd rules for special models called champions, standard bearers and musicians, which are found in many Warhammer Age of Sigmar units. BATTLE TACTICS (pg 40) Add an additional tactical challenge to your games by including secondary objectives called Battle Tactics. BUILD YOUR OWN BATTLEPACK Tere are many, many ways to play Warhammer Age of Sigmar, from Path to Glory battlepacks, which focus on narrative-driven battles linked in an ongoing campaign, to Spearhead and Matched Play battlepacks, in which the emphasis is on balance and competitive play. In addition to using oГЇВ¬Ж’cial battlepacks, weГўв‚¬d encourage you to use the rules in this section as a toolbox, mixing and matching the elements within to build your own battleplan or battlepack to play with your friends. Are you excited to ГЇВ¬ВЃeld your entire collection against your friends or to see which monster reigns supreme? Maybe you want to design your own battleplans and special rules, or perhaps you want to keep things simple in order to teach a younger sibling how to play. Tese rules provide an open framework that makes each of these things possible. Here are some ideas to get you started: Гўв‚¬Вў Instead of using the Advanced Rules for Army Composition, make an army based on your favourite Black Library story or based on a made-up scenario that sounds exciting to you. For instance, how would 5 diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent Stormcast Eternals heroes fare against a pair of massive Chaos monsters? Гўв‚¬Вў Design your own battleplan by dividing portions of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld into a territory for each player, placing objectives and thinking up a fun twist for the battle. Have fun and experiment with diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent ideas Гўв‚¬" why not try alternating placing objectives instead of having ГЇВ¬ВЃxed locations, or try having two smaller battleГЇВ¬ВЃelds connected by realmgates? Гўв‚¬Вў Learn the Advanced Rules by introducing each module into your battles one at a time Гўв‚¬" for instance, start by experimenting with Commands, then try adding Magic into your battles, and ГЇВ¬ВЃnally put them all together to outwit your opponent and score Battle Tactics. 28 ADVANCED RULES

1.0 COMMANDS OVERVIEW Some abilities, called commands, require that you spend one or more command points ( ) to use that ability. Any ability that has a command point cost (indicated in the top right corner of the ability) is a command. 1.1 EARNING COMMAND POINTS At the start of each battle round, afer determining the underdog, each player gains 4 command points. If there is an underdog, they gain 1 extra command point. At the end of the battle round, the playersГўв‚¬ command points are reset to 0 (any that have not been used are lost). 1.2 USING COMMANDS Commands are used in a similar manner to any other ability. However, each unit can only use 1 command in each phase, each command can only be used 1 time by each army in each phase, and you must spend a number of command points equal to the command point cost to use a command. 2.0 HERO PHASE COMMANDS MAGICAL INTERVENTION: A warrior learned in the mystic arts utters a rapid incantation to disrupt the enemyГўв‚¬s plans. Declare: Pick a friendly Wizard or Priest to use this ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Tat friendly unit can use a Spell or Prayer ability (as appropriate) as if it were your hero phase. If you do so, subtract 1 from casting rolls or chanting rolls made as part of that ability. RLLY: With an inspiring call to action, wounded soldiers get a second wind and fresh troops take the ГЇВ¬ВЃeld to replace their fallen comrades. Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not in combat to use this ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Make 6 rally rolls of D6. For each 4+, you receive 1 rally point. Rally points can be spent in the following ways: Гўв‚¬Вў For each rally point spent, Heal (1) that unit. Гўв‚¬Вў You can spend a number of rally points equal to the Health characteristic of that unit to return a slain model to that unit. You can spend the rally points in any combination of the above. Unspent rally points are then lost. Any Hero Phase Enemy Hero Phase d 1 d 1 3.0 MOVEMENT PHASE COMMANDS AT THE DOUBLE: At a command from their general, the warriors sprint to a key position on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. Used By: Te unit using that Run ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Do not make a run roll as part of that Run ability. Instead, add 6" to that unitГўв‚¬s Move characteristic to determine the distance each model in that unit can move as part of that Run ability. 1 Reaction: You declared a Run ability COMMANDS Г‚ВЌ Any ability with a command point cost ( ) is a command. Г‚ВЌ Each player gains 4Г‚ command points at the start of each battle round. Г‚ВЌ Te underdog gains 1 extra command point. Г‚ВЌ Each unit can only use 1 command in each phase. Г‚ВЌ Each command can only be used 1 time by each army in each phase. Г‚ВЌ You must pay the command point cost to use a command. REDEPLOY: Taking initiative, the warriors hastily reposition to respond to enemy movements. Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not inГ‚ combat to use this ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Each model in that unit can move up to D6". Tat move cannot pass through or end within the combat range of an enemy unit. Keywords Move, Run Enemy Movement Phase ly 1 29 ADVANCED RULES

ALL-OUT ATTACK: Te warriors ГЇВ¬ВЃght with a frenzied vigour. Used By: Te unit using that Attack ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Add 1 to hit rolls for attacks made as part of that Attack ability. Tis also aГЇВ¬в‚¬ects weapons that have the Companion weapon ability. 4.0 SHOOTING PHASE COMMANDS FORWARD TO VICTORY: With their charge faltering, a rousing command gives the unit renewed energy to reach their foes. Used By: Te unit using that Charge ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: You can re-roll the charge roll. Reaction: You declared a Charge ability 5.0 CHARGE PHASE COMMANDS COUNTER-CHARGE: Seeing the need for decisive action, the warriors charge forth to stall the enemyГўв‚¬s advance or enact a bold interception. Enemy Charge Phase 2 1 6.0 ATTACKING (SHOOTING AND COMBAT) COMMANDS Reaction: You declared an Attack ability 7.0 DEFENSIVE COMMANDS ALL-OUT DEFENCE: Te soldiers close ranks to hold oГЇВ¬в‚¬ an incoming attack. Ten, the unit using this ability can move a distance up to its Move characteristic. It can pass through and end that move within the combat ranges of enemy units that were in combat with it at the start of the move, but not those of other enemy units. 1.0 TERRIN OVERVIEW Terrain features add interest and tactical challenges to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. Te battleplan or battlepack you are using will explain how to set up terrain. 1.1 WHOLLY ON TERRIN AND BEHIND TERRIN A model is wholly on a terrain feature if its base is on that terrain feature and no part of its base extends past the edge of that terrain feature. A unit is wholly on a terrain feature if every model in that unit is wholly on that terrain feature. When a unit is targeted by an attack, the unit is considered to be behind a terrain feature if it is impossible to draw a straight line from a model in the attacking unit to a model in the target unit without that line passing across that terrain feature. Ignore parts of the terrain feature within the attacking unitГўв‚¬s combat range for the purposes of determining if the target is behind that terrain feature. ACTIVATE PLACE OF POWER: Te hero draws on the power of a nearby mythical landmark. Declare: Pick a friendly Hero within 3" of any Places of Power to use this ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Roll a dice. On a 1, inГЇВ¬вЂљict D3 mortal damage on that Hero. On a 2+: Гўв‚¬Вў If that Hero is a Wizard or Priest, add 1 to casting rolls or chanting rolls for that Hero this turn. Гўв‚¬Вў If that Hero is not a Wizard or Priest, they can use the Гўв‚¬ЛњUnbindГўв‚¬ or Гўв‚¬ЛњBanish ManifestationГўв‚¬ ability this turn as if they had Wizard (1). Keywords Core Start of Any Turn TERRIN 1.2 UNIVERSAL TERRIN ABILITIES Each terrain feature has one or more of the following passive abilities: 1.3 TERRIN SIZES Гўв‚¬Вў Terrain that ГЇВ¬ВЃts into an area no larger than 7" Гѓ- 7" is small. Гўв‚¬Вў Terrain that is not small and ГЇВ¬ВЃts into an area no larger than 7" Гѓ- 12" is medium. Гўв‚¬Вў Terrain that is too big to ГЇВ¬ВЃt into an area 7" Гѓ- 12" is large. 1.4 TERRIN TYPES Each terrain feature in Warhammer Age of Sigmar is one of the following types: Гўв‚¬Вў Obstacle Гўв‚¬Вў Obscuring Terrain Гўв‚¬Вў Area Terrain Гўв‚¬Вў Place of Power Гўв‚¬Вў Faction Terrain Before the battle begins, players must agree on which type applies to each terrain feature. 1.4.1 OBSTACLES Examples: Ruins, debris, statues, barricades Terrain Abilities: Cover, Unstable 1.4.2 OBSCURING TERRIN Examples: Wyldwood, fortress wall Terrain Abilities: Cover, Obscuring, Unstable 1.4.3  AREA TERRIN Examples: Hills, Stormvault Terrain Abilities: Cover 1.4.4 PLACES OF POWER Examples: Realmgate, Cleansing Aqualith, Nexus Syphon Terrain Abilities: Cover, Place of Power, Unstable Cover: Subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that target a unit that is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless that unit charged this turn or has the Fly keyword. Impassable: Models cannot move across, be set up on or end moves on any part of this terrain feature. Obscuring: A unit cannot be targeted by shooting attacks made by enemy units that are not within its combat range if it is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless it has the Fly keyword. Place of Power: Heroes within 3" of this terrain feature can use the Гўв‚¬ЛњActivate Place of PowerГўв‚¬ ability (see below). Unstable: Models can move across but cannot be set up on or end any type of move on any part of this terrain feature that is more than 1" tall. Te Citadel Terrain List (pgГ‚ 65) includes a list of Warhammer Age of Sigmar terrain features and tells you which terrain abilities each of them has. 31 ADVANCED RULES

## Page 33

1.5 FACTION TERRIN Some factions have special terrain features called faction terrain features. Faction terrain features have their own warscrolls. Tey are not considered to be units, with the following exceptions: Гўв‚¬Вў In the combat phase, they are treated as if they were units for the purposes of movement, combat range and being in combat. Units can ГЇВ¬ВЃnish a charge move within Г‚ВЅ" of an enemy faction terrain feature as if it were a unit. Гўв‚¬Вў Tey can be picked as targets of enemy abilities as if they were units. Tey are not aГЇВ¬в‚¬ected by enemy abilities that do not involve picking targets. Гўв‚¬Вў Damage points can be inГЇВ¬вЂљicted on them as if they were units and they can be destroyed. Гўв‚¬Вў If a terrain feature has any melee or ranged weapons, it can use the Гўв‚¬ЛњFightГўв‚¬ and Гўв‚¬ЛњShootГўв‚¬ Core abilities as if it were a unit. Гўв‚¬Вў Terrain features that have a Move characteristic of 0" (Гўв‚¬Лњ-Гўв‚¬) cannot move (note that pile-in moves are a type of move). Some faction terrain features allow you to place a unit on them (this will be clearly speciГЇВ¬ВЃed in that terrain featureГўв‚¬s rules). When doing so, instead of measuring range or visibility to and from the unit that is on that terrain feature, measure to and from the terrain feature instead. Unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃed, units on a faction terrain feature cannot use Fight abilities. If a unit placed on a terrain feature is removed from it (e.g. if an ability removes them from the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld), that unit counts as having lef that terrain feature and loses any special role or beneГЇВ¬ВЃts it had as a result of being placed on it. 1.6 CHARGING UNITS ON TERRIN In some cases, it is not possible to reach an enemy unit using a Charge ability when they are wholly on a terrain feature (e.g. a unit on top of a tower) because the charging unit is unable to end their move mid-way up the terrain feature and there is not enough room for the models to be placed at the top. In these cases, the charging unit can end their charge within Г‚ВЅ" of that terrain feature instead if this would result in the charging unit ending their move inГ‚ combat with any enemy units wholly on a terrain feature. 1.7 SCENERY PIECES Some terrain features, such as wyldwoods or collections of smaller timeworn ruins, are made up of multiple scenery pieces. Each scenery piece that makes up a part of the same terrain feature must be set up so that all of the scenery pieces ГЇВ¬ВЃt into the area that corresponds to the terrain featureГўв‚¬s size (see 1.3). Te full collection of scenery pieces is considered to be a single terrain feature. Te Citadel Terrain List (pgГ‚ 65) shows the number of scenery pieces each terrain feature can consist of. If you are using terrain features that are not included on that list, agree with your opponent before setting up the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld how many scenery pieces make up each terrain feature. Tis battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld uses Citadel terrain, so the players were able to simply look up the terrain type of each terrain feature in the Citadel Terrain List (pg 65). Ruined Domicile (Obstacle) Domicile Shell (Obstacle) Guardian Idol (Obstacle) Cleansing Aqualith (PlaceГ‚ of Power) Nexus Syphon (PlaceГ‚ of Power) Wyldwood (ObscuringГ‚ Terrain) 1 2 3 4 5 6 6 5 1 2 2 1 4 3 3 32 ADVANCED RULES

MAGIC 1.0 WIZARDS AND PRIESTS Wizards are special units that can cast spells, and Priests are special units that can chant prayers. Spells and prayers are powerful abilities that can have a dramatic impact on the battle. 1.1 POWER LEVEL Each Wizard and Priest has a power level, shown in brackets afer the keyword, e.g. Wizard (2). A Wizard or PriestГўв‚¬s power level determines the number of Spell, Prayer or Banish abilities they can use per phase, in any combination. For example, a Wizard (2) unit could use 1 Spell and 1 Banish ability in their commanderГўв‚¬s hero phase. 2.0 SPELLS Te declare step of each spell will tell you to make a casting roll of 2D6. If the roll does not equal or exceed the spellГўв‚¬s casting value (at the top-right corner of the spell), the spell fails and its eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect is not resolved. If the unmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed casting roll includes 2 or more rolls of 1, the spell is miscast: the spell fails, its eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect is not resolved, D3 mortal damage is inГЇВ¬вЂљicted on the Wizard that used it, and that Wizard cannot use any more spells in that phase. Enemy reactions can only be used if the casting roll equals or exceeds the spellГўв‚¬s casting value. So long as the spell is not unbound (seeГ‚ 4.0), then it is successfully cast: resolve its eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect. 3.0 PRYERS Te declare step of each prayer will tell you to make a chanting roll ofГ‚ D6. On an unmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed chanting roll of 1, the prayer fails, its eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect is not resolved and you must remove D3 ritual points from the Priest using the prayer. On an unmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed chanting roll of 2 or more, pick one of the following: Гўв‚¬Вў Give a number of ritual points to the Priest equal to the chanting roll (ritualГ‚ points can be accumulated over multiple turns). Гўв‚¬Вў Add the PriestГўв‚¬s ritual points to the chanting roll. If the chanting roll equals or exceeds the prayerГўв‚¬s chanting value (at the top-right corner of the prayer), it is answered: resolve the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect of the prayer, then reset the PriestГўв‚¬s ritual points total to 0. 4.0 UNBINDING SPELLS Each Wizard can use the Гўв‚¬ЛњUnbindГўв‚¬ reaction a number of times per phase equal to their power level. Tis is an exception to Te Rules of One (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.3). Г‚ВЌ Wizards can cast spells; Priests can chant prayers. Г‚ВЌ Each Wizard and Priest can use a number of spells or prayers per phase equal to their power level. Г‚ВЌ Each Wizard can use a number of Unbind abilities per phase equal to their power level. Г‚ВЌ Each spell or prayer can only be used once per turn. UNBIND: Te wizard saps the energy from an enemyГўв‚¬s spell, nullifying its eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects. Used By: A friendly Wizard within 30" of the enemy Wizard casting the spell. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Make an unbinding roll of 2D6. If the roll exceeds the castingГ‚ roll for the spell, then the spell is unbound and its eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect is not resolved. Tis reaction cannot be used more than once per casting roll. Keywords Unbind Reaction: Opponent declared a Spell ability MYSTIC SHIELD: Te casterГўв‚¬s allies are bathed in an unearthly glow that protects them from harm. Declare: Pick a visible friendly unit wholly within 12" of this Wizard to be the target. Ten, make a casting roll of 2D6. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Te target has Ward (6+) this turn. Keywords Spell Your Hero Phase RESURRECTION: A fallen hero is brought back from death, their vitality fully restored. Declare: Make a chanting roll of D6. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Pick a friendly Infantry Hero that has been slain and return them to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. Set up that Hero wholly within 3" of this Priest. Keywords Prayer Once Per Battle, YourГ‚ HeroГ‚ Phase 7 6 EXAMPLE SPELL EXAMPLE PRYER 33 ADVANCED RULES
