PICK YOUR REGIMENT ABILITY AND ENHANCEMENT Each Spearhead army has a choice of 2 diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent regiment abilities and 4 diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent enhancements for your general. At the start of the battle, you must pick 1 of each for your army (see Spearhead Armies, pg 49). 5. FIGHT THE BATTLE! Spearhead battles usually last around an hour and a half. During the battle, the players will each try to score victory points, and at the end of the battle, the player with the most is declared the winner! 4. PREPARE YOUR WARSCROLLS Each set of Spearhead rules includes the warscrolls for all the units used in your Spearhead army. A warscroll details how fast and tough a unit is, as well as what weapons it is armed with and any special abilities it has. Гўв‚¬Вў Every Spearhead army has a set of battle traits, regiment abilities and enhancements. Гўв‚¬Вў Te battle traits are rules and abilities for your army that you will use in every battle. Гўв‚¬Вў Te regiment abilities and enhancements are picked at the start of every battle. You can experiment with diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent combinations to see what works best for you. Some regiment abilities and enhancements are better against certain foes, so it pays to study the strengths and weaknesses of the enemy before making your choice! Гўв‚¬Вў Every unit in a Spearhead army has its own warscroll, where you can see its characteristics and weapons. Гўв‚¬Вў Any unique abilities the unit can use will also be found on its warscroll. Гўв‚¬Вў At the bottom of every warscroll you can ГЇВ¬ВЃnd its keywords, such as Hero, Wizard, Monster or Infantry. 48 SPEARHEAD

Any Movement Phase 10 SPEARHEAD BATTLEPACK FIRE AND JADE 1.0 SPEARHEAD ARMIES Each player in a Spearhead battle is the commander of a Spearhead army. A Spearhead army is a set of units that comes with its own rules and warscrolls. You can download the rules for 25 diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent Spearhead armies for free from warhammer-community.com. 1.1 ARMY COMPOSITION On the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst page of every Spearhead armyГўв‚¬s rules, you will ГЇВ¬ВЃnd the army composition. Tis lists which units are included in the Spearhead army and who the general is. Unlike other Warhammer Age of Sigmar armies, the composition of a Spearhead army is ГЇВ¬ВЃxed and cannot be changed. 1.2 BATTLE TRITS Every Spearhead army has one or more battle traits. Tese are powerful and thematic abilities that apply to some or all of the units in the army. For example, the Гўв‚¬ЛњHoly OrdersГўв‚¬ battle trait enables the Stormcast Eternals to use powerful once-per-battle abilities to smite their foes, while the Гўв‚¬ЛњGnawhole AmbushГўв‚¬ battle trait allows Skaven warriors to arrive on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld behind enemy lines. 1.3 REGIMENT ABILITIES Every Spearhead army has a choice of 2 regiment abilities, each of which provides the army with a diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent strength in battle. 1.4 ENHANCEMENTS Every Spearhead army has a set of 4 enhancements to pick from. Tese are upgrades for the armyГўв‚¬s general that make them more powerful in a variety of ways. 1.5 SPEARHEAD WARSCROLLS Te warscrolls for the units in each Spearhead army are included with the other rules for the army. Although Spearhead warscrolls are similar to the warscrolls found in battletomes, they have been tailored to work speciГЇВ¬ВЃcally with Spearhead and cannot be used with other battlepacks. WIZARDS AND PRIESTS IN SPEARHEAD Some units in Spearhead have the Wizard or Priest keyword. If you are familiar with other battlepacks, it is important to note the Magic module in the Advanced Rules is not used in Spearhead. As a result, there are no unbinding rolls and no chance of miscasts (among other things). Spells and prayers are resolved like any other ability. THE CORE RULES To play a game using this battlepack, youГўв‚¬ll need the Core Rules. Te Core Rules explain the basics of Warhammer Age of Sigmar, such as how to move your models and how to use abilities. You can ГЇВ¬ВЃnd the Core Rules on pages 5-27. You donГўв‚¬t need the Advanced Rules to use this battlepack. 49 SPEARHEAD

HY TWIST Гўв‚¬Вў PRE-BATTLE SEQUENCE 1. Roll oГЇВ¬в‚¬. Te winner chooses who is the attacker and who is the defender. 2.  Te attacker picks their regiment ability and their enhancement. TenГ‚ the defender does the same. 3.  Te defender chooses which side of the realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld the players will ГЇВ¬ВЃght on: Aqshy or Ghyran. 4.  Te defender picks 1 of the deployment maps on the right and chooses which territory belongs to which player. 5.  Te defender sets up their terrain features, followed by the attacker. Each terrain feature must be set up wholly within friendly territory, more than 6" from all other terrain features and more than 3" from both long battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld edges and enemy territory. Terrain features cannot be set up on top of the objectives (either wholly or partially). THY LIFE IS A WEAPON Гўв‚¬" WIELD IT WELL TERRITORY A TERRITORY A TERRITORY B TERRITORY B BATTLEPLAN FIRE AND JADE As the Hour of Ruin dawns, the ГЇВ¬ВЃght to secure sacred sites and geomantic nexuses rages ГЇВ¬ВЃercer than ever. It is in Aqshy and Ghyran that the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst battles of this new age are fought, each faction sending out vanguard forces to take and hold these places of power in the name of their deities Гўв‚¬" or even the godbeasts of myth. So unpredictable are these frontiers of war that the land itself can lend its blessing to the chosen or send wracking curses against those who incur its wrath. DEPLOYMENT Do not deploy armies as described in the Core Rules. Instead, the attacker sets up all the units in their army ГЇВ¬ВЃrst, followed by the defender. Each unit must be set up wholly within friendly territory and more than 6" from enemy territory. BATTLE LENGTH Te battle lasts for 4 battle rounds. SPECIAL RULES PREPARING THE SPEARHEAD DECK Tis battle uses the Fire and Jade Spearhead deck. One player takes the twist deck that corresponds to the side of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld the players are ГЇВ¬ВЃghting on, shuГЇВ¬вЂћes it and places it face down next to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld (the other twist deck is not used). Ten, each player takes 1 battle tactic deck, shuГЇВ¬вЂћes it and places it face down where they can reach it during the battle. 55 SPEARHEAD TACTIC Гўв‚¬Вў AND Гўв‚¬Вў e warriors assume lunts the blows of geted by that acteristic of his phase. Reaction: Opponent declared an Attack ability nemy territory and actic at the end of your re wholly within n combat. START OF THE BATTLE ROUND SEQUENCE Te following sequence is resolved at the start of eachГ‚ battle round: 1.  If it is the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst battle round, the attacker chooses who will take the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst turn. In any other battle round, the players make a priority roll (CoreГ‚ Rules, 12.0). 2.  Determine the underdog (CoreГ‚ Rules, 12.0). 3. Te twist card is drawn (pg 50). 4.  Te players draw battle tactic cards (see below). 5.  Any abilities with the Г‚ StartГ‚ ofГ‚ BattleГ‚ RoundГ‚  timing are used. TWIST CARDS One twist card is drawn at the start of each battle round. Follow the rules on it as soon as it is drawn. BATTLE TACTIC CARDS At the start of the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst battle round, each player draws a hand of 3Г‚ battleГ‚ tacticГ‚ cards. At the start of each subsequent battle round, each player draws battle tactic cards until they once more have a hand of 3 battle tactic cards. Before they do so, they can discard any number of battle tactic cards inГ‚ their hand. Cards are discarded face-up. VICTORY POINTS Each player scores victory points at the end of each of their turns as follows: Гўв‚¬Вў  Score 1 victory point if you control at least one objective. Гўв‚¬Вў  Score 1 victory point if you control two or more objectives. Гўв‚¬Вў  Score 1 victory point if you control more objectives than your opponent. Гўв‚¬Вў  Score 1 victory point for each battle tactic you completed this turn. GLORIOUS VICTORY Te player with the most victory points at the end of the battle is the winner. If the players are tied on victory points, the battle is a draw. SEIZING THE INITIATIVE If the player who went second in the previous battle round wins the priority roll and chooses to go ГЇВ¬ВЃrst, it is called seizing the initiative. When a player seizes the initiative, they do not draw any battle tactic cards for that battle round unless they are the underdog and the diГЇВ¬в‚¬erence in victory points between the two players is 5 or greater. 56 SPEARHEAD orneГўв‚¬s presence is blood be spilled y point at the nit destroyed in ers is the iendly unit Add 1 to hit hat unit this T Гўв‚¬Вў ST Гўв‚¬Вў ESSING: Te uch of Nurgle, ted by sputum, e eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects below. rs roll oГЇВ¬в‚¬ and ГЇВ¬в‚¬ect. unit on the ls for that unit unit on the made for that IC Гўв‚¬Вў he nd to not in D6". Reaction: Opponent declared an Attack ability my territory and c at the end of your wholly within combat. 2.0 REALM BATTLEFIELDS Spearhead battles are fought on realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃelds. Once the players have picked their Spearhead armies, they must agree on a realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld to use. Each realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld comes with a matching SpearheadГ‚ deck, along with a battleplan that explains how to ГЇВ¬ВЃght a battle on that realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. A realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld is 30"Г‚ Гѓ-Г‚ 22" and double-sided, with each side corresponding to a diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent Mortal Realm. Te ГЇВ¬ВЃrst realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld is Fire and Jade, which has one side representing Aqshy, the Realm of Fire, and the y,y reverse representing Ghyran, the Realm of Life. 3.0 SPEARHEAD DECKS Spearhead decks are sets of cards that are used in Spearhead battles. Each realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld has its own Spearhead deck. Each SpearheadГ‚ deck consists of 2 twist decks (one for each side of the realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld) and 2 battle tactic decks (one for each player). 3.1 TWIST CARDS Each twist deck is made up of k twist cards. A twist is a special rule that applies to the battle round being played. Each deck of twist cards corresponds to a speciГЇВ¬ВЃc side of the realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. 3.2 BATTLE TACTIC CARDS Each battle tactic deck is made up of k k battle tactic cards. Te rules on each are split into 2 parts: a battle tactic and a command. You can either score the battle tactic at the end of your turn if you meet its conditions (to gain victory points) or you can use the command on the card for a more immediate beneГЇВ¬ВЃt. You cannot do both, so choose wisely! Each command will say when it can be used and what eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect it has. Afer the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect has been resolved, the card is discarded. You can issue multiple commands to the same unit in the same phase. 4.0 SPEARHEAD BATTLEPLANS Each realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld has a corresponding battleplan. Te battleplan is a set of instructions on how to ГЇВ¬ВЃght the battle and how to determine the winner. You can ГЇВ¬ВЃnd the battleplan for the Fire and Jade realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld on pages 55-56. 50 SPEARHEAD

## Page 51

5.0 TERRIN In every Spearhead battle, the armies ГЇВ¬ВЃght around 2Г‚ large terrain features and 2 small terrain features. Before the armies are deployed, each player will set up 1 large terrain feature and 1 small terrain feature wholly within their territory. Each terrain feature has certain passive abilities depending onГ‚ its size: Large Terrain Feature: Cover, Obscuring, Unstable Small Terrain Feature: Cover, Unstable COVER: Subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that target a unit that is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless that unit charged or has the Fly keyword. OBSCURING: A unit cannot be targeted by shooting attacks if it is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless it has the Fly keyword. UNSTABLE: Models can move across but cannot be set up on or end any type of move on any part of this terrain feature that is more than 1" tall. Ruined Domicile (Large Terrain Feature) Ravaged Shrine (Small Terrain Feature) Ruined Domicile (Large Terrain Feature) Ravaged Shrine (Small Terrain Feature) 51 SPEARHEAD

Each objective is named afer a mythical godbeast, as shown below: D r a c o t h i o n V u l c a t r i x N a g e n d r a I g n a x B e h e m a t 7.0 REINFORCEMENTS Some units have the Reinforcements keyword. Tese units can be brought back to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld afer being destroyed as replacement units (CoreГ‚ Rules, 24.2). If any of your units have the Reinforcements keyword, you can use the following ability: CALL FOR REINFORCEMENTS: A second wave of troops enters the fray, replacing those lost to the rigours of battle. Declare: Pick a friendly Reinforcements unit that has been destroyed. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Set up an identical replacement unit on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld, wholly within friendly territory, wholly within 6" of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld edge and notГ‚ in combat. Each Reinforcements unit can only be replaced once. Replacement units cannot themselves be replaced. Keywords Core Once Per Turn, Your Movement Phase 6.0 OBJECTIVES In Spearhead battles, objectives are printed on the surface of each realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld (the objective is the whole circle, not just the symbol in the centre). 6.1 CONTESTING OBJECTIVES In Spearhead battles, while a model is on an objective (wholly or partially), it is contesting it. Units with the Reinforcements keyword have this icon in the top-right corner of their warscrolls for ease of use. 52 SPEARHEAD

## Page 53

A SPEARHEAD BATTLE Below you can see a game of Spearhead that is underway. Upon the scorched plains of the Great Parch in Aqshy, a force of Stormcast Eternals clashes with a Skaven clawpack that seeks to bring ruin to a Dawnbringer outpost. Tis unit of Clanrats has been destroyed but it can return to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld because their warscroll has the Reinforcements keyword. Flying units, such as these Prosecutors, can soar directly over other models and terrain, meaning they are very good at capturing objectives at the lastГ‚ moment. A playerГўв‚¬s battle tactics are normally kept secret from their opponent. Te large terrain features block visibility, so units can hide behind them Гўв‚¬" but not from units with Fly! 8 8 9 9 10 10 1 1 2 53 SPEARHEAD

## Page 54

Te twist deck is placed at the side of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld in reach of both players. Each battle round, a twist card is drawn, which can lead to interesting opportunities or hazards for either player. Generally, twists favour the underdog. Because he has a ranged weapon in the form of a Warplock Musket, the Warlock Engineer can hang back and shoot whilst also contesting an objective. Each player has a hand of battle tactic cards, which tell them what they should be trying to achieve. Battle tactics can instead be used as commands for a more immediate beneГЇВ¬ВЃt. Te unit of Liberators is contesting the Dracothion objective in the centre of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld, but the Clanrats to their ГЇВ¬вЂљank are in a good position to charge and swarm the objective. Each Spearhead army has a general, a powerful unit that has access to enhancements. Here, a Lord-Vigilant astride a Gryph-stalker is locked in a duel with a devious Clawlord mounted on a Gnaw-beast. Six-sided dice and measuring sticks (or tape measures) are your essential tools in any Warhammer Age of Sigmar battle. Te 3" measuring stick is perfect for measuring combatГ‚ ranges. 6 6 7 7 5 5 4 4 3 3 2 54 SPEARHEAD

Keywords Core, Attack, Shoot Your Shooting Phase Your Charge Phase Any Combat Phase UNIVERSAL WEAPON ABILITIES Anti-X (+1 Rend): Add 1 to this weaponГўв‚¬s Rend characteristic if the target has the keyword afer Гўв‚¬ЛњAnti-Гўв‚¬ or fulГЇВ¬ВЃls the condition afer Гўв‚¬ЛњAnti-Гўв‚¬. Multiples of this ability are cumulative. For example, if a weapon has both Anti-charge (+1 Rend) and Anti-Hero (+1 Rend), then add 2 to the Rend characteristic of the weapon for attacks that target a Hero that charged in the same turn. Charge (+1 Damage): Add 1 to this weaponГўв‚¬s Damage characteristic if the attacking unit charged this turn. Companion: Tis weapon is not aГЇВ¬в‚¬ected by abilities used by a friendly unit that aГЇВ¬в‚¬ect the Attacks characteristic or the attack sequence. Crit (2 Hits): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack scores 2 hits on the target unit instead of 1. Make a wound roll for each hit. Crit (Auto-wound): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack automatically wounds the target. Make a save roll as normal. Crit (Mortal): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack inГЇВ¬вЂљicts mortal damage on the target unit equal to the Damage characteristic of that weapon and the attack sequence ends. Shoot in Combat: Tis weapon can be used to make shooting attacks even if the attacking unit is in combat. 58 SPEARHEAD

## Page 59

GLOSSARY abilities: Te things that units can do in the game. Some abilities, like Гўв‚¬ЛњNormal MoveГўв‚¬ and Гўв‚¬ЛњFightГўв‚¬, are common to all units; others are speciГЇВ¬ВЃc to certain units and only appear on their warscroll. Sometimes the players themselves use abilities. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.0) active player: Te player whose turn is taking place. Tere are 2 turns in a battle round. Te active player can use abilities with the Гўв‚¬ЛњYour PhaseГўв‚¬ timing. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 12.0) allocate: Damage points are allocated one at a time to a unit afer all the damage points inГЇВ¬вЂљicted by an ability have been totalled in the damage pool. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 18.2) All-out Attack: Command, 1CP, reaction. Attacking unit adds 1 to hit rolls. (Commands, 6.0) All-out Defence: Command, 1CP, reaction. Target unit adds 1 to save rolls. (Commands, 7.0) Anti-X (+1 Rend): Weapon ability. +1 Rend for attacks that target units that fulГЇВ¬ВЃl the condition afer Гўв‚¬ЛњAnti-Гўв‚¬. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) Any (Гўв‚¬В¦) Phase: Timing. Either player can use abilities with this timing, regardless of who the active player is. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 13.0) Army of Renown: A special type of army that uses a modiГЇВ¬ВЃed set of faction rules. (Army Composition, 2.2) army: All of a playerГўв‚¬s units are collectively referred to as their army. (Core Rules, 1.0) army composition: Te step before a battle in which the players build their armies. Te battlepack may specify in more detail what they can and canГўв‚¬t include in their armies. army roster: Te list of units in a playerГўв‚¬s army, and any other important information like enhancements. Players can check each otherГўв‚¬s rosters any time they like. (Army Composition, 1.1) artefact of power: Enhancement. You can give 1 Hero in your army an artefact of power. (Army Composition, 4.1) At the Double: Command, 1CP, reaction. Add 6 to Move instead of making the run roll. (Commands, 3.0) attack sequence: Make hit roll, wound roll and save roll, then determine damage points inГЇВ¬вЂљicted. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.0) auxiliary unit: A unit on your army roster that is not part of a regiment. (Army Composition, 3.6) Banish Manifestation: Your Hero Phase, 30", banishment roll of 2D6. Equal or beat banishment value of the manifestation to remove it from play. (Magic, 7.2) battle proГЇВ¬ВЃle: Important information about a unit that you will need to refer to if you wish to include it in your army, such as its points value and minimum unit size. (Army Composition, 1.3) battle round: Te battle lasts a number of battle rounds speciГЇВ¬ВЃed in the battleplan. Each battle round has 2 turns (1 for each player), and each turn has 7 phases. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 11.0) battle tactics: Attempt by using the Гўв‚¬ЛњTactical GambitГўв‚¬ ability at the start of your turn. Complete at the end of your turn for 4 victory points. (Battle Tactics, 1.0) battle trait: A type of faction rule that usually aГЇВ¬в‚¬ects your army as a whole and reГЇВ¬вЂљects the way your faction does battle, as opposed to individual units. battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld: Te ГЇВ¬вЂљat surface on which the battle is fought. Usually has 2 short edges and 2 long edges, which may collectively be referred to as Гўв‚¬Лњthe battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld edgeГўв‚¬. It is divided into 4 large quarters, each of which is divided into 4 small quarters (eГЇВ¬в‚¬ectively sixteenths). battleplan: Te set of rules that describes the scenario of the battle and determines things such as where the armies deploy, where any objectives are located and how the players win the battle. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 9.0) begins deployment: Te player who is allowed to use a Deploy ability during deployment ГЇВ¬ВЃrst is said to be the one who begins deployment. Te battleplan will specify which player does so. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 10.0) behind a terrain feature: Impossible to draw a line from a model in the attacking unit to a model in the target unit without it passing across the terrain feature. (Terrain, 1.1) Cavalry: A unit keyword. Mounted on warbeasts. Cavalry are fast and hard-hitting but are generally vulnerable to attritional combat. Champion: ModelГўв‚¬s melee weapons have +1 to Attacks characteristic. (Command Models, 1.0) Charge: Your Charge Phase, charge roll of 2D6. Must get within Г‚ВЅ" of any visible enemy units to succeed, otherwise unit stays where it is. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 14.3) Tis glossary is an alphabetised list of rules deГЇВ¬ВЃnitions. It is intended to be a useful resource if you need a quick reminder of what a rule does or a short summary of a game term. It is not intended as an exhaustive list of precise deГЇВ¬ВЃnitions Гўв‚¬" if you need the full rule, refer to the relevant rules module. If there are any contradictions between this glossary and a rules module, the rules module takes precedence. 59

Charge (+1 Damage): Weapon ability. +1 Damage if attacking unit charged. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) climb: Movement vertically up or down the surface of a terrain feature. Te modelГўв‚¬s base is assumed to stay in contact with the terrain feature as it moves and remain parallel with the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld ГЇВ¬вЂљoor. (Core rules, 15.2) coherency: When a unit is set up or ends a move, each model in the unit must be within coherency range. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 15.1) coherency range: Г‚ВЅ" horizontally and any distance vertically of 1 or more other models if unit has 6 or fewer models; 2 or more if unit has 7+ models. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 15.1) combat attack: An attack made with a melee weapon. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 16.0) combat range: ModelГўв‚¬s combat range = 3" horizontally and any distance vertically from model. UnitГўв‚¬s combat range = 3" horizontally and any distance vertically from all models in unit. A unit is in combat if any enemy models are within its combat range. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 7.0) command points: Each player gets 4 command points at start of round. Underdog gets 1 extra. (Commands, 1.0) commander: You are the commander of your army and everything in it (units, models, etc.). commands: Abilities that require you to spend a number of command points in order to use them. (Commands, 1.0) Companion: Weapon ability. Weapon doesnГўв‚¬t get bonuses from friendly abilities that aГЇВ¬в‚¬ect attacks. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) contesting: Models contest objectives in their combat range (3"). Te same unit cannot contest more than 1 objective at the same time; the player must pick 1 if more than 1 is within range. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 32.1) control: Determined at the start of the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst battle round and the end of each turn. Objectives are controlled by player that has the highest army control score for that objective. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 32.2) Control characteristic: Determines how good unit is at contesting objectives (see Гўв‚¬ЛњcontrolГўв‚¬). (CoreГ‚ Rules, 4.0) control score: A unitГўв‚¬s control score is determined by adding all the Control characteristics of models in the unit contesting the objective in question. An armyГўв‚¬s control score for that objective is determined by adding together all the control scores of units in the army contesting that objective. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 32.2) Core: Basic ability that can only be used by each unit once per phase, e.g. Move, Shoot, Fight. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.1) Counter-charge: Command, 1CP. Charge in enemy charge phase. (Commands, 5.0) Cover: -1 to hit rolls for attacks that target a unit behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless unit charged or has Fly. (Terrain, 1.2) Covering Fire: Command, 1CP. Shoot in enemy shooting phase, but -1 to hit rolls and must target nearest enemy unit. (Commands, 4.0) Crit (2 Hits): Weapon ability. UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed hit roll of 6 scores 2 hits on target. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) Crit (Auto-wound): Weapon ability. UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed hit roll of 6 skips wound roll step. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) Crit (Mortal): Weapon ability. UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed hit roll of 6 inГЇВ¬вЂљicts an amount of mortal damage equal to the Damage characteristic of the weapon and the sequence ends. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) critical hit: UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed hit roll of 6. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.0) D3: Roll a D6 and halve the result, rounding up. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 2.2) damaged: A unit is damaged if it has any damage points currently allocated to it (i.e. if the damage points allocated to it were not enough to slay a model in the unit). Some abilities Heal damaged units. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 18.2) Damage characteristic: Determines number of damage points inГЇВ¬вЂљicted by successful attack. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.0) damage points: Damage points are inГЇВ¬вЂљicted by successful attacks and mortal damage. Damage points go into a damage pool ГЇВ¬ВЃrst, then they must be allocated one at a time to the target unit. Each time the number of damage points allocated to a unit equals its Health characteristic, a model in the unit is slain. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.0) damage pool: InГЇВ¬вЂљicted damage points are added to the targetГўв‚¬s damage pool until all damage points inГЇВ¬вЂљicted by the ability have been calculated. Ten, each of those damage points must be allocated one at a time to the target unit. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.0) Declare: Te step of every ability afer which players may use reactions but before the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect is resolved. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.2) deployment phase: Phase in which players use Deploy abilities to deploy their armies. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 10.0) destroyed: A unit is destroyed when the last model in the unit is slain. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 18.4) destroyed by: A unit is said to have destroyed another unit when an attack it made or an ability it used caused the last model in the target unit to be slain. eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Abilities have eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects, which are resolved afer the declare and reaction step. Te thing to which the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect is applied is said to be aГЇВ¬в‚¬ected by the ability. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.2) endless spells: A type of manifestation summoned by a Wizard. (Magic, 7.0) 60

enemy models/units: Models/ units in your opponentГўв‚¬s army. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 1.0) Enemy (Гўв‚¬В¦) Phase: Timing. A phase in which your opponent is the active player. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 13.0) enhancements: A type of faction rule. You can take 1 enhancement of each type for your army, you canГўв‚¬t take the same enhancement more than once, and you canГўв‚¬t give the same unit more than 1 enhancement of the same type. (Army Composition, 4.1) faction: Te units in your army must all belong to the same faction (excepting Regiments of Renown). Most factions in Warhammer Age of Sigmar have their own battletome, which contains the rules for the units from that faction, along with the battle traits and enhancements for that faction. faction rules: Tese are split into battle traits and enhancements. Battle traits tend to be army-wide abilities, while enhancements are given to speciГЇВ¬ВЃc units. Fight: Te Core ability that units use to attack each other in combat. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 14.4) Fly: A keyword. Units that can ГЇВ¬вЂљy ofen ignore intervening models and terrain when moving. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 15.4) Forward to Victory: Command, 1CP, reaction. Re-roll charge roll. (Commands, 5.0) friendly models/units: Models/ units in your army. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 1.0) general: You must pick 1 Hero in your army to be your general. Certain rules will interact with your general. (Army Composition, 3.2) Guarded Hero: Passive ability that all Heroes have. If Hero is within 3" of a friendly non-Hero unit, then shooting attacks targeting the Hero are -1 to hit, and if Hero is Infantry, they also cannot be picked as the target of shooting attacks made by units more than 12" from them. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 25.0) Heal: Remove a number of damage points that have been allocated to a unit equal to the number in brackets afer Гўв‚¬ЛњHealГўв‚¬. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 21.0) Health characteristic: Te characteristic that shows the number of damage points that can be allocated to the unit before a model in the unit is slain. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 4.0) heroic trait: Enhancement that can be given to a Hero in your army. (Army Composition, 4.1) hit roll modiГЇВ¬ВЃers: Add up all modiГЇВ¬ВЃers to a hit roll and cap at +1 if the result is a positive value or -1 if the result is a negative value. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.1) in combat: A unit is in combat if any visible enemy models are within its combat range (within 3"). (CoreГ‚ Rules, 7.0) Infantry: A unit keyword. Models on foot. Infantry form the backbone of most armies and tend to be good at contesting objectives. inГЇВ¬вЂљict: Successful attacks inГЇВ¬вЂљict damage points. InГЇВ¬вЂљict is not the same as allocate. Ward rolls can be made for inГЇВ¬вЂљicted damage points before they are allocated. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.0) invocation: A type of manifestation summoned by a Priest. (Magic, 7.0) jump down: Models can move directly down through the air from a terrain feature to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. Tey can do so any distance, but it ends their move (CoreГ‚ Rules, 15.2.1) keyword: A term that carries Keyword Format, allowing the thing that has it to be tagged and referenced by other rules. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.1) Magical Intervention: Command, 1CP. Wizard or Priest can cast or chant in enemy hero phase, but -1 to casting/chanting roll. (Commands, 2.0) manifestations: Arcane or divine entities or phenomena that have their own miniatures and are summoned to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld by a Wizard or Priest. (Magic, 7.0) manifestation lore: If taken, all Wizards in your army know all spells from that lore, and all Priests in your army know all prayers from that lore. (Army Composition, 4.2) miscast: Casting roll that contains two or more 1s is a miscast (D3 mortal damage, attempt fails, and caster canГўв‚¬t cast any more spells that phase). (Magic, 2.0) models: Te warriors in your army are referred to as models and are grouped into units. Some units consist of only 1 model. modiГЇВ¬ВЃers (dice rolls): Many rules let you change the result of a dice roll. Sometimes, multiple modiГЇВ¬ВЃers will apply to the same roll. ModiГЇВ¬ВЃers to some types of rolls, such as hit and wound rolls, are capped. modiГЇВ¬ВЃers (characteristics): Characteristics cannot be modiГЇВ¬ВЃed to less than 0. Round down any fractions. Set, multiply or divide, then add or subtract, in that order (if more than one modiГЇВ¬ВЃer applies). (Core rules, 27.0) Monster: A unit keyword. Monsters are large and imposing units, ofen consisting of a single model that can have a large impact all by itself. mortal damage: If mortal damage is inГЇВ¬вЂљicted, just add that many damage points to the damage pool. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.2) 61

mount trait: Enhancement that can be given to a Hero riding a warbeast. (Army Composition, 4.1) Move characteristic: Characteristic that determines the number of inches a unit can move across the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld when using certain abilities, most commonly Normal Move. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 4.0) Musician: While unit contains any musicians, if it uses the Гўв‚¬ЛњRallyГўв‚¬ command, make one additional rally roll of D6. (Command Models, 2.0) neutral territory: Te area of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld that is neither playerГўв‚¬s territory. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 9.1.2) Normal Move: Move each model in unit up to Move characteristic but models cannot move into combat. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 14.1) objectives: Many battleplans require players to gain control of speciГЇВ¬ВЃc points on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld to secure victory. Objectives are represented by 40mm round markers. Models can move over and end a move on an objective marker. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 32.0) Obscuring: Unit behind or wholly on this terrain feature cannot be targeted by shooting attacks unless it has Fly. (Terrain, 1.2) passive abilities: Te eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects of passive abilities are applied any time their conditions are met. You cannot use reactions in response to passive abilities. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.4) persisting abilities: Abilities that have eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects that last for a speciГЇВ¬ВЃed length of time, for example, Гўв‚¬Лњadd 1 to wound rolls for attacks made by this unit until the start of your next turn.Гўв‚¬ phases: Tere are 7 phases in each turn: Start of Turn, Hero Phase, Movement Phase, Shooting Phase, Charge Phase, Combat Phase, End of Turn. Ability timings can be Your Phase (you are the active player), Enemy Phase (opponent is active player) or Any Phase (either player is active player). (CoreГ‚ Rules, 13.0) pile-in: A short move, usually 3", that allows a unit using a Fight ability to get a bit closer to the unit(s) it is in combat with. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 15.3) points limit: Te combined points cost of units in your army cannot exceed the points limit for the battle. Players usually agree on the points limit before the battle, but it may be pre-determined by the battlepack being used. (Army Composition, 1.2) power level: Te number in brackets afer the Wizard or Priest keyword that determines the number of spells or prayers they can cast or unbind per phase. (Magic, 1.1) prayer: A special type of ability that can only be used by Priests. (Magic, 3.0) prayer lore: If taken, all eligible Priests in your army know the prayers from that lore. (Army Composition, 4.2) Priest: Unit that can use prayers. (Magic, 1.0) Rally: Command, Any Hero Phase, 1CP. Roll 6 dice; for each 4+, gain 1 rally point. For each rally point, Heal (1) or spend a number of rally points equal to Health characteristic to return 1 slain model. (Commands, 2.0) random characteristic: Generate the characteristic before applying modiГЇВ¬ВЃers, and each time it is needed for an ability. Random Attacks characteristics are generated per Attack ability; other random weapon characteristics are generated per attack made with the weapon. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 26.0) Range characteristic: A characteristic, most ofen on weapon proГЇВ¬ВЃles, that determines how close something must be for it to be an eligible target. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 16.0) Reaction: Afer an ability is declared, players alternate using any eligible reactions they wish to. Ten the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect of the ability is resolved. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.2) Redeploy: Command, 1CP. D6" move in enemy movement phase. (Commands, 3.0) regiment: A group of units, including a Hero, that are taken as a set during Army Composition. Regiments ofen allow multiple units to be deployed at the same time. (Army Composition, 3.1) Regiment of Renown: A ГЇВ¬ВЃxed regiment that has its own rules for including it in an army and may also have a special ability or abilities. (Army Composition, 3.5) reinforced unit: A unit whose minimum unit size and points cost is doubled when you add it to your army. You cannot reinforce units with a minimum unit size of 1. (Army Composition, 3.3) replacement unit: A unit that is brought back to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld afer it has been destroyed. Although the same miniatures are used, the unit counts as an entirely new unit in the rules, unless speciГЇВ¬ВЃed otherwise. (Core Rules, 24.2) Rend characteristic: Subtract the Rend of the weapon from save rolls made for the target unit. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.0) reserves/in reserve: A unit set up in reserve is set up oГЇВ¬в‚¬ the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld and will be set up on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld at a speciГЇВ¬ВЃed time once the battle is under way. (Core Rules, 24.1) Retreat: InГЇВ¬вЂљict D3 mortal damage on the unit then move it out of combat. Cannot use Shoot or Charge abilities later in turn. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 14.1) return slain model: Set up model that was slain, in coherency with its unit and with no damage points allocated to it. Only set up in combat if its unit is already in combat. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 22.0) 62

roll oГЇВ¬в‚¬: Each player rolls a D6; player who rolls highest wins. If tied, keep rolling oГЇВ¬в‚¬ until there is a winner. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 2.2) Run: Roll D6 and add to unitГўв‚¬s Move, then move unit up to that distance. Prevents unit from using Shoot or Charge later in turn. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 14.1) Save characteristic: Te number you need to equal or beat to make a successful save roll for the unit. Attacks that are saved do not inГЇВ¬вЂљict damage points. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 4.0) save roll modiГЇВ¬ВЃers: Save roll modiГЇВ¬ВЃers are capped at +1 but there is no negative cap. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.1) set up: When setting up  a unit, all models in the unit must be set up, otherwise the ability cannot be used. A unit set up on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld in a phase other than the deployment phase cannot use Move abilities in the movement phase of the same turn. (Special Rules, 24.0) Shoot: Attack with ranged weapons. Units cannot shoot if they are in combat. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 14.2) Shoot in Combat: Weapon ability. Weapon can be used to make shooting attacks even if attacking unit is in combat. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) spell: A special type of ability that can only be used by Wizards. (Magic, 2.0) spell lore: If taken, all eligible Wizards in your army know the spells from that lore. (Army Composition, 4.2) slain: A model in a unit is slain each time the number of damage points allocated to the unit equals its Health characteristic. Each time a model is slain, the number of damage points allocated to the unit resets to 0. If there are still damage points in the damage pool, keep allocating them and removing slain models until there are no damage points lef. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 18.2) slain by: A model is said to have been slain by a unit when an attack made by or an ability used by that unit caused that model to be slain. Standard Bearer: Add 1 to the control score of units with any standard bearers. (Command Models, 3.0) straight line: If a rule asks you to draw a line between two points, it is assumed to be an imaginary line 1mm wide. Strike-first: Units with this keyword use Fight abilities before units that donГўв‚¬t have this keyword. If both players have units with Strike-first, they alternate ГЇВ¬ВЃghting with those units before other units get to ГЇВ¬ВЃght, starting with the active player. Afer those units have fought, the active player chooses the next unit to ГЇВ¬ВЃght. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 19.0) Strike-last: Units with this keyword use Fight abilities afer all units that donГўв‚¬t have this keyword. If both players have units with Strike-last, they alternate ГЇВ¬ВЃghting with those units afer all other eligible units have fought, starting with the active player. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 19.0) summon: Manifestations are summoned to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld using the spell or prayer on their warscroll. (Magic, 7.0) Tactical Gambit: You must use this ability at the start of your turn in order to attempt a battle tactic. (Battle Tactics, 2.0) target (attacks): You must declare the targets of all the attacks you are making with a unit before following the attack sequence for those attacks, and you can split attacks freely between eligible target units. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 16.0) terrain features: Models that reГЇВ¬вЂљect the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld environment. Tey ofen have their own rules. (Terrain, 1.0) territory (within/wholly within): A unit is within a territory if any part of the base of any model in the unit is within that territory. A unit is wholly within a territory if all parts of the bases of all models in the unit are within that territory. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 9.1.2) token: Tokens are not considered models for rules purposes. Teir main purpose is to act as reminders for persisting eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects that apply to speciГЇВ¬ВЃc units. Tey are otherwise ignored and can be freely moved if they get in the way. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 23.0) timing: Te part of an ability that speciГЇВ¬ВЃes when it can be used. Usually appears as a colour- coded bar at the top of the ability. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.2) turns: Each battle round consists of 2 turns (1 for each player), each comprising 7 phases. Te player whose turn is taking place is the active player. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 11.0) Unbind: Reaction to a Spell ability. Make an unbinding roll of 2D6. If the roll beats the casting roll, the Spell ability fails. (Magic, 4.0) underdog: Player with fewest victory points. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 12.0) Unique: A unit with this keyword can only be included in your army once. (Army Composition, 3.4) unique enhancements: Special types of enhancement that some factions have access to. Tey will be accompanied by rules that tell you which units can be given them. units: A group of models that move and ГЇВ¬ВЃght together. An army is made up of a number of units. Each unit has a warscroll that details its characteristics and abilities. Unstable: Models can move across but cannot be set up on or end a move on parts of this terrain feature taller than 1". ## Page 4

Tis download explains how to play games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar with your collection of Citadel Miniatures. It is divided into 4 sections: THE RULES: DIGITAL DOWNLOAD CORE RULES (pg 5-27) Te Core Rules explain the basic concepts of the game, including how your units move, shoot, charge and ГЇВ¬ВЃght. Tese rules are used in all games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar. ADVANCED RULES (pg 28-40) Te Advanced Rules explain how to use special unit types like Wizards and Priests, how to ГЇВ¬ВЃll your army roster, how to score battle tactics, and more. Tis section also covers expanded rules for terrain. GLOSSARY (pg 59-64) Te Glossary is an alphabetised list of important rules terms along with summarised deГЇВ¬ВЃnitions. It is a handy reference tool if you need a quick reminder of what a particular rule does. CITADEL TERRIN LIST (pg 65-66) Te Citadel Terrain List shows you which terrain types apply to which sets of Citadel Terrain as well as clarifying how many scenery pieces each terrain feature comprises. Te rules are numbered to make it easy to look up a rule referenced elsewhere. For example, if a rule told you to see Гўв‚¬ЛњCore Rules, 1.0Гўв‚¬, you would look up the rule numbered 1.0 in the Core Rules, which happens to be Гўв‚¬ЛњCore ConceptsГўв‚¬. 3 e Core Concepts . to b

## Page 5

While some battlepacks focus on balance and competitive play, and others put the emphasis on the story, every Warhammer Age of Sigmar battle is an awe-inspiring spectacle with fully painted armies and terrain. BATTLEPACKS While the rules explain how to play Warhammer Age of Sigmar, battlepacks provide a unique experience for each of your games. Te battlepack you choose will tell you which Advanced Rules to use, and it may include additional special rules. In addition, each battlepack contains a collection of scenarios called battleplans that explain things like where the armies deploy and how to win the battle. WeГўв‚¬d recommend reading through the Core Rules ГЇВ¬ВЃrst to understand the mechanics of the game and then picking a battlepack to play. SPEARHEAD: FIREГ‚ AND JADE (pg 43-58) Spearhead is the simplest way to play Warhammer Age of Sigmar, yet it has amazing tactical depth and re-playability. Te armies used in games of Spearhead are pre-made, so you can walk straight into your nearest Warhammer store and grab a Spearhead army oГЇВ¬в‚¬ the shelf! Te Spearhead battlepack Fire and Jade includes rules for you and your opponent to ГЇВ¬ВЃght battles in the lush, overgrown jungles of Ghyran and on the ГЇВ¬ВЃery lava plains of Aqshy. MATCHED PLAY: BORDER WAR (pg 41-42) Matched Play is a gaming mode designed for balance and equal victory conditions for both players. Te Border War battlepack (a truncated version of the First Blood battlepack in the Core Book) introduces you to Matched Play, providing conditions that give no particular advantage to either side in order to see which player is the canniest commander. 4 CORE RULES

CORE RULES 1.0 CORE CONCEPTS Battles in Warhammer Age of Sigmar are fought on a surface that is referred to as the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld. Each battle uses a scenario called a battleplan, which will explain how to set up the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld and how to determine the winner. Each player is the commander of an army. Each army belongs to a y.y faction and is made up of groups of models called units. Models and units in your army are referred to as friendly models and units, and models and units in y y your opponentГўв‚¬s army are referred to as enemy models and units. y y 2.0 TOOLS OF WAR To ГЇВ¬ВЃ ght a battle, you will need: Гўв‚¬Вў A tape measure  Гўв‚¬Вў  A surface to play on (the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld) Гўв‚¬Вў Some dice Гўв‚¬Вў 2 armies of Citadel Miniatures 2.1 MEASURING DISTANCES Distances in Warhammer Age of Sigmar are measured in inches ("), between the closest points on the bases of the models youГўв‚¬re measuring to and from. You can measure distances whenever you wish. When measuring the distance between units, always measure the distance between the closest points on the bases of the closest models in each unit. If a model does not have a base, measure to and from the closest point on the model instead. 2.1.1 WITHIN AND WHOLLY WITHIN A model is within a certain distance of something (e.g. another unit, a territory or an objective) if any part of its base is within that distance. A model is wholly within a certain distance of something if every part of its base is within that distance. A unit is within a certain distance of something if any part of the base of any model in the unit is within that distance. A unit is wholly within a certain distance of something if every part of the bases of all of the models in the unit is within that distance. 2.2 DICE Warhammer Age of Sigmar uses six-sided dice, of en abbreviated to D6. Гўв‚¬Вў  Some rules refer to Гўв‚¬Лњ2D6Гўв‚¬, Гўв‚¬Лњ3D6Гўв‚¬ and so on Гўв‚¬" in such cases, roll a number of dice equal to the number before Гўв‚¬ЛњD6Гўв‚¬ and add the results together. Гўв‚¬Вў  A roll of Гўв‚¬Лњ2+Гўв‚¬ means a roll of 2 or more, a roll of Гўв‚¬Лњ3+Гўв‚¬ means a roll of 3 or more, and so on. Гўв‚¬Вў  If a rule requires you to roll a D3, roll a dice and halve the total, rounding up. For D3 rolls, a roll of Гўв‚¬Лњ2+Гўв‚¬ means a result of 2 or more af er halving the total and rounding up. Гўв‚¬Вў  EГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects that allow you to modify a dice roll are called modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers. Гўв‚¬Вў  Some rules allow you to re-roll a dice roll, which means you get to roll some or all of the dice again. You cannot re-roll a dice more than once, and re-rolls happen before modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers to the roll (if any) are applied. Гўв‚¬Вў  If you are instructed to roll oГЇВ¬в‚¬ , each player should roll a dice. ГЇВ¬в‚¬, ГЇВ¬в‚¬ Whichever player has a higher roll wins the roll-oГЇВ¬в‚¬ . If the result is a tie, each player should roll oГЇВ¬в‚¬  again until there is a clear winner, unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed. THE MOST T IMPORTANTГ‚ RULE I While youГўв‚¬re playing W Warhammer Age of Sigmar, W you might encounter a rules y situation that you arenГўв‚¬t sure s how to resolve. If youГўв‚¬re new h o the game, we recommend t discussing with your d opponent and agreeing on a o fair and reasonable solution f so you can get on with the s game as quickly as possible. g Otherwise, the O Special Rules section includes detailed s nstructions on how to resolve in hese situations. t RULES UPDATES R We are committed to W supporting Warhammer s Age of Sigmar rules via A regular free updates based r on community feedback. o Please check P warhammer- community.com c to download he latest updates and FAQs. t T is book also includes the T corrections from the oГЇВ¬Ж’  cial c errata for the rules at the time e of going to print. o RULES SUMMARIES Г‚ВЌ Many pages in the rules have red boxes in the sidebar, such as this one. Г‚ВЌ T ese give you short summaries of important rules. Г‚ВЌ If you need to read a rule in full, refer to the main text. It takes both cunning and viciousness to hold their lofy position, and successful Clawlords must be willing to stoop to any foul end and ГЇВ¬ВЃght like cornered animals in order to keep their star in the ascendant. Accumulating favours from members of the other Great Clans is a ГЇВ¬ВЃne way to do this, and so the most powerful Clawlords will ride upon a Gnaw-beast bred by the Clans Moulder Гўв‚¬" a gangly, shrieking abomination whose hunger is even more voracious than that of its Skaven master. KEYWORDS CORNERED RAT: A Clawlord in fear for their life ГЇВ¬ВЃghts with rabid ferocity. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: While this unit is damaged, add 3 to the Attacks characteristic of its Warpforged Halberd. Each faction has a set of faction rules, which include battle traits, battleГ‚ formations, enhancements and lores. T ese can be found in various publications but most commonly in the factionГўв‚¬s battletome. 4.0 WARSCROLLS T e rules for each unit are contained on a warscroll: 6 7 8 5 4 1 3 T e MOVE characteristic determines how quickly the unit can move across the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld (see 15.0). T e HEALTH characteristic determines how many damage points can be allocated to a unit before a model in the unit is slain (see 18.2). T e CONTROL characteristic determines how well each model in the unit can contest objectives (see 32.2). T e SAVE characteristic determines how well armoured the unit is. T is characteristic is the roll you need to equal or exceed to save a model from harm, so a lower value is better (see 17.0). T e UNIT TYPE. T e KEYWORDS that the unit has (see 5.1) T e WEAPONS the unit is armed with. Weapons are either melee weapons, used to make combat attacks, or ranged weapons, used to make shooting attacks. 5.0 ABILITIES Te vast majority of things that units can do in Warhammer Age of Sigmar are called abilities. Tis is an example of an ability: Timing Name and description Declare instructions EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect Keywords 5.1 KEYWORDS Some abilities have one or more keywords listed at the bottom of the ability on their keywords bar, e.g. the Гўв‚¬ЛњShootГўв‚¬ ability shown above has the Core, Attack and Shoot keywords. Units also have a keywords bar on their warscroll. Keywords let you know which abilities can be used or which units can be picked as targets for an ability. For example, the Гўв‚¬ЛњChargeГўв‚¬ ability can only be used by a unit if it did not use an ability with the Run or Retreat keyword earlier in the turn. Te singular and plural forms of a keyword are synonymous for rules purposes. 4 1 2 3 Ability icons give you an idea what kind of eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect the ability has at a glance. Movement Ability OГЇВ¬в‚¬ensive Ability Defensive Ability Shooting Ability Rallying Ability Special Ability Control Ability SHOOT: Te warriors take aim and unleash a deadly volley. Declare: Pick a friendly unit that has not used a Run or Retreat ability this turn to use this ability. Ten, pick one or more enemy units as the target(s) of that unitГўв‚¬s attacks (see 16.0). EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Resolve shooting attacks against the target unit(s). Keywords Core, Attack, Shoot Your Shooting Phase 5.2 USING ABILITIES Te timing of an ability tells you when it can be used. When using an ability, follow these steps: 1. Declare the Ability: Tell your opponent which ability is being used. If the ability has Declare instructions, resolve them at this step. 2. Use Reactions: Starting with the player using the ability, the players alternate using any abilities with an appropriate Reaction timing. Players can choose to pass instead of using a reaction, but once both players consecutively pass, no further reactions to that ability can be used. 3. Resolve the EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Follow the instructions in the EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect part of the ability. 5.3 THE RULES OF ONE Гўв‚¬Вў A unit cannot use more than 1 Core ability per phase (see 14.0). Гўв‚¬Вў A unit cannot use the same ability more than once per phase, unless speciГЇВ¬ВЃed otherwise. Гўв‚¬Вў A unit cannot be aГЇВ¬в‚¬ected by the same passive ability more than once at the same time. For example, if a unit is within range of two diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent terrain features that have the Гўв‚¬ЛњCoverГўв‚¬ passive ability, the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect only applies to it once. 5.4 PASSIVE ABILITIES Abilities that have the Passive timing are called passive abilities. Passive abilities are not declared. Te eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects of passive abilities always apply if the conditions of the ability are met, and they must be applied if it is possible to do so. 5 2 3 4 1 5 CORE RULES

6.0 VISIBILITY A target model is visible to another model (which weГўв‚¬ll call the Гўв‚¬Лњobserving modelГўв‚¬) if you can draw a straight line through the air (whether horizontal, diagonal or vertical) from any point on the observing model to any point on the target model that does not intersect any objects except for other models in the observing modelГўв‚¬s unit. A target unit is visible to an observing unit if at least 1 model in the target unit is visible to at least 1 model in the observing unit. A model is always considered to be visible to itself. Г‚ВЌ If any part of another model can be seen by an observing model, both the target model and its unit are visible to that observing model. T e Liberator at the back of this unit can see through their fellows. Models donГўв‚¬t block the visibility of other models in their unit Гўв‚¬" it is assumed that they ГЇВ¬ВЃ ght in such a way as to not obstruct one anotherГўв‚¬s attacks. ГЇВ¬ВЃ An observing modelГўв‚¬s visibility can be blocked by enemy models. T e A Plaguebearer in this shot is not visible to the Liberator because it is hidden by the Great Unclean One. Even though only half of this Liberator can be seen, he counts as being visible. A model is visible no matter how much of the model is blocked from sight or A how little of it is visible; if any part of the model can be seen, it is visible. n some cases, it might not be I mmediately clear whether a im model is visible. If so, stoop m down to get a look from d behind the observing model. b f any part of the other model I s visible, even if it is just the is ip of a spear, then that model t s visible for rules purposes. is CORE RULES 9

7.0 COMBAT R NGE Each model has a combat range that extends 3" horizontally from its base and any distance vertically from that circle to form a cylinder. T e combat range of a unit extends 3" horizontally and any distance vertically from every model in that unit. Units from opposing armies that are within each otherГўв‚¬s combat range and that are visible to each other are inГ‚ combat with each other. When a unit that is not in combat enters the combat range of a visible enemy unit, it movesГ‚ into combat. A model is considered to be in combat with an enemy unit if that unit is within the modelГўв‚¬s combat range and visible to it. A modelГўв‚¬s combat range extends 3" horizontally outwards from it in all directions and an inГЇВ¬ВЃ nite distance vertically. Note that this is measured from the edge of its base, not the centre. Г‚ВЌ A unitГўв‚¬s combat range extends 3" out from every model in that unit. Г‚ВЌ If any enemy models are within a unitГўв‚¬s combat range and visible to it, that unit is in combat. A unitГўв‚¬s combat range extends 3" horizontally and any distance vertically from all models in the unit. As you can see, this forms a sort of Гўв‚¬ЛњcloudГўв‚¬. Even though both Rat Ogors are within the combat range of the Liberator, the Rat Ogor behind the wall is not visible to the Liberator and is therefore not in combat. A unit is in combat with a visible enemy unit if any of its nit if models are within the combat range of that enemy unit. Even though 1 Rat Ogor is not within the LiberatorsГўв‚¬ combat range, its unit is still in combat. ГЇЖ’Вј ГЇЖ’В» 3" 3" 3" 3" 3" CORE RULES 10

10.0 THE DEPLOYMENT PHASE Once the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld is ready, the players must deploy their armies. T is is y y done in a special phase called the deployment phase. T e deployment phase happens once per battle, before the start of the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst battle round (see 12.0). STEP 1 Гўв‚¬" DEPLOY FACTION TERR IN FEATURES Some factions have faction terrain features, which have their own warscrolls and are set up using Deploy Terrain abilities. T e player who begins deployment must use Deploy Terrain abilities ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst, followed by their opponent. STEP 2 Гўв‚¬" DEPLOY ARMIES Unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed in the battleplan, the players alternate using Deploy abilities to deploy their units, starting with the player who begins deployment. Once one player has no more Deploy abilities to use, their opponent must continue to use Deploy abilities until they also have no more to use. STEP 3 Гўв‚¬" USE DEPLOYMENT PHASE ABILITIES Af er both players have ГЇВ¬ВЃ nished deploying their units, the player who begins deployment can use any Г‚ DeploymentГ‚ PhaseГ‚  abilities that are not Deploy or Deploy Terrain abilities, in the order of their choosing, then their opponent can do the same. 10.1 UNIVERSAL DEPLOYMENT PHASE ABILITIES DEPLOY FACTION TERR IN: T e army has taken to the ГЇВ¬ВЃ eld surrounding an important and powerful landmark. Declare: Pick a friendly faction terrain feature that has not been deployed to be the target. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect: Set up the target faction terrain feature wholly within friendly territory, more than 3" from all objectives and other terrain features. Af er you have done so, it has been deployed. Keywords Deploy Terrain Deployment Phase DEPLOY REGIMENT: A mighty hero leads their retinue into battle. Declare: Pick a regiment from your army roster to be the target. No units in that regiment can have already been deployed. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect: Keep using Deploy abilities without alternating until all units in that regiment have been deployed. You cannot pick units that are not in that regiment as the target of any of those Deploy abilities. Keywords Deploy Deployment Phase DEPLOY UNIT: Additional forces take to the ГЇВ¬ВЃ eld. Declare: Pick a unit from your army roster that has not been deployed to be the target. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect: Set up the target unit wholly within friendly territory and more than 9" from enemy territory. Af er you have done so, it has been deployed. Keywords Deploy Deployment Phase A regiment is a collection of units. It is one of the main building blocks of an army (seeГ‚ Гўв‚¬ЛњArmy CompositionГўв‚¬ in the Advanced Rules). Some factions have special Deploy abilities, such as the Гўв‚¬ЛњT e Celestial RealmГўв‚¬ ability of the Stormcast Eternals. Г‚ВЌ Player who begins deployment can use any Deploy Terrain abilities, followed by their opponent. Г‚ВЌ Players alternate using Deploy abilities. Г‚ВЌ Player who begins deployment must use any other Deployment Phase abilities, then their opponent can do so. CORE RULES 12

11.0 BATTLE SEQUENCE Each battle lasts a number of battle rounds, speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed in the battleplan. During each battle round, each player will take a turn. Each turn is broken down into multiple phases (see 13.0). 12.0 START OF BATTLE ROUND At the start of each battle round, follow these steps: 1.  Determine the Active Player: y If it is the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst battle round, the player who ГЇВ¬ВЃ nished setting up their army ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst decides who will take the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst turn (unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed in the battlepack or battleplan). If it is not the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst battle round, the players make a roll-oГЇВ¬в‚¬  called the priorityГ‚ roll and the winner decides who will take the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst turn. If the roll-oГЇВ¬в‚¬  is a tie, the player who took the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst turn in the previous battle round decides who will take the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst turn in the current battle round. When it is a playerГўв‚¬s turn, regardless of whether they take the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst or second turn, they are referred to as the active player. 2. Determine the Underdog:g Whichever player has the fewest victory points is the underdog for the battle round. If the players are tied (e.g. in the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst g battle round), then there is no underdog, unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed. 3. Start of Battle Round Abilities: T e active player can use any Г‚ StartГ‚ ofГ‚ BattleГ‚ RoundГ‚  abilities ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst, then their opponent can do the same. 13.0 TURN PHASES Each playerГўв‚¬s turn is broken down into 7 phases, as shown on the lef . In each phase, the active player can use any abilities with the Гўв‚¬ЛњYourГ‚ (Гўв‚¬В¦)Г‚ PhaseГўв‚¬ or Гўв‚¬ЛњAny (Гўв‚¬В¦) Phase Гўв‚¬ЛњA Гўв‚¬Лњ Гўв‚¬ timing in the order of their choosing. For example, in the Movement Phase, the active player could use abilities with the Г‚ YourГ‚ MovementГ‚ PhaseГ‚  or Г‚ AnyГ‚ MovementГ‚ PhaseГ‚  timing. Once the active player has ГЇВ¬ВЃ nished using abilities, their opponent can then use any abilities with an Гўв‚¬ЛњEnemy (Гўв‚¬В¦) PhaseГўв‚¬ or Гўв‚¬ЛњAny (Гўв‚¬В¦) Phase Гўв‚¬ЛњA Гўв‚¬Лњ Гўв‚¬ timing, in the order of their choosing. 13.1 FIGHT ABILITIES Abilities with the Fight keyword follow a diГЇВ¬в‚¬ erent sequence to other abilities. When the players are using  CombatГ‚ Phase  abilities as described in 13.0, they cannot use Fight abilities. Af er the players have ГЇВ¬ВЃ nished using  CombatГ‚ Phase abilities that are not Fight abilities, they must alternate picking 1 eligible unit to use a Fight ability, starting with the active player. Each unit in combat must use a Fight ability if it is able to. Once a player has no more units that are eligible to use a Fight ability, the other player continues to pick units that are eligible to use a Fight ability, one af er another, until there are no more units that are eligible to use a Fight ability. THE BATTLE Г‚ВЌ T e battle lasts a ГЇВ¬ВЃ xed number of battle rounds. Г‚ВЌ Each battle round contains 2 turns. Г‚ВЌ T e player whose turn is taking place is the active player. Г‚ВЌ Each turn contains 7 phases. Г‚ВЌ In each phase, the active player uses abilities ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst, then their opponent does the same. Г‚ВЌ T e active player uses Combat Phase abilities that are not Fight abilities, then the opponent does the same. Г‚ВЌ Players alternate picking a unit to use a Fight ability, starting with the active player. Г‚ВЌ Each unit must use a Fight ability if it is able to. Active playerГўв‚¬s CombatГ‚ Phase abilities (not Fight) OpponentГўв‚¬s CombatГ‚ Phase abilities (not Fight) Players alternate using Fight abilities, starting with the active player Start of Turn Hero Phase Movement Phase Shooting Phase Charge Phase Combat Phase End of Turn CORE RULES 13

## Page 16

MOVEMENT 15.0 MOVEMENT Some abilities allow a unit to move. When a unit moves, each model in that unit can move, one at a time, in the order chosen by that unitГўв‚¬s commander. When a model moves, it can pivot and change direction as of en as you like, but no part of that modelГўв‚¬s base can move a greater total distance than the maximum allowed by the ability used. Models cannot move beyond the edge of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld or through other models, and they can never end a move standing on top of another model (the base counts as part of the model). A modelГўв‚¬s base is assumed to remain parallel with the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld as it moves. If a model ends its move on an uneven surface, it must be able to stand up by itself with its base as close to parallel with the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld as possible (in other words, models cannot lie ГЇВ¬вЂљ at on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld or lean against terrain to stop them from falling over). Г‚ВЌ When moving a unit, move any number of models in that unit. Г‚ВЌ Each model can turn and pivot, but no part of its base can move farther than the maximum distance. Г‚ВЌ Units can move any distance up to the maximum speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed in the ability. Г‚ВЌ Units must end their move in coherency. Г‚ВЌ Af er ГЇВ¬ВЃ nishing a move, a unit must be in a single group. Г‚ВЌ Coherency range is Г‚ВЅ" horizontally. Г‚ВЌ Each model must be within coherency range of a diГЇВ¬в‚¬ erent model from the same unit. Г‚ВЌ While a unit has 7+ models, each model must be in coherency with 2 other models in the unit. 15.1 COHERENCY Any time a unit is set up or ends a move, it must be in a single group. A unit is considered to be in a coherent group if each model in that unit is within coherency range, measured horizontally, of at least 1 other model in that unit (ignore diГЇВ¬в‚¬ erences in height between the two models). For the majority of units, coherency range is Г‚ВЅ", though some units (particularly those with large models with overhanging parts) have a longer coherency range noted on their warscroll for ease of play. While there are 7 or more models in a unit, that unit is considered to be in a coherent group if each model in that unit is within coherency range of atГ‚ least 2 other models in that unit. If it is not possible for a unit to end a move in a single coherent group, that move cannot be made. T is Grey Seer is using the Гўв‚¬ЛњNormal MoveГўв‚¬ ability to change its position on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld. No part of that move can be within the combat range of the Liberators, so the Grey Seer has to stay outside the area marked in blue. 6" CORE RULES 15

## Page 17

As a model moves across terrain, its base is assumed to stay in contact with the terrain feature and parallel to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld. Models can climb up or down terrain. T ey can jump down too, but this ends their move. 15.2 MOVING ACROSS TERR IN When a model moves, it can move over terrain features but not through them. A model can be moved over terrain features that are 1" or less in height as if they were not there. A model can be moved vertically in order to climb up or down any terrain features that are taller than 1", counting the vertical distance as part of its move. Models cannot end a move mid-climb. 15.2.1 JUMPING DOWN When a model that is on a terrain feature moves, it can jump down from the edge of the terrain feature to land on a lower part of the same or a diГЇВ¬в‚¬ erent terrain feature or to land on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld. To do so, you must trace the path of the modelГўв‚¬s move to the edge of the terrain feature. T en, that model can jump down any distance, but doing so immediately ends their move. Models cannot end any kind of move mid-jump Гўв‚¬" if it is not possible to end the move as a result, that move cannot be made. FLY: Soaring above the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld, this unit can reach destinations that are inaccessible to other troops. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect: As this unit moves, it ignores other models, terrain features and the combat ranges of enemy units. It cannot end its move in combat unless speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed in the ability that allowed it to move. Ignore any vertical distance moved for this unit. Passive Г‚ВЌ Models can move freely over terrain features 1" or less in height. Г‚ВЌ Models can climb up and down terrain features, and they can jump down from a higher ledge. Г‚ВЌ Models cannot end a move mid-climb or mid-jump. Г‚ВЌ Pile in: move up to 3". Г‚ВЌ If in combat, the unit must end the move closer, or at least as close, to the target enemy unit. 15.3 PILE-IN MOVES Some abilities, such as Fight abilities, allow a unit to make a short move called a pile-in move to get into a better position for combat. To do so: If your unit is in combat: y Pick an enemy unit your unit is in combat with to be the target of the pile-in move. Each model in your unit can move up to 3". T at move can pass through the combat ranges of any enemy units, but each model must end that move no further from the target unit. At the end of the move, your unit must still be in combat with all units that it was in combat with at the start of the move. If your unit is not in combat: y Each model in your unit can move 3" in any direction. T at move can pass through and end within the combat ranges of any enemy units. 15.4 FLYING Units with the Fly keyword have the following passive ability: When moving ГЇВ¬вЂљ ying units, move them horizontally in any direction, ignoring intervening models and terrain, and place them where you wish, so long as they are allowed to end their move on that spot. Note that some units have the Fly keyword even if that unit canГўв‚¬t really ГЇВ¬вЂљ y. T is of en represents units that bounce, bound or skitter across the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld so adeptly that they might as well be ГЇВ¬вЂљ ying! CORE RULES 16

## Page 18

16.0 PICKING TARGETS When declaring an Attack ability for a unit, you must pick the target unit(s) for its attacks. T e number of attacks each model can make is equal to the Attacks characteristic of the weapons it is using. In most cases, models attack with every weapon on their warscroll (melee weapons for combat attacks, ranged weapons for shooting attacks), though some warscrolls specify that certain models in the unit are armed with special weapons or that the unit must pick between multiple weapons when it attacks. If the unit is in combat, it can only target units that are in combat with it. If a model has more than one attack, you can split the attacks between eligible targets as you wish. Гўв‚¬Вў Combat attacks are made with melee weapons. T e target unit(s) must be within the combat range of the attacking model and visible to it. T e model must attack with all of the melee weapons it is armed with. Гўв‚¬Вў Shooting attacks g are made with ranged weapons. T e target unit(s) must be within a distance equal to the Range characteristic of the weapon being used and visible to the attacking model. Models cannot make shooting attacks if their unit is in combat, unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed (see 20.0 Weapon Abilities). 17.0 THE ATTACK SEQUENCE Resolve steps 1-4 below for each attack made against a target unit, one attack at a time. If you picked more than one target unit for the Attack ability, resolve all the attacks made against one unit before moving on to the next, in an order of your choosing. Once you are familiar with the attack sequence, see Гўв‚¬ЛњFast Dice RollingГўв‚¬ (see 17.3) if you want to speed up play. 1.  Hit Roll: Roll a dice. If the roll equals or exceeds the attacking weaponГўв‚¬s Hit characteristic, the attack scores a successful hit: move on to the next step. If not, the attack fails and the attack sequence ends. UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed hit rolls of 1 always fail. If an unmodiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed hit roll for an attack made with a weapon is a 6, that attack is a critical hit. 2.  Wound Roll: Roll a dice. If the roll equals or exceeds the attacking weaponГўв‚¬s Wound characteristic, the attack successfully wounds: move on to the next step. If not, the attack fails and the attack sequence ends. UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed wound rolls of 1 always fail. 3.  Save Roll: T e commander of the target unit rolls a dice, subtracting the attacking weaponГўв‚¬s Rend characteristic from the roll. UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed save rolls of 1 always fail. If the roll equals or exceeds the Save characteristic of the target unit, the attack fails and the attack sequence ends. If not, it is a successful attack: move on to the next step. 4. Determine Damage: g  T e attack inГЇВ¬вЂљ icts a number of damageГ‚ points on the unit equal to the Damage characteristic of the weapon. InГЇВ¬вЂљ icted damage points are added to a temporary damage pool for the target unit. Af er completing these steps for all of the attacks made as part of that Attack ability, the attacks for that ability are resolved and you can move on to the damage sequence (see 18.0). ATTACKING A Critical hits have no eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect on C heir own but of en trigger t additional eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects (see 20.0 a Weapon Abilities). W Keep track of the number of K damage points in the damage d pools of target units. Placing p dice next to those units is a d handy way of doing this. h Г‚ВЌ Pick targets for all attacks when declaring an Attack ability. Г‚ВЌ If a unit is in combat, it can only attack units it is inГ‚ combat with and that are visible to it. Г‚ВЌ If making a combat attack, the target must be within the attacking modelГўв‚¬s combat range. Г‚ВЌ If making a shooting attack, the target must be visible and within the weaponГўв‚¬s Range. Г‚ВЌ A unit cannot make shooting attacks if it is in combat, unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed. CORE RULES 17

## Page 19

17.1 ATTACK MODIFIERS AND CAPS ModiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers to hit rolls, wound rolls and save rolls are capped to prevent overwhelming combinations of abilities. When making a hit roll or a wound roll, add up all positive and negative modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers that apply to the roll, then cap the result at a maximum of +1 (if positive) or a minimum of -1 (if negative). When making a save roll, add up all the positive and negative modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers that apply to the roll, then cap the result at a maximum of +1. Note that, unlike hit rolls and wound rolls, there is no cap on the amount that can be subtracted from save rolls. 17.2 MORTAL DAMAGE Some abilities inГЇВ¬вЂљ ict mortal damage. If an ability inГЇВ¬вЂљ icts mortal damage on a unit, add that number of damage points to the unitГўв‚¬s damage pool for that ability (see 18.2 Allocating Damage). 17.3 FAST DICE ROLLING In order to speed up play, it is of en possible to make several attacks at once. If you choose to do so, all of the attacks must have the same Hit, Wound, Rend and Damage characteristics, the same weapon abilities (if any Гўв‚¬" see 20.0), and they must target the same enemy unit. If this is the case, make all of the hit rolls at the same time, then all of the wound rolls and ГЇВ¬ВЃ nally all of the save rolls. T en, add up all the damage points inГЇВ¬вЂљ icted on the target unit and move on to the damage sequence (see 18.0). If the attack sequence ends for an attack made while fast dice rolling, it doesnГўв‚¬t stop the attack sequence for all the attacks, just that speciГЇВ¬ВЃ c attack.k. T e bold advance of the Stormcast Eternals enrages a pack of Rat Ogors, triggering a mad charge. T ese hulking Skaven aim to tear apart their foes with brute strength and ferocity before a counter-attack can be launched. CORE RULES 18

19.0 STRIKE-FIRST AND STRIKE-LAST If there are any Strike-first units in combat after any non-Fight combat phase abilities have been used, other units cannot be picked to use a Fight ability until those units have been picked to use a Fight ability. Af er all those Strike-first units have fought, the active player picks the next unit to ГЇВ¬ВЃ ght. If there are any Strike-last units in combat, they cannot be picked to use a Fight ability if there are any units in combat that do not have Strike-last and have not yet used a Fight ability. If a unit has Strike-first and Strike-last, treat it as if it had neither. 20.0 WEAPON ABILITIES Some weapons have one or more of the passive weapon abilities below. If a weapon has multiple weapon abilities that apply on a criticalГ‚ hit, before the attack sequence starts, the commander of the attacking model must pick 1 of those abilities to use. 21.0 HEALING Some abilities allow you to heal a unit. To heal a unit, remove a number of damage points from the unit equal to the number in brackets af er Гўв‚¬ЛњHealГўв‚¬, e.g. Heal (2). SPECIAL RULES UNIVERSAL WEAPON ABILITIES Anti-X (+1 Rend): Add 1 to this weaponГўв‚¬s Rend characteristic if the target has the keyword af er Гўв‚¬ЛњAnti- Гўв‚¬ЛњA Гўв‚¬Лњ Гўв‚¬ or fulГЇВ¬ВЃ ls the condition af er Гўв‚¬ЛњAnti- Гўв‚¬ЛњA Гўв‚¬Лњ Гўв‚¬. Multiples of this ability are cumulative. For example, if a weapon has both Anti-charge (+1 Rend) and Anti-Hero (+1 Rend), then add 2 to the Rend characteristic of the weapon for attacks that target a Hero that charged in the same turn. Charge (+1 Damage): Add 1 to this weaponГўв‚¬s Damage characteristic if the attacking unit charged this turn. Companion: Unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed, this weapon is not aГЇВ¬в‚¬ ected by friendly abilities that aГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect weapon characteristics or the attack sequence, except for those that apply negative modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers to it (e.g. Гўв‚¬ЛњCovering FireГўв‚¬). Crit (2 Hits): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack scores 2 hits on the target unit instead of 1. Make a wound roll for each hit. Crit (Auto-wound): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack automatically wounds the target. Make a save roll as normal. Crit (Mortal): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack inГЇВ¬вЂљ icts mortal damage on the target unit equal to the Damage characteristic of that weapon and the attack sequence ends. Shoot in Combat: T is weapon can be used to make shooting attacks even if the attacking unit is in combat. T ere may be situations when T a unit that has a Strike-first s not in combat at the start is of the phase, but because of o moves such as pile-in moves, m t is Гўв‚¬Лњpulled into combatГўв‚¬ later it n the phase. In such cases, in Strike-first S has no eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect on that unit because it was o not in combat at the start of n he phase. t Abilities that allow a unit A o use a t Fight ability mmediately af er another im unit do not override u he t Strike-first or Strike-last S constraints, so you could not pick a unit s with w Strike-last to ГЇВ¬ВЃ ght mmediately af er a unit im with w Strike-first. T e T Companion weapon ability restricts things like a mounts from beneГЇВ¬ВЃ ting from m most eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects that augment a m unitГўв‚¬s capabilities. CORE RULES
