Г‚ВЌ Returned models must be set up in coherency with the models in their unit that were not returned as part of that ability. Г‚ВЌ Returned models can only be set up in combat with an enemy unit if their unit is already in combat with it. Tis Gryph-crow is a token. 22.0 RETURNING AND ADDINGГ‚ MODELS Some abilities allow you to return slain models to a unit or add new models to a unit. In either case, set up those models, one at a time (see 15.1) within coherency range of the model(s) in that unit that were not returned or added this turn. If that unit has 7 or more models (including the model being set up), it must be set up within coherency range of at least 2 other models in that unit that were not returned or added this turn. Te new models can only be set up in combat with an enemy unit if their unit is already in combat with that enemy unit. 23.0 TOKENS Tokens are not considered to be models for rules purposes and can be ignored or moved out of the way for purposes of visibility, coherency or abilities. Tey cannot be picked as the target of abilities. Teir purpose is to indicate persisting eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects on units they are placed next to. Each time a unit with a token ГЇВ¬ВЃnishes a move or is set up, place the token next to the unit. 24.0 SETTING UP UNITS Some abilities allow you to set up a unit on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. When doing so, you must set up all models in that unit. If this is impossible, you cannot use that ability. A unit set up on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld in a phase other than the deployment phase cannot use Move abilities in the movement phase of the same turn. 24.1 RESERVE UNITS Some abilities allow you to set up units in reserve. Tese units are placed to one side instead of being set up on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. At the start of the fourth battle round, units that were set up in reserve using a Deploy ability and that are still in reserve are destroyed. 24.2 REPLACEMENT UNITS Some abilities allow you to set up a replacement unit. When setting up that unit, it should have the same warscroll type, weapon options and number of models as the original unit, unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃed in the ability. Many of these abilities specify the proportion of models in the replacement unit (e.g. half the number of models from the original unit). In these cases, you can pick which models from the original unit are set up. Te replacement unit is otherwise treated as a new unit; any keywords or abilities the original unit gained during the battle, and any persisting eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects that applied to it, do not apply to the replacement unit. Each unit can only be replaced once, and you cannot replace replacement units. 25.0 GUARDED HEROES All Heroes that are not Monsters or War Machines have the Гўв‚¬ЛњGuardedГ‚ HeroГўв‚¬ passive ability: GUARDED HERO: Te leader is shielded by their warriors, who close ranks around them to protect them from harm. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: If this Hero is within the combat range of a friendly unit that is not a Hero: Гўв‚¬Вў Subtract 1 from hit rolls for shooting attacks that target this Hero. Гўв‚¬Вў If this Hero is Infantry, they cannot be picked as the target of shooting attacks made by models more than 12" from them. Passive Understandably, Monsters and War Machines donГўв‚¬t beneГЇВ¬ВЃt from the Гўв‚¬ЛњGuarded HeroГўв‚¬ ability. Tese units are very conspicuous targets even if they are surrounded by their fellows! Because a replacement unit is treated as a completely new unit, it could, for instance, use a Once Per Battle ability on its warscroll even if the unit it replaced used that ability earlier in the battle. CORE RULES

26.0 R NDOM CHAR CTERISTICS Some warscrolls show a random characteristic roll instead of a ГЇВ¬ВЃ xed value. When this is the case, the value of the characteristic is generated v by the commander of the unit in question each time an ability requires that characteristic. In the case of random weapon characteristics, generate a random Attacks characteristic for each model in the attacking unit each time you declare an Attack ability, and generate a random Damage characteristic each time you inГЇВ¬вЂљ ict damage with that weapon (roll once for each attack). 27.0 MODIFIER ORDER T e eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of some abilities modify a characteristic or roll. Unless stated otherwise, a characteristic or roll cannot be modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed to less than 1. T e exception to this is Rend, which can be modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed to a minimum of 0 (Гўв‚¬Лњ-Гўв‚¬). If a characteristic uses a random characteristic roll (see above), apply characteristic modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers af er that characteristic has been generated. If a characteristic or roll is halved or would be modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ed to a value that is not a whole number, round it down to the nearest whole number. Apply characteristic modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers in the following order: 1.  ModiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers that set a characteristic to a ГЇВ¬ВЃ xed value. 2.  ModiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers that multiply or y y divide a characteristic. 3.  ModiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers that add to or subtract from a characteristic. If there are multiple modiГЇВ¬ВЃ ers of the same type (e.g. that set a characteristic), apply them in the Order of EГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects (see 30.0). The effects of some abilities (e.g. Гўв‚¬ЛњAn Excess of DepravityГўв‚¬) allow you to replace a roll with a fixed value. When doing so, you must replace the roll before rolling the dice for it. Г‚ВЌ When using a random characteristic, generate it each time it is needed for an ability. Г‚ВЌ When using random Damage, generate it for each attack made. Skaven technology is infamously unpredictable. T eir warpstone-powered weapons are just as likely to belch out a S thunderous volley as they are a single forlorn shot Гўв‚¬" or they might just explode without warning! CORE RULES 25

28.0 ADVANCED ABILITY RULES Гўв‚¬Вў  Unless stated otherwise, units using or picked as part of an ability must be onГ‚ the battleГЇВ¬ВЃ eld. Гўв‚¬Вў  If an eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect states that a unit can do something, its commander can choose whether to resolve that part of the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect or not. Гўв‚¬Вў  If an eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect states that a unit must do something, its commander has no choice and must resolve that part of the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect. If this is impossible, no part of the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect is applied but the ability is still considered to have been used. Гўв‚¬Вў  When Гўв‚¬Лњthis unitГўв‚¬ appears in ability text (most of en on warscrolls), it means the unit that is using the ability. Гўв‚¬Вў  If an ability aГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects more than one unit, the player who used the ability can choose the order in which units are aГЇВ¬в‚¬ ected by it. Гўв‚¬Вў  When resolving an eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect, if you need to roll a dice for multiple aГЇВ¬в‚¬ ected units, roll and resolve the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect for one unit before moving on to the next. Гўв‚¬Вў  If an ability instructs you to pick more than 1 unit, each unit you pick must be a diГЇВ¬в‚¬ erent unit unless stated otherwise. 28.1 PERSISTING EFFECTS Some abilities have eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects that arenГўв‚¬t immediately resolved (e.g. Гўв‚¬Лњadd 1 to save rolls for this unit for the rest of the turnГўв‚¬ or Гўв‚¬Лњthis unit has WardГ‚ (5+) for the rest of the turnГўв‚¬). T ese eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects count as the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of passive abilities (see 5.4) for their duration. 28.2 Гўв‚¬ЛњONCE PERГўв‚¬ TIMINGS T e timing part of some abilities says Once Per Phase, Once Per Turn or Once Per Battle. If the ability is used by a unit, it can be used a maximum of one time in that phase, turn or battle by each unit that can use the ability. If the ability is used by a player, it can be used a maximum of one time in that timing window by that player. Some abilities that are used by units say Once Per Phase (Army), Once Per Turn (Army) or Once Per Battle (Army). T ese abilities can be used a maximum of one time in that phase, turn or battle regardless of the number of units in the army that can use the ability. 29.0 CONTR DICTORY RULES If two or more rules contradict, if one of those rules states that something cannot do something, this takes precedence over rules that state it can or must do that thing, unless the second rule speciГЇВ¬ВЃ cally overrides the restriction of the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst. For example, while the Гўв‚¬ЛњNormal MoveГўв‚¬ ability states you cannot move into combat during that move, the Гўв‚¬ЛњFlyГўв‚¬ ability speciГЇВ¬ВЃ es to ignore the combat ranges of enemy models during a move. Excepting the above, the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect of the most recently used ability takes precedence. 30.0 ORDER OF EFFECTS T e eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of passive abilities are considered to be applied more recently than the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of other abilities and rules. T e eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of the active playerГўв‚¬s passive abilities are considered to be applied more recently than the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of their opponentГўв‚¬s passive abilities, whose passiveГ‚ abilities are considered to be applied more recently than the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of neutral passive abilities (e.g. passive abilities on a terrain feature that is in neither playerГўв‚¬s army). T e active player chooses the order in which neutral passive eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects are applied. Some abilities have a delayed eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ect (e.g.Г‚ Гўв‚¬Лњeach time a friendly unit uses a Fight ability, af er that ability has been resolved, Heal (D3) that unitГўв‚¬). If more than one of these eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects would be resolved at the same time, the active player resolves the delayed eГЇВ¬в‚¬ ects of their abilities ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst, in an order of their choosing, then their opponent does the same. WHO IS USING THEГ‚ ABILITY? Most abilities are found on warscrolls, but many appear elsewhere Гўв‚¬" the best examples being the Universal Core Abilities (see 14.0). While abilities on warscrolls are always used by the unit whose warscroll it is, some abilities that do not appear on warscrolls will tell you explicitly to pick a unit to use the ability. In both cases, it should be clear which unit is using the ability. Some abilities that do not appear on warscrolls, most commonly enhancements (see Army Composition, 4.1), are given to certain units in your army. In such cases, the unit to which the enhancement was given is the one using the ability. Abilities that neither appear on a warscroll nor tell you to pick a unit to use the ability are used by you, the player. Finally, for the purposes of the rules in this section, when a rule refers to a player using an ability, this includes abilities used by units in that playerГўв‚¬s army. CORE RULES 26

## Page 28

31.0 END OF TURN OVERVIEW At the end of each turn, follow these steps: 1.  T e active player can use any abilities with the Г‚ EndГ‚ ofГ‚ YourГ‚ TurnГ‚ or Г‚ EndГ‚ ofГ‚ AnyГ‚ TurnГ‚  timing, in the order of their choosing, then their opponent can use any abilities with the Г‚ EndГ‚ ofГ‚ EnemyГ‚ TurnГ‚ or Г‚ EndГ‚ ofГ‚ AnyГ‚ TurnГ‚  timing, in the order of their choosing. 2.  Determine which player controls each objective (if any). 3.  T e active player scores victory points as described in the battleplan. 32.0 OBJECTIVES Many battleplans award victory points for controlling objectives, which are represented by objective markers. Unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed, objective markers are round and 40mm wide. Models can move over and end their moves on objective markers. If an objective marker is on the border between territories, it is within all those territories but wholly within none of them. Objective markers donГўв‚¬t block visibility. 32.1 CONTESTING OBJECTIVES Unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed, if an objective is within a modelГўв‚¬s combat range, that model is contesting that objective. If any models in a unit are g g contesting an objective, that unit is contesting that objective. Each unit can only count as contesting a single objective for the purposes of determining objective control (see 32.2). Before determining objective control, for each of their units contesting two or more objectives, the active player must pick one of those objectives for it to contest. T en, their opponent does the same. DesignerГўв‚¬s Note: For purposes other than determining objective control, a unit can contest more than one objective. 32.2 OBJECTIVE CONTROL At the start of the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst battle round and at the end of each turn, follow this sequence for each objective in an order chosen by the active player: 1.  Starting with the active player, each player determines the control score of each of their units that is contesting that objective. A unitГўв‚¬s control score is the combined Control characteristics of all the models in that unit that are contesting the objective. Some abilities modify a unitГўв‚¬s control score, but it cannot be reduced to less than 1. 2.  Each player adds up the control scores of all of their units contesting that objective. T is is their army control score for that objective. 3.  T e players compare their army control scores for that objective. If one playerГўв‚¬s score is higher, that player gains control of that objective. Once a player gains control of an objective, it remains under their control until their opponent gains control of it. 32.3 TERR IN CONTROL Some battleplans require you to gain control of a terrain feature. Terrain features are controlled in the same way as objectives, but terrain features do not remain in your control if your units are no longer contesting them. Each unit can contest 1 objective and 1 terrain feature at the same time. 33.0 END OF BATTLE ROUND At the end of each battle round, the active player can use any Г‚ EndГ‚ ofГ‚ BattleГ‚ RoundГ‚  abilities, then their opponent can do the same. Each battleplan will specify the number of battle rounds that should be fought. If you have completed the number of battle rounds speciГЇВ¬ВЃ ed in your battleplan, the battle has ended, and you should follow the rules included in that battleplan to determine the winner. END OF TURN Г‚ВЌ An objective marker is a 40mm round marker. Г‚ВЌ A model contests an objective if the objective marker is within its combat range. Г‚ВЌ A player gains control of an objective if the sum of the Control characteristics of friendly models contesting that objective is higher than that of enemy models. Г‚ВЌ Check if you gain control of objectives at the start of the ГЇВ¬ВЃ rst battle round and at the end of each turn. Г‚ВЌ An objective remains in your control until your opponent gains controlГ‚ of it. Г‚ВЌ Terrain features are controlled in the same way as objective markers but do not remain in your control if no friendly models are contesting them. Sometimes objective markers S get accidentally nudged g while you are moving models w around. T is is perfectly a ГЇВ¬ВЃne Гўв‚¬" just remember to put ГЇВ¬ВЃ hem back in their proper t positions when determining p objective control. o CORE RULES 27

## Page 29

ADVANCED RULES Each Advanced Rules section is designed to be modular, allowing each battlepack to specify which Advanced Rules to use with that battlepack. Tis book contains the following Advanced Rules sections: COMMANDS (pg 29-30) Tese rules explain how you can earn command points to use powerful commands that allow you to react to abilities and interact in your opponentГўв‚¬s turn. If you are not using the Commands rules, ignore any abilities that have the command point symbol in the corner. TERRIN (pg 31-32) Tese rules go into more detail about how your models interact with terrain features, such as how they can take cover or even draw power from arcane nexuses. MAGIC (pg 33-34) If youГўв‚¬d like to wield unlimited mystical power by including Wizards, Priests and manifestations in your battles, see Magic. ARMY COMPOSITION (pg 35-38) If youГўв‚¬re ready to build your own army roster and customise your army with regiments, battle formations and enhancements, see Army Composition. COMMAND MODELS (pg 39) Here you will ГЇВ¬ВЃnd rules for special models called champions, standard bearers and musicians, which are found in many Warhammer Age of Sigmar units. BATTLE TACTICS (pg 40) Add an additional tactical challenge to your games by including secondary objectives called Battle Tactics. BUILD YOUR OWN BATTLEPACK Tere are many, many ways to play Warhammer Age of Sigmar, from Path to Glory battlepacks, which focus on narrative-driven battles linked in an ongoing campaign, to Spearhead and Matched Play battlepacks, in which the emphasis is on balance and competitive play. In addition to using oГЇВ¬Ж’cial battlepacks, weГўв‚¬d encourage you to use the rules in this section as a toolbox, mixing and matching the elements within to build your own battleplan or battlepack to play with your friends. Are you excited to ГЇВ¬ВЃeld your entire collection against your friends or to see which monster reigns supreme? Maybe you want to design your own battleplans and special rules, or perhaps you want to keep things simple in order to teach a younger sibling how to play. Tese rules provide an open framework that makes each of these things possible. Here are some ideas to get you started: Гўв‚¬Вў Instead of using the Advanced Rules for Army Composition, make an army based on your favourite Black Library story or based on a made-up scenario that sounds exciting to you. For instance, how would 5 diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent Stormcast Eternals heroes fare against a pair of massive Chaos monsters? Гўв‚¬Вў Design your own battleplan by dividing portions of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld into a territory for each player, placing objectives and thinking up a fun twist for the battle. Have fun and experiment with diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent ideas Гўв‚¬" why not try alternating placing objectives instead of having ГЇВ¬ВЃxed locations, or try having two smaller battleГЇВ¬ВЃelds connected by realmgates? Гўв‚¬Вў Learn the Advanced Rules by introducing each module into your battles one at a time Гўв‚¬" for instance, start by experimenting with Commands, then try adding Magic into your battles, and ГЇВ¬ВЃnally put them all together to outwit your opponent and score Battle Tactics. 28 ADVANCED RULES

1.0 COMMANDS OVERVIEW Some abilities, called commands, require that you spend one or more command points ( ) to use that ability. Any ability that has a command point cost (indicated in the top right corner of the ability) is a command. 1.1 EARNING COMMAND POINTS At the start of each battle round, afer determining the underdog, each player gains 4 command points. If there is an underdog, they gain 1 extra command point. At the end of the battle round, the playersГўв‚¬ command points are reset to 0 (any that have not been used are lost). 1.2 USING COMMANDS Commands are used in a similar manner to any other ability. However, each unit can only use 1 command in each phase, each command can only be used 1 time by each army in each phase, and you must spend a number of command points equal to the command point cost to use a command. 2.0 HERO PHASE COMMANDS MAGICAL INTERVENTION: A warrior learned in the mystic arts utters a rapid incantation to disrupt the enemyГўв‚¬s plans. Declare: Pick a friendly Wizard or Priest to use this ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Tat friendly unit can use a Spell or Prayer ability (as appropriate) as if it were your hero phase. If you do so, subtract 1 from casting rolls or chanting rolls made as part of that ability. RLLY: With an inspiring call to action, wounded soldiers get a second wind and fresh troops take the ГЇВ¬ВЃeld to replace their fallen comrades. Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not in combat to use this ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Make 6 rally rolls of D6. For each 4+, you receive 1 rally point. Rally points can be spent in the following ways: Гўв‚¬Вў For each rally point spent, Heal (1) that unit. Гўв‚¬Вў You can spend a number of rally points equal to the Health characteristic of that unit to return a slain model to that unit. You can spend the rally points in any combination of the above. Unspent rally points are then lost. Any Hero Phase Enemy Hero Phase d 1 d 1 3.0 MOVEMENT PHASE COMMANDS AT THE DOUBLE: At a command from their general, the warriors sprint to a key position on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. Used By: Te unit using that Run ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Do not make a run roll as part of that Run ability. Instead, add 6" to that unitГўв‚¬s Move characteristic to determine the distance each model in that unit can move as part of that Run ability. 1 Reaction: You declared a Run ability COMMANDS Г‚ВЌ Any ability with a command point cost ( ) is a command. Г‚ВЌ Each player gains 4Г‚ command points at the start of each battle round. Г‚ВЌ Te underdog gains 1 extra command point. Г‚ВЌ Each unit can only use 1 command in each phase. Г‚ВЌ Each command can only be used 1 time by each army in each phase. Г‚ВЌ You must pay the command point cost to use a command. REDEPLOY: Taking initiative, the warriors hastily reposition to respond to enemy movements. Declare: Pick a friendly unit that is not inГ‚ combat to use this ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Each model in that unit can move up to D6". Tat move cannot pass through or end within the combat range of an enemy unit. Keywords Move, Run Enemy Movement Phase ly 1 29 ADVANCED RULES

ALL-OUT ATTACK: Te warriors ГЇВ¬ВЃght with a frenzied vigour. Used By: Te unit using that Attack ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Add 1 to hit rolls for attacks made as part of that Attack ability. Tis also aГЇВ¬в‚¬ects weapons that have the Companion weapon ability. 4.0 SHOOTING PHASE COMMANDS FORWARD TO VICTORY: With their charge faltering, a rousing command gives the unit renewed energy to reach their foes. Ten, the unit using this ability can move a distance up to its Move characteristic. 1.0 TERRIN OVERVIEW Terrain features add interest and tactical challenges to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. Te battleplan or battlepack you are using will explain how to set up terrain. 1.1 WHOLLY ON TERRIN AND BEHIND TERRIN A model is wholly on a terrain feature if its base is on that terrain feature and no part of its base extends past the edge of that terrain feature. A unit is wholly on a terrain feature if every model in that unit is wholly on that terrain feature. When a unit is targeted by an attack, the unit is considered to be behind a terrain feature if it is impossible to draw a straight line from a model in the attacking unit to a model in the target unit without that line passing across that terrain feature. Ignore parts of the terrain feature within the attacking unitГўв‚¬s combat range for the purposes of determining if the target is behind that terrain feature. ACTIVATE PLACE OF POWER: Te hero draws on the power of a nearby mythical landmark. Declare: Pick a friendly Hero within 3" of any Places of Power to use this ability. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Roll a dice. On a 1, inГЇВ¬вЂљict D3 mortal damage on that Hero. On a 2+: Гўв‚¬Вў If that Hero is a Wizard or Priest, add 1 to casting rolls or chanting rolls for that Hero this turn. Гўв‚¬Вў If that Hero is not a Wizard or Priest, they can use the Гўв‚¬ЛњUnbindГўв‚¬ or Гўв‚¬ЛњBanish ManifestationГўв‚¬ ability this turn as if they had Wizard (1). Keywords Core Start of Any Turn TERRIN 1.2 UNIVERSAL TERRIN ABILITIES Each terrain feature has one or more of the following passive abilities: 1.3 TERRIN SIZES Гўв‚¬Вў Terrain that ГЇВ¬ВЃts into an area no larger than 7" Гѓ- 7" is small. Гўв‚¬Вў Terrain that is not small and ГЇВ¬ВЃts into an area no larger than 7" Гѓ- 12" is medium. Гўв‚¬Вў Terrain that is too big to ГЇВ¬ВЃt into an area 7" Гѓ- 12" is large. 1.4 TERRIN TYPES Each terrain feature in Warhammer Age of Sigmar is one of the following types: Гўв‚¬Вў Obstacle Гўв‚¬Вў Obscuring Terrain Гўв‚¬Вў Area Terrain Гўв‚¬Вў Place of Power Гўв‚¬Вў Faction Terrain Before the battle begins, players must agree on which type applies to each terrain feature. 1.4.1 OBSTACLES Examples: Ruins, debris, statues, barricades Terrain Abilities: Cover, Unstable 1.4.2 OBSCURING TERRIN Examples: Wyldwood, fortress wall Terrain Abilities: Cover, Obscuring, Unstable 1.4.3  AREA TERRIN Examples: Hills, Stormvault Terrain Abilities: Cover 1.4.4 PLACES OF POWER Examples: Realmgate, Cleansing Aqualith, Nexus Syphon Terrain Abilities: Cover, Place of Power, Unstable Cover: Subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that target a unit that is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless that unit charged this turn or has the Fly keyword. Impassable: Models cannot move across, be set up on or end moves on any part of this terrain feature. Obscuring: A unit cannot be targeted by shooting attacks made by enemy units that are not within its combat range if it is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless it has the Fly keyword. Place of Power: Heroes within 3" of this terrain feature can use the Гўв‚¬ЛњActivate Place of PowerГўв‚¬ ability (see below). Unstable: Models can move across but cannot be set up on or end any type of move on any part of this terrain feature that is more than 1" tall. Te Citadel Terrain List (pgГ‚ 65) includes a list of Warhammer Age of Sigmar terrain features and tells you which terrain abilities each of them has. 31 ADVANCED RULES

## Page 33

1.5 FACTION TERRIN Some factions have special terrain features called faction terrain features. Faction terrain features have their own warscrolls. Tey are not considered to be units, with the following exceptions: Гўв‚¬Вў In the combat phase, they are treated as if they were units for the purposes of movement, combat range and being in combat. Units can ГЇВ¬ВЃnish a charge move within Г‚ВЅ" of an enemy faction terrain feature as if it were a unit. Гўв‚¬Вў Tey can be picked as targets of enemy abilities as if they were units. Tey are not aГЇВ¬в‚¬ected by enemy abilities that do not involve picking targets. Гўв‚¬Вў Damage points can be inГЇВ¬вЂљicted on them as if they were units and they can be destroyed. Гўв‚¬Вў If a terrain feature has any melee or ranged weapons, it can use the Гўв‚¬ЛњFightГўв‚¬ and Гўв‚¬ЛњShootГўв‚¬ Core abilities as if it were a unit. Гўв‚¬Вў Terrain features that have a Move characteristic of 0" (Гўв‚¬Лњ-Гўв‚¬) cannot move (note that pile-in moves are a type of move). Some faction terrain features allow you to place a unit on them (this will be clearly speciГЇВ¬ВЃed in that terrain featureГўв‚¬s rules). When doing so, instead of measuring range or visibility to and from the unit that is on that terrain feature, measure to and from the terrain feature instead. Unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃed, units on a faction terrain feature cannot use Fight abilities. If a unit placed on a terrain feature is removed from it (e.g. if an ability removes them from the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld), that unit counts as having lef that terrain feature and loses any special role or beneГЇВ¬ВЃts it had as a result of being placed on it. 1.6 CHARGING UNITS ON TERRIN In some cases, it is not possible to reach an enemy unit using a Charge ability when they are wholly on a terrain feature (e.g. a unit on top of a tower) because the charging unit is unable to end their move mid-way up the terrain feature and there is not enough room for the models to be placed at the top. In these cases, the charging unit can end their charge within Г‚ВЅ" of that terrain feature instead if this would result in the charging unit ending their move inГ‚ combat with any enemy units wholly on a terrain feature. 1.7 SCENERY PIECES Some terrain features, such as wyldwoods or collections of smaller timeworn ruins, are made up of multiple scenery pieces. Each scenery piece that makes up a part of the same terrain feature must be set up so that all of the scenery pieces ГЇВ¬ВЃt into the area that corresponds to the terrain featureГўв‚¬s size (see 1.3). Te full collection of scenery pieces is considered to be a single terrain feature. Te Citadel Terrain List (pgГ‚ 65) shows the number of scenery pieces each terrain feature can consist of. If you are using terrain features that are not included on that list, agree with your opponent before setting up the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld how many scenery pieces make up each terrain feature. Tis battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld uses Citadel terrain, so the players were able to simply look up the terrain type of each terrain feature in the Citadel Terrain List (pg 65). Ruined Domicile (Obstacle) Domicile Shell (Obstacle) Guardian Idol (Obstacle) Cleansing Aqualith (PlaceГ‚ of Power) Nexus Syphon (PlaceГ‚ of Power) Wyldwood (ObscuringГ‚ Terrain) 1 2 3 4 5 6 6 5 1 2 2 1 4 3 3 32 ADVANCED RULES

MAGIC 1.0 WIZARDS AND PRIESTS Wizards are special units that can cast spells, and Priests are special units that can chant prayers. Spells and prayers are powerful abilities that can have a dramatic impact on the battle. 1.1 POWER LEVEL Each Wizard and Priest has a power level, shown in brackets afer the keyword, e.g. Wizard (2). A Wizard or PriestГўв‚¬s power level determines the number of Spell, Prayer or Banish abilities they can use per phase, in any combination. For example, a Wizard (2) unit could use 1 Spell and 1 Banish ability in their commanderГўв‚¬s hero phase. 2.0 SPELLS Te declare step of each spell will tell you to make a casting roll of 2D6. If the roll does not equal or exceed the spellГўв‚¬s casting value (at the top-right corner of the spell), the spell fails and its eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect is not resolved. If the unmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed casting roll includes 2 or more rolls of 1, the spell is miscast: the spell fails, its eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect is not resolved, D3 mortal damage is inГЇВ¬вЂљicted on the Wizard that used it, and that Wizard cannot use any more spells in that phase. Enemy reactions can only be used if the casting roll equals or exceeds the spellГўв‚¬s casting value. So long as the spell is not unbound (seeГ‚ 4.0), then it is successfully cast: resolve its eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect. 3.0 PRYERS Te declare step of each prayer will tell you to make a chanting roll ofГ‚ D6. On an unmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed chanting roll of 1, the prayer fails, its eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect is not resolved and you must remove D3 ritual points from the Priest using the prayer. On an unmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed chanting roll of 2 or more, pick one of the following: Гўв‚¬Вў Give a number of ritual points to the Priest equal to the chanting roll (ritualГ‚ points can be accumulated over multiple turns). Гўв‚¬Вў Add the PriestГўв‚¬s ritual points to the chanting roll. If the chanting roll equals or exceeds the prayerГўв‚¬s chanting value (at the top-right corner of the prayer), it is answered: resolve the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect of the prayer, then reset the PriestГўв‚¬s ritual points total to 0. 4.0 UNBINDING SPELLS Each Wizard can use the Гўв‚¬ЛњUnbindГўв‚¬ reaction a number of times per phase equal to their power level. Tis is an exception to Te Rules of One (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.3). Г‚ВЌ Wizards can cast spells; Priests can chant prayers. Г‚ВЌ Each Wizard and Priest can use a number of spells or prayers per phase equal to their power level. Г‚ВЌ Each Wizard can use a number of Unbind abilities per phase equal to their power level. Г‚ВЌ Each spell or prayer can only be used once per turn. UNBIND: Te wizard saps the energy from an enemyГўв‚¬s spell, nullifying its eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects. Used By: A friendly Wizard within 30" of the enemy Wizard casting the spell. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Make an unbinding roll of 2D6. If the roll exceeds the castingГ‚ roll for the spell, then the spell is unbound and its eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect is not resolved. Tis reaction cannot be used more than once per casting roll. Keywords Unbind Reaction: Opponent declared a Spell ability MYSTIC SHIELD: Te casterГўв‚¬s allies are bathed in an unearthly glow that protects them from harm. Declare: Pick a visible friendly unit wholly within 12" of this Wizard to be the target. Ten, make a casting roll of 2D6. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Te target has Ward (6+) this turn. Keywords Spell Your Hero Phase RESURRECTION: A fallen hero is brought back from death, their vitality fully restored. Declare: Make a chanting roll of D6. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Pick a friendly Infantry Hero that has been slain and return them to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. Set up that Hero wholly within 3" of this Priest. Keywords Prayer Once Per Battle, YourГ‚ HeroГ‚ Phase 7 6 EXAMPLE SPELL EXAMPLE PRYER 33 ADVANCED RULES

## Page 35

BUT KHORNE HATESГ‚ SORCERY! Even though wizards and priests draw upon very diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent sources of power in the lore, the former harnessing the motes of magic that permeate the realms and the latter calling upon the divine might of the gods themselves, such nuances are lost on the average person in the realms! For rules purposes, the miraculous abilities of both wizards and priests are dealt with in the Magic module. 5.0 JEALOUS MAGES AND FICKLE GODS No more than 1 friendly Wizard can cast the same spell per turn, unless that spell has the Unlimited keyword. Likewise, no more than 1 friendly Priest can chant the same prayer per turn, unless that prayer has the Unlimited keyword. Keep in mind that each unit can still only use any given spell or prayer ability once per phase (see Te Rules of One, CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.3). 6.0 KNOWN SPELLS AND PRYERS Wizards and Priests can only use spells or prayers that they know. Each Wizard and Priest knows any spells or prayers on its warscroll, plus all of the spells and prayers in the spell lores, manifestation lores and prayerГ‚ lores you take for your army, as appropriate (see the Army Composition module). 7.0 MANIFESTATIONS Tere are two types of manifestation: endless spells, which can be summoned by Wizards, and invocations, which can be summoned by Priests. Each manifestation has its own warscroll, and the spell or prayer that allows that manifestation to be summoned will be found in the appropriate manifestation lore. No more than 1 friendly Wizard or Priest can attempt to summon the same manifestation per turn, and a friendly Wizard or Priest cannot attempt to summon a friendly manifestation that was removed from play in the same turn. Manifestations are not considered to be units, with the following exceptions: Гўв‚¬Вў Tey are treated as if they were units for the purposes of movement, combat range, being in combat and setting up other units. Units can ГЇВ¬ВЃnish a charge move within Г‚ВЅ" of an enemy manifestation as if it were a unit. Гўв‚¬Вў If they have any melee or ranged weapons, they can use the Гўв‚¬ЛњFightГўв‚¬ and Гўв‚¬ЛњShootГўв‚¬ Core abilities as if they were units. Гўв‚¬Вў If they have a Move characteristic greater than 0" (Гўв‚¬Лњ-Гўв‚¬), they can use Core Move abilities as if they were units. Гўв‚¬Вў Tey can be picked as targets of enemy abilities as if they were units. Tey are not aГЇВ¬в‚¬ected by enemy abilities that do not involve picking targets. Гўв‚¬Вў Damage points can be inГЇВ¬вЂљicted on them as if they were units and they can be destroyed. Гўв‚¬Вў Manifestations that have a Move characteristic of 0" (Гўв‚¬Лњ-Гўв‚¬) cannot move. For the purposes of movement, combat range, being in combat and setting up other units, they are only treated as if they were units in the combat phase. Units can ГЇВ¬ВЃnish a charge move within Г‚ВЅ" of an enemy manifestation that has a Move characteristic of 0" (Гўв‚¬Лњ-Гўв‚¬) instead of within Г‚ВЅ" of an enemy unit. Гўв‚¬Вў Models can move through manifestations but cannot end a move on them. 7.1 SEVERED CONNECTION If the Wizard or Priest that summoned a manifestation is slain, that manifestation is removed from the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. 7.2 BANISHING MANIFESTATIONS All Wizards and Priests can use the Гўв‚¬ЛњBanish ManifestationГўв‚¬ ability: BANISH MANIFESTATION: Te wizard or priest disrupts the arcane forces sustaining a manifestation, wiping it from existence. Declare: Pick a friendly Wizard or Priest to use this ability, pick a manifestation within 30" of them to be the target, then make a banishment roll of 2D6. Add 1 to the banishment roll for each additional enemy manifestation on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld afer the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: If the banishment roll equals or exceeds the banishment value listed on the manifestationГўв‚¬s warscroll, it is banished and removed from play. You cannot pick the same manifestation as the target of this ability more than once per turn. Keywords Banish Your Hero Phase ADVANCED RULES

ARMY COMPOSITION 1.0 GETTING STARTED 1.1 ARMY ROSTER Tese rules will explain how to create an army roster to prepare for a battle. You can go to warhammer-community.com to download a printable copy. 1.2 POINTS LIMIT Before you start ГЇВ¬ВЃlling your roster, you and your opponent must agree on a points limit for the battle. You can agree on any points limit, but most players use a limit of 1000 points, 2000 points or somewhere in between. No more than half of your points can be spent on a single unit. 1.2.1 UNDERSPENDING In many cases, the total points cost of the units in your army will not add up to the exact points limit, but you might not have enough points lef to add another unit. If the points cost of your army is 50 or more points lower than the points limit of the battle, you gain 1 extra command point at the start of the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst battle round. 1.3 BATTLE PROFILES Te information youГўв‚¬ll need when building your army roster is found in each unitГўв‚¬s battle proГЇВ¬ВЃle. Battle proГЇВ¬ВЃles for each unit are available at warhammer-community.com. 2.0 FACTIONS Te ГЇВ¬ВЃrst thing you need to do when building your army roster is to pick your faction (e.g. Stormcast Eternals or Skaven). Each faction has its own warscrolls, battle proГЇВ¬ВЃles and faction rules. Tese can be found in various publications but most commonly in the factionГўв‚¬s battletome. 2.1 BATTLE FORMATIONS Many factions contain one or more battle formations. Each battle formation grants unique beneГЇВ¬ВЃts to your army. Te faction rules for your faction will explain any battle formation options. 2.2 ARMIES OF RENOWN Some factions have access to rules for themed armies called Armies of Renown. If you choose to use an Army of Renown, the rules for that Army of Renown replace the normal faction rules for that army. 3.0 ADDING UNITS 3.1 REGIMENTS Armies are made up of one or more regiments, each of which is led by a Hero. You must have at least 1 regiment in your army, and you can include a maximum of 5 regiments. To add a regiment, pick 1 Hero from your faction, then pick up to 3Г‚ non-Hero units to accompany them. Each HeroГўв‚¬s battle proГЇВ¬ВЃle lists which units can be added to their regiment, and each non-Hero unitГўв‚¬s battle proГЇВ¬ВЃle lists any relevant keywords it has. Te battle proГЇВ¬ВЃles of some Heroes (such as named characters) may say that they can be added to the regiment of another Hero. Battle proГЇВ¬ВЃles are regularly updated to improve balance. When we republish them, the new version takes precedence over versions with an earlier publication date or no publication date. Te latest points values are available in the Warhammer Age of Sigmar app, and points updates are available at warhammer-community.com ADVANCED RULES

---

## Page 37

3.2 THE GENERL You must pick 1 Hero in your army that is leading a regiment to be your general. If any units in your army have the Warmaster keyword, you must pick one of those units to be your general. Your generalГўв‚¬s regiment can include up to 4Г‚ non-Hero units in addition to your general. 3.3 REINFORCED UNITS When you add a unit to your army roster, you can add it as a reinforced unit. A reinforced unit has twice as many models as its minimum unit size and costs twice as many points. If a unit has a minimum unit size ofГ‚ 1, it cannot be reinforced. 3.4 UNIQUE UNITS You cannot include the same Unique unit more than once in your army. Unique units cannot be reinforced. 3.5 REGIMENTS OF RENOWN In addition to creating your own regiments, you can also spend points to include 1 Regiment of Renown. Regiments of Renown are pre-built regiments, each with their own special abilities. Te rules for each Regiment of Renown will specify which factions can include it. A unit in a Regiment of Renown cannot be your general even if it is a Warmaster. Гўв‚¬Вў You cannot include more than 1 Regiment of Renown in your army unless otherwise specified in the notes column of that regimentГўв‚¬s battle profile. Гўв‚¬Вў Units in a Regiment of Renown cannot use (but can be picked as the target of, if otherwise eligible) any faction rules from the faction they are allied into, including enhancements and lores, unless they have a keyword that matches that factionГўв‚¬s name. Гўв‚¬Вў If an ability allows you to set up a replacement unit (Core Rules, 24.2) for a unit in a Regiment of Renown, that unit also counts as being part of that Regiment of Renown. Г‚ВЌ Pick a points limit: 1000 points or 2000 points. If other points limit, agree with opponent. Г‚ВЌ Pick your faction. Г‚ВЌ Pick your battle formation (if any). Г‚ВЌ Form regiments. Each regiment has 1 Hero and 0-3 non-Hero units (0-4 if your generalГўв‚¬s regiment). Г‚ВЌ Add auxiliary units (if any). Auxiliary units are units that arenГўв‚¬t part of a regiment. If your opponent has more auxiliary units than you, you receive 1 extra command point each battle round. Г‚ВЌ Add faction terrain features (if any). Г‚ВЌ Pick enhancements (1 from each enhancement table). Г‚ВЌ Pick up to 1 spell lore, 1 prayer lore and 1 manifestation lore. 3.6 AUXILIARY UNITS Auxiliary units allow you to take any units from your faction without the constraints of regiments, at the cost of potentially giving your opponent an advantage. You can add any number of units to the auxiliary units section of your army roster. However, the player with the fewest auxiliary units on their roster gains 1 extra command point at the start of each battle round (if the players have the same number of auxiliary units, neither player receives an extra command point). Heroes that have compulsory regiment options cannot be taken as auxiliary units. 3.7 FACTION TERRIN FEATURES If your faction has a faction terrain feature, or a set of faction terrain features, you can pick 1 to include on your army roster. Faction terrain features do not cost any points. 4.0 FINISHING TOUCHES 4.1 ENHANCEMENTS Each set of faction rules include a number of enhancements that can be given to units, e.g. heroic traits, mount traits and artefacts of power. You can take 1 enhancement from each enhancement table in your factionГ‚ rules. Each enhancement table lists which units are eligible to be given that enhancement. Unique units cannot be given enhancements. While some abilities allow you to take extra enhancements, the same unit can never have more than 1 enhancement of the same type, and you can never include the same enhancement in your army more than once. 4.2 LORES You can pick 1 spell lore available to your faction. If you do, all Wizards in your army know all spells from that lore. You can pick 1 prayer lore available to your faction. If you do, all Priests in your army know all prayers from that lore. You can pick 1 manifestation lore available to your faction. If you do, all Wizards in your army know all spells from that lore, and all Priests know all prayers from that lore. 36 ADVANCED RULES

COMMAND MODELS 1.0 CHAMPIONS Units with the Champion keyword have one or more champion models. If there is a number afer the Champion keyword, that number indicates the proportion of models in that unit that can be champions. In any other case, 1 model in the unit can be a champion. Units with the Champion keyword have the following passive ability: CHAMPION: A veteran ГЇВ¬ВЃghter leads the charge. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Add 1 to the Attacks characteristic of weapons used by champions in this unit. STANDARD BEARER: Te unitГўв‚¬s standard provides a rallying point in the chaos of battle. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: While this unit contains any standard bearers, add 1 to this unitГўв‚¬s control score. MUSICIAN: Te beat of a drum or the blast of a horn drives the soldiers ever onward. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: While this unit contains any musicians, if it uses the Гўв‚¬ЛњRallyГўв‚¬ command, you can make one additional rally roll of D6. Passive Passive Passive 2.0 MUSICIANS Units with the Musician keyword have one or more musician models. Te number afer the Musician keyword indicates the proportion of models in that unit that can be musicians. Units with the Musician keyword have the following passive ability: 3.0 STANDARD BEARERS Units with the Standard Bearer keyword have one or more standardГ‚ bearer models. Te number afer the Standard Bearer keyword indicates the proportion of models in that unit that can be standard bearers. Units with the Standard Bearer keyword have the following passive ability: If a unit had Champion (1/10) on its keywords bar, then 1 model in that unit could be a champion for every 10 models in the unit. Let your opponent know which models in a unit are champions, musicians and standard bearers if itГўв‚¬s not already clear from the miniatures. Clanrat Standard Bearer Clanrat Champion Clanrat Musician 39 ADVANCED RULES

## Page 41

BATTLE TACTICS 1.0 BATTLE TACTICS OVERVIEW During a battle, each player can score extra victory points by completing battle tactics. 2.0 PICKING BATTLE TACTICS Battle tactics are picked by using the Гўв‚¬ЛњTactical GambitГўв‚¬ ability, below. 2.1 COMPLETING BATTLE TACTICS If, at the end of your turn, you have completed the battle tactic you chose to attempt this turn, you score 4 victory points. 3.0 UNIVERSAL BATTLE TACTICS TACTICAL GAMBIT: You order your troops to seize an opportunity that has arisen amidst the ГЇВ¬вЂљow of battle. You cannot use this ability if you went second in the previous battle round and chose to go ГЇВ¬ВЃrst in the current battle round. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Pick 1 battle tactic that you have not yet attempted. You can attempt to complete that battle tactic this turn. Once Per Battle Round, Start of Your Turn DO NOT WAVER: We must not falter on any front. Stand fast, and keep the enemy at bay. You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 2 or more friendly units fought this turn and no friendly units were destroyed this turn. SLAY THE ENTOURGE: Leave the enemyГўв‚¬s commander exposed by targeting members of their personal guard. Pick a unit in the enemy generalГўв‚¬s regiment. You complete this battle tactic if that unit is destroyed this turn. ATTACK ON TWO FRONTS: Divide and conquer by approaching the foe from two directions. You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you control 2 or more objectives that you did not control at the start of your turn and at least 1 of those objectives was controlled by your opponent at the start of your turn. TAKE THEIR LAND: Secure a foothold within enemy territory. Pick a terrain feature wholly or partially within enemy territory and wholly outside friendly territory. You complete this battle tactic if you control that terrain feature at the end of your turn. SEIZE THE CENTRE: Secure a strong central position from which your forces can dictate the ГЇВ¬вЂљow of battle. You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if 2 or more friendly units are within 3" of the centre of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld and are not in combat. TAKE THE FLANKS: Surround the enemy, then close in for the kill. You complete this battle tactic at the end of your turn if you have at least 1 friendly unit within 6" of each short battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld edge, none of those units are wholly within friendly territory, and none of those units were set up this turn. Г‚ВЌ Battle tactics are picked using the Гўв‚¬ЛњTactical GambitГўв‚¬ ability at the start of your turn and are completed at the end of your turn. Г‚ВЌ Each battle tactic is worth 4Г‚ victory points. 40 ADVANCED RULES

## Page 42

MATCHED PLAY BATTLEPACK BORDER WAR 1. PICK YOUR ARMIES Each player picks an army using the rules in Army Composition (pg 35). 2. SET UP THE BATTLEFIELD Te players roll oГЇВ¬в‚¬. Te winner chooses which player is the attacker and which is the defender. Next, the defender sets up objectives in the locations indicated by gold circles on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld map p . Ten, the defender sets up terrain features. Each terrain feature must be set up more than 3" from the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld edge, more than 6" from all other terrain features and more than 3" from all objectives. Afer objectives and terrain have been set up, the attacker picks which territory is their territory. Te other territory is the defenderГўв‚¬s territory. Te players then resolve the deployment phase. Te attacker begins deployment (CoreГ‚ Rules, 10.0). VICTORY POINTS Each player scores victory points at the end of each of their turns as follows: Гўв‚¬Вў Score 2 victory points if you control at least 1 objective. Гўв‚¬Вў Score 2 victory points if you control 2 or more objectives. Гўв‚¬Вў Score 2 victory points if you control more objectives than your opponent. ADVANCED RULES In addition to the Core Rules, Matched Play: Border War uses the following Advanced Rules: Гўв‚¬Вў Commands (pg 29) Гўв‚¬Вў Terrain (pg 31) Гўв‚¬Вў Magic (pg 33) Гўв‚¬Вў Army Composition (pg 35) Гўв‚¬Вў Command Models (pg 39) Гўв‚¬Вў Battle Tactics (pg 40) BATTLEFIELD SIZE For a 1000-point battle, we recommend a 30"Г‚ Гѓ-Г‚ 44" battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld with 4Г‚ terrain features. For a 2000-point battle, we recommend a 44"Г‚ Гѓ-Г‚ 60" battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld with 8Г‚ terrain features. If you have agreed on a points limit outside these bounds, feel free to adjust the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld size and number of terrain features appropriately. 41 MATCHED PLAY

## Page 43

N BATTLEPLAN BORDER WAR Two armies are determined to capture vital ground that separates their domains. From there, they can strike deep into enemy territory. TWIST: Score 2 additional victory points if you control the objective wholly within enemy territory. If there is an underdog, they score 1 additional victory point for each objective they control that is within both playersГўв‚¬ territories. ATTACKERГўв‚¬S TERRITORY DEFENDERГўв‚¬S TERRITORY BATTLE LENGTH Border War battles last for 4 battle rounds. GLORIOUS VICTORY Te player with the most victory points at the end of the battle wins a majorГ‚ victory. If the players are tied on victory points at the end of the battle, and only one playerГўв‚¬s general has survived the battle, that player wins a minor victory. If the players are tied on victory points and both generals or neither general survived the battle, the battle is a draw. Protected by armour forged by the gods themselves, the Stormcast Eternals are indomitable upon the ГЇВ¬ВЃeld of battle Гўв‚¬" but for every gleaming warrior amongst their ranks, half a dozen Skaven sharpen their knives in the shadowsГўв‚¬В¦ 42 MATCHED PLAY

## Page 46

Te Age of Sigmar grows fraught. On horizons yonder, the forces of Chaos loom, threatening to engulf the Mortal Realms in mayhem and violence once more. In the wake of these troubling times, Spearhead forces are dispatched across the realms to seek out ancient sites of power that still reside deep within the uncharted hinterlands. Will you take up the call to battle and lead a Spearhead force to victory? SPEARHEAD Welcome to the world of Spearhead. In this game mode, players engage in small-scale battles that are fast and tactical, suitable for newcomers and veterans alike. In Spearhead, each army is available as a self-contained boxГ‚ set, allowing players to quickly muster a force. In addition, Spearhead battles are fought upon a compact battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld, meaning you can set up a game even with limited space. Spearhead uses specially tailored rules to ensure that every battle is a tense and exciting aГЇВ¬в‚¬air that comes right down to the ГЇВ¬ВЃnal moments. SPEARHEAD BATTLEPACKS Veteran players will notice similarities between the rules on Spearhead warscrolls and those found in battletomes. However, they have been tweaked to make sure they work speciГЇВ¬ВЃcally with Spearhead. Tis means aГ‚ battletome is not required to play Spearhead Гўв‚¬" all the rules you need to play are available to download for free from warhammer-community.com. Tis makes Spearhead the ideal format for newcomers to Warhammer Age of Sigmar, as well as for players looking for a quick and casual format with lots of re- playability and tactical depth. SPEARHEAD BOX SETS Fighting a Spearhead battle could not be easier. Each player picks their army by choosing one of the many Spearhead box sets available. Each of these is a complete army, including all the miniatures a player will need to do battle. Te rules for 25 diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent Spearhead box sets are available to download for free from warhammer-community.com, allowing you to get stuck in as soon as your models are assembled.

---

## Page 47

SPEARHEAD AND BEYOND Once you have mastered one Spearhead army, you may ГЇВ¬ВЃnd yourself looking for a fresh challenge. Some players choose to Гўв‚¬Лњcollect broadГўв‚¬ Гўв‚¬" this means picking up diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent Spearhead box sets to use with Spearhead battlepacks. Perhaps you want to see how a diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent faction plays in Spearhead, or you want to collect a Spearhead army from each of the Grand Alliances: Order, Chaos, Death and Destruction. Other players choose to Гўв‚¬Лњcollect deepГўв‚¬. Tis means picking up further units from their Spearhead armyГўв‚¬s faction to build an army they can use with diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent battlepacks, such as Path to Glory and Matched Play battlepacks. If you choose to do so, picking up your factionГўв‚¬s battletome should be your ГЇВ¬ВЃrst port of call. BATTLETOMES Battletomes are the ultimate guide to the diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent factions of the Mortal Realms. Each is ГЇВ¬ВЃlled with detailed lore, inspiring artwork and awesome showcases of miniatures. Crucially, they contain the faction rules and warscrolls youГўв‚¬ll need to use your army in most games of Warhammer Age of Sigmar. HOW SHOULD I BUILD MYГ‚ MODELS? When assembling the miniatures in your Spearhead box, you may ГЇВ¬ВЃnd options to build one of two diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent units. Check the Spearhead rules for the army to see which unit to build. Many more units will have options, such as adding a champion, standard bearer and musician, or perhaps a special weapon or two. While the rules for these options have in most cases been simpliГЇВ¬ВЃed in Spearhead, we still recommend you assemble your units with these extras. Not only will they look glorious on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld, if you decide to use the army in other battlepacks, youГўв‚¬ll be able to take advantage of the additional rules they provide! Tree-Revenant Musician Sylvaneth With Spearhead, setting up a game of Warhammer Age of Sigmar with friends has never been quicker or simpler. JustГ‚ put your Spearhead force on the table, shuГЇВ¬вЂћe your cards and get playing! 46 SPEARHEAD

Гўв‚¬Вў Hailing from one of Warhammer Age of SigmarГўв‚¬s many factions, each Spearhead army oГЇВ¬в‚¬ers not only a diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent set of miniatures but a unique theme and playstyle. PICK YOUR REGIMENT ABILITY AND ENHANCEMENT Each Spearhead army has a choice of 2 diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent regiment abilities and 4 diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent enhancements for your general. At the start of the battle, you must pick 1 of each for your army (see Spearhead Armies, pg 49). 5. FIGHT THE BATTLE! Spearhead battles usually last around an hour and a half. During the battle, the players will each try to score victory points, and at the end of the battle, the player with the most is declared the winner! 4. PREPARE YOUR WARSCROLLS Each set of Spearhead rules includes the warscrolls for all the units used in your Spearhead army. A warscroll details how fast and tough a unit is, as well as what weapons it is armed with and any special abilities it has. Гўв‚¬Вў Every Spearhead army has a set of battle traits, regiment abilities and enhancements. Гўв‚¬Вў Te battle traits are rules and abilities for your army that you will use in every battle. Гўв‚¬Вў Te regiment abilities and enhancements are picked at the start of every battle. You can experiment with diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent combinations to see what works best for you. Some regiment abilities and enhancements are better against certain foes, so it pays to study the strengths and weaknesses of the enemy before making your choice! Гўв‚¬Вў Every unit in a Spearhead army has its own warscroll, where you can see its characteristics and weapons. Гўв‚¬Вў Any unique abilities the unit can use will also be found on its warscroll. Гўв‚¬Вў At the bottom of every warscroll you can ГЇВ¬ВЃnd its keywords, such as Hero, Wizard, Monster or Infantry. 48 SPEARHEAD

Any Movement Phase 10 SPEARHEAD BATTLEPACK FIRE AND JADE 1.0 SPEARHEAD ARMIES Each player in a Spearhead battle is the commander of a Spearhead army. A Spearhead army is a set of units that comes with its own rules and warscrolls. You can download the rules for 25 diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent Spearhead armies for free from warhammer-community.com. 1.1 ARMY COMPOSITION On the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst page of every Spearhead armyГўв‚¬s rules, you will ГЇВ¬ВЃnd the army composition. Tis lists which units are included in the Spearhead army and who the general is. Unlike other Warhammer Age of Sigmar armies, the composition of a Spearhead army is ГЇВ¬ВЃxed and cannot be changed. 1.2 BATTLE TRITS Every Spearhead army has one or more battle traits. Tese are powerful and thematic abilities that apply to some or all of the units in the army. For example, the Гўв‚¬ЛњHoly OrdersГўв‚¬ battle trait enables the Stormcast Eternals to use powerful once-per-battle abilities to smite their foes, while the Гўв‚¬ЛњGnawhole AmbushГўв‚¬ battle trait allows Skaven warriors to arrive on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld behind enemy lines. 1.3 REGIMENT ABILITIES Every Spearhead army has a choice of 2 regiment abilities, each of which provides the army with a diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent strength in battle. 1.4 ENHANCEMENTS Every Spearhead army has a set of 4 enhancements to pick from. Tese are upgrades for the armyГўв‚¬s general that make them more powerful in a variety of ways. 1.5 SPEARHEAD WARSCROLLS Te warscrolls for the units in each Spearhead army are included with the other rules for the army. Although Spearhead warscrolls are similar to the warscrolls found in battletomes, they have been tailored to work speciГЇВ¬ВЃcally with Spearhead and cannot be used with other battlepacks. WIZARDS AND PRIESTS IN SPEARHEAD Some units in Spearhead have the Wizard or Priest keyword. If you are familiar with other battlepacks, it is important to note the Magic module in the Advanced Rules is not used in Spearhead. As a result, there are no unbinding rolls and no chance of miscasts (among other things). Spells and prayers are resolved like any other ability. THE CORE RULES To play a game using this battlepack, youГўв‚¬ll need the Core Rules. Te Core Rules explain the basics of Warhammer Age of Sigmar, such as how to move your models and how to use abilities. You can ГЇВ¬ВЃnd the Core Rules on pages 5-27. You donГўв‚¬t need the Advanced Rules to use this battlepack. 49 SPEARHEAD

THY LIFE IS A WEAPON Гўв‚¬" WIELD IT WELL TERRITORY A TERRITORY A TERRITORY B TERRITORY B BATTLEPLAN FIRE AND JADE As the Hour of Ruin dawns, the ГЇВ¬ВЃght to secure sacred sites and geomantic nexuses rages ГЇВ¬ВЃercer than ever. It is in Aqshy and Ghyran that the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst battles of this new age are fought, each faction sending out vanguard forces to take and hold these places of power in the name of their deities Гўв‚¬" or even the godbeasts of myth. So unpredictable are these frontiers of war that the land itself can lend its blessing to the chosen or send wracking curses against those who incur its wrath. DEPLOYMENT Do not deploy armies as described in the Core Rules. Instead, the attacker sets up all the units in their army ГЇВ¬ВЃrst, followed by the defender. Each unit must be set up wholly within friendly territory and more than 6" from enemy territory. BATTLE LENGTH Te battle lasts for 4 battle rounds. SPECIAL RULES PREPARING THE SPEARHEAD DECK Tis battle uses the Fire and Jade Spearhead deck. One player takes the twist deck that corresponds to the side of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld the players are ГЇВ¬ВЃghting on, shuГЇВ¬вЂћes it and places it face down next to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld (the other twist deck is not used). Ten, each player takes 1 battle tactic deck, shuГЇВ¬вЂћes it and places it face down where they can reach it during the battle. 55 SPEARHEAD TACTIC Гўв‚¬Вў AND Гўв‚¬Вў e warriors assume lunts the blows of geted by that acteristic of his phase. Reaction: Opponent declared an Attack ability nemy territory and actic at the end of your re wholly within n combat. START OF THE BATTLE ROUND SEQUENCE Te following sequence is resolved at the start of eachГ‚ battle round: 1. If it is the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst battle round, the attacker chooses who will take the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst turn. In any other battle round, the players make a priority roll (CoreГ‚ Rules, 12.0). 2. Determine the underdog (CoreГ‚ Rules, 12.0). 3. Te twist card is drawn (pg 50). 4. Te players draw battle tactic cards (see below). 5. Any abilities with the Г‚ StartГ‚ ofГ‚ BattleГ‚ RoundГ‚  timing are used. TWIST CARDS One twist card is drawn at the start of each battle round. Follow the rules on it as soon as it is drawn. BATTLE TACTIC CARDS At the start of the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst battle round, each player draws a hand of 3Г‚ battleГ‚ tacticГ‚ cards. At the start of each subsequent battle round, each player draws battle tactic cards until they once more have a hand of 3 battle tactic cards. Before they do so, they can discard any number of battle tactic cards inГ‚ their hand. Cards are discarded face-up. VICTORY POINTS Each player scores victory points at the end of each of their turns as follows: Гўв‚¬Вў Score 1 victory point if you control at least one objective. Гўв‚¬Вў Score 1 victory point if you control two or more objectives. Гўв‚¬Вў Score 1 victory point if you control more objectives than your opponent. Гўв‚¬Вў Score 1 victory point for each battle tactic you completed this turn. GLORIOUS VICTORY Te player with the most victory points at the end of the battle is the winner. If the players are tied on victory points, the battle is a draw. SEIZING THE INITIATIVE If the player who went second in the previous battle round wins the priority roll and chooses to go ГЇВ¬ВЃrst, it is called seizing the initiative. When a player seizes the initiative, they do not draw any battle tactic cards for that battle round unless they are the underdog and the diГЇВ¬в‚¬erence in victory points between the two players is 5 or greater. 56 SPEARHEAD orneГўв‚¬s presence is blood be spilled y point at the nit destroyed in ers is the iendly unit Add 1 to hit hat unit this T Гўв‚¬Вў ST Гўв‚¬Вў ESSING: Te uch of Nurgle, ted by sputum, e eГЇВ¬в‚¬ects below. rs roll oГЇВ¬в‚¬ and ГЇВ¬в‚¬ect. unit on the ls for that unit unit on the made for that IC Гўв‚¬Вў he nd to not in D6". Reaction: Opponent declared an Attack ability my territory and c at the end of your wholly within combat. 2.0 REALM BATTLEFIELDS Spearhead battles are fought on realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃelds. Once the players have picked their Spearhead armies, they must agree on a realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld to use. Each realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld comes with a matching SpearheadГ‚ deck, along with a battleplan that explains how to ГЇВ¬ВЃght a battle on that realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. A realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld is 30"Г‚ Гѓ-Г‚ 22" and double-sided, with each side corresponding to a diГЇВ¬в‚¬erent Mortal Realm. Te ГЇВ¬ВЃrst realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld is Fire and Jade, which has one side representing Aqshy, the Realm of Fire, and the y,y reverse representing Ghyran, the Realm of Life. 3.0 SPEARHEAD DECKS Spearhead decks are sets of cards that are used in Spearhead battles. Each realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld has its own Spearhead deck. Each SpearheadГ‚ deck consists of 2 twist decks (one for each side of the realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld) and 2 battle tactic decks (one for each player). 3.1 TWIST CARDS Each twist deck is made up of k twist cards. A twist is a special rule that applies to the battle round being played. Each deck of twist cards corresponds to a speciГЇВ¬ВЃc side of the realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld. 3.2 BATTLE TACTIC CARDS Each battle tactic deck is made up of k k battle tactic cards. Te rules on each are split into 2 parts: a battle tactic and a command. You can either score the battle tactic at the end of your turn if you meet its conditions (to gain victory points) or you can use the command on the card for a more immediate beneГЇВ¬ВЃt. You cannot do both, so choose wisely! Each command will say when it can be used and what eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect it has. Afer the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect has been resolved, the card is discarded. You can issue multiple commands to the same unit in the same phase. 4.0 SPEARHEAD BATTLEPLANS Each realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld has a corresponding battleplan. Te battleplan is a set of instructions on how to ГЇВ¬ВЃght the battle and how to determine the winner. You can ГЇВ¬ВЃnd the battleplan for the Fire and Jade realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld on pages 55-56. 50 SPEARHEAD

## Page 52

5.0 TERRIN In every Spearhead battle, the armies ГЇВ¬ВЃght around 2Г‚ large terrain features and 2 small terrain features. Before the armies are deployed, each player will set up 1 large terrain feature and 1 small terrain feature wholly within their territory. Each terrain feature has certain passive abilities depending onГ‚ its size: Large Terrain Feature: Cover, Obscuring, Unstable Small Terrain Feature: Cover, Unstable COVER: Subtract 1 from hit rolls for attacks that target a unit that is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless that unit charged or has the Fly keyword. OBSCURING: A unit cannot be targeted by shooting attacks if it is behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless it has the Fly keyword. UNSTABLE: Models can move across but cannot be set up on or end any type of move on any part of this terrain feature that is more than 1" tall. Ruined Domicile (Large Terrain Feature) Ravaged Shrine (Small Terrain Feature) Ruined Domicile (Large Terrain Feature) Ravaged Shrine (Small Terrain Feature) 51 SPEARHEAD

Each objective is named afer a mythical godbeast, as shown below: D r a c o t h i o n V u l c a t r i x N a g e n d r a I g n a x B e h e m a t 7.0 REINFORCEMENTS Some units have the Reinforcements keyword. Tese units can be brought back to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld afer being destroyed as replacement units (CoreГ‚ Rules, 24.2). If any of your units have the Reinforcements keyword, you can use the following ability: CALL FOR REINFORCEMENTS: A second wave of troops enters the fray, replacing those lost to the rigours of battle. Declare: Pick a friendly Reinforcements unit that has been destroyed. EГЇВ¬в‚¬ect: Set up an identical replacement unit on the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld, wholly within friendly territory, wholly within 6" of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld edge and notГ‚ in combat. Each Reinforcements unit can only be replaced once. Replacement units cannot themselves be replaced. Keywords Core Once Per Turn, Your Movement Phase 6.0 OBJECTIVES In Spearhead battles, objectives are printed on the surface of each realm battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld (the objective is the whole circle, not just the symbol in the centre). 6.1 CONTESTING OBJECTIVES In Spearhead battles, while a model is on an objective (wholly or partially), it is contesting it. Units with the Reinforcements keyword have this icon in the top-right corner of their warscrolls for ease of use. 52 SPEARHEAD

A SPEARHEAD BATTLE Below you can see a game of Spearhead that is underway. Upon the scorched plains of the Great Parch in Aqshy, a force of Stormcast Eternals clashes with a Skaven clawpack that seeks to bring ruin to a Dawnbringer outpost. Tis unit of Clanrats has been destroyed but it can return to the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld because their warscroll has the Reinforcements keyword. Flying units, such as these Prosecutors, can soar directly over other models and terrain, meaning they are very good at capturing objectives at the lastГ‚ moment. A playerГўв‚¬s battle tactics are normally kept secret from their opponent. Te large terrain features block visibility, so units can hide behind them Гўв‚¬" but not from units with Fly! 8 8 9 9 10 10 1 1 2 53 SPEARHEAD

## Page 55

Te twist deck is placed at the side of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld in reach of both players. Each battle round, a twist card is drawn, which can lead to interesting opportunities or hazards for either player. Generally, twists favour the underdog. Because he has a ranged weapon in the form of a Warplock Musket, the Warlock Engineer can hang back and shoot whilst also contesting an objective. Each player has a hand of battle tactic cards, which tell them what they should be trying to achieve. Battle tactics can instead be used as commands for a more immediate beneГЇВ¬ВЃt. Te unit of Liberators is contesting the Dracothion objective in the centre of the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld, but the Clanrats to their ГЇВ¬вЂљank are in a good position to charge and swarm the objective. Each Spearhead army has a general, a powerful unit that has access to enhancements. Here, a Lord-Vigilant astride a Gryph-stalker is locked in a duel with a devious Clawlord mounted on a Gnaw-beast. Six-sided dice and measuring sticks (or tape measures) are your essential tools in any Warhammer Age of Sigmar battle. Te 3" measuring stick is perfect for measuring combatГ‚ ranges. 6 6 7 7 5 5 4 4 3 3 2 54 SPEARHEAD

Keywords Core, Attack, Shoot Your Shooting Phase Your Charge Phase Any Combat Phase UNIVERSAL WEAPON ABILITIES Anti-X (+1 Rend): Add 1 to this weaponГўв‚¬s Rend characteristic if the target has the keyword afer Гўв‚¬ЛњAnti-Гўв‚¬ or fulГЇВ¬ВЃls the condition afer Гўв‚¬ЛњAnti-Гўв‚¬. Multiples of this ability are cumulative. For example, if a weapon has both Anti-charge (+1 Rend) and Anti-Hero (+1 Rend), then add 2 to the Rend characteristic of the weapon for attacks that target a Hero that charged in the same turn. Charge (+1 Damage): Add 1 to this weaponГўв‚¬s Damage characteristic if the attacking unit charged this turn. Companion: Unless otherwise speciГЇВ¬ВЃed, this weapon is not aГЇВ¬в‚¬ected by friendly abilities that aГЇВ¬в‚¬ect weapon characteristics or the attack sequence, except for those that apply negative modiГЇВ¬ВЃers to it (e.g.Г‚ Гўв‚¬ЛњCovering FireГўв‚¬). Crit (2 Hits): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack scores 2 hits on the target unit instead of 1. Make a wound roll for each hit. Crit (Auto-wound): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack automatically wounds the target. Make a save roll as normal. Crit (Mortal): If an attack made with this weapon scores a criticalГ‚ hit, that attack inГЇВ¬вЂљicts mortal damage on the target unit equal to the Damage characteristic of that weapon and the attack sequence ends. Shoot in Combat: Tis weapon can be used to make shooting attacks even if the attacking unit is in combat. 58 SPEARHEAD

GLOSSARY abilities: Te things that units can do in the game. Some abilities, like Гўв‚¬ЛњNormal MoveГўв‚¬ and Гўв‚¬ЛњFightГўв‚¬, are common to all units; others are speciГЇВ¬ВЃc to certain units and only appear on their warscroll. Sometimes the players themselves use abilities. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.0) active player: Te player whose turn is taking place. Tere are 2 turns in a battle round. Te active player can use abilities with the Гўв‚¬ЛњYour PhaseГўв‚¬ timing. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 12.0) allocate: Damage points are allocated one at a time to a unit afer all the damage points inГЇВ¬вЂљicted by an ability have been totalled in the damage pool. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 18.2) All-out Attack: Command, 1CP, reaction. Attacking unit adds 1 to hit rolls. (Commands, 6.0) All-out Defence: Command, 1CP, reaction. Target unit adds 1 to save rolls. (Commands, 7.0) Anti-X (+1 Rend): Weapon ability. +1 Rend for attacks that target units that fulГЇВ¬ВЃl the condition afer Гўв‚¬ЛњAnti-Гўв‚¬. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) Any (Гўв‚¬В¦) Phase: Timing. Either player can use abilities with this timing, regardless of who the active player is. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 13.0) Army of Renown: A special type of army that uses a modiГЇВ¬ВЃed set of faction rules. (Army Composition, 2.2) army: All of a playerГўв‚¬s units are collectively referred to as their army. (Core Rules, 1.0) army composition: Te step before a battle in which the players build their armies. Te battlepack may specify in more detail what they can and canГўв‚¬t include in their armies. army roster: Te list of units in a playerГўв‚¬s army, and any other important information like enhancements. Players can check each otherГўв‚¬s rosters any time they like. (Army Composition, 1.1) artefact of power: Enhancement. You can give 1 Hero in your army an artefact of power. (Army Composition, 4.1) At the Double: Command, 1CP, reaction. Add 6 to Move instead of making the run roll. (Commands, 3.0) attack sequence: Make hit roll, wound roll and save roll, then determine damage points inГЇВ¬вЂљicted. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.0) auxiliary unit: A unit on your army roster that is not part of a regiment. (Army Composition, 3.6) Banish Manifestation: Your Hero Phase, 30", banishment roll of 2D6. Equal or beat banishment value of the manifestation to remove it from play. (Magic, 7.2) battle proГЇВ¬ВЃle: Important information about a unit that you will need to refer to if you wish to include it in your army, such as its points value and minimum unit size. (Army Composition, 1.3) battle round: Te battle lasts a number of battle rounds speciГЇВ¬ВЃed in the battleplan. Each battle round has 2 turns (1 for each player), and each turn has 7 phases. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 11.0) battle tactics: Attempt by using the Гўв‚¬ЛњTactical GambitГўв‚¬ ability at the start of your turn. Complete at the end of your turn for 4 victory points. (Battle Tactics, 1.0) battle trait: A type of faction rule that usually aГЇВ¬в‚¬ects your army as a whole and reГЇВ¬вЂљects the way your faction does battle, as opposed to individual units. battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld: Te ГЇВ¬вЂљat surface on which the battle is fought. Usually has 2 short edges and 2 long edges, which may collectively be referred to as Гўв‚¬Лњthe battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld edgeГўв‚¬. It is divided into 4 large quarters, each of which is divided into 4 small quarters (eГЇВ¬в‚¬ectively sixteenths). battleplan: Te set of rules that describes the scenario of the battle and determines things such as where the armies deploy, where any objectives are located and how the players win the battle. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 9.0) begins deployment: Te player who is allowed to use a Deploy ability during deployment ГЇВ¬ВЃrst is said to be the one who begins deployment. Te battleplan will specify which player does so. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 10.0) behind a terrain feature: Impossible to draw a line from a model in the attacking unit to a model in the target unit without it passing across the terrain feature. (Terrain, 1.1) Cavalry: A unit keyword. Mounted on warbeasts. Cavalry are fast and hard-hitting but are generally vulnerable to attritional combat. Champion: ModelГўв‚¬s weapons have +1 to Attacks characteristic. (Command Models, 1.0) Charge: Your Charge Phase, charge roll of 2D6. Must get within Г‚ВЅ" of any visible enemy units to succeed, otherwise unit stays where it is. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 14.3) Tis glossary is an alphabetised list of rules deГЇВ¬ВЃnitions. It is intended to be a useful resource if you need a quick reminder of what a rule does or a short summary of a game term. It is not intended as an exhaustive list of precise deГЇВ¬ВЃnitions Гўв‚¬" if you need the full rule, refer to the relevant rules module. If there are any contradictions between this glossary and a rules module, the rules module takes precedence. 59

Charge (+1 Damage): Weapon ability. +1 Damage if attacking unit charged. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) climb: Movement vertically up or down the surface of a terrain feature. Te modelГўв‚¬s base is assumed to stay in contact with the terrain feature as it moves and remain parallel with the battleГЇВ¬ВЃeld ГЇВ¬вЂљoor. (Core Rules, 15.2) coherency: When a unit is set up or ends a move, each model in the unit must be within coherency range. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 15.1) coherency range: Г‚ВЅ" horizontally and any distance vertically of 1 or more other models if unit has 6 or fewer models; 2 or more if unit has 7+ models. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 15.1) combat attack: An attack made with a melee weapon. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 16.0) combat range: ModelГўв‚¬s combat range = 3" horizontally and any distance vertically from model. UnitГўв‚¬s combat range = 3" horizontally and any distance vertically from all models in unit. A unit is in combat if any enemy models are within its combat range. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 7.0) command points: Each player gets 4 command points at start of round. Underdog gets 1 extra. (Commands, 1.0) commander: You are the commander of your army and everything in it (units, models, etc.). commands: Abilities that require you to spend a number of command points in order to use them. (Commands, 1.0) Companion: Weapon ability. Weapon doesnГўв‚¬t get bonuses from friendly abilities that aГЇВ¬в‚¬ect attacks. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) contesting: Models contest objectives within their combat range (3"). When determining objective control, the same unit cannot contest more than 1 objective at the same time; the player must pick 1 if more than 1 is within range. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 32.1) control: Determined at the start of the ГЇВ¬ВЃrst battle round and the end of each turn. Objectives are controlled by player that has the highest army control score for that objective. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 32.2) Control characteristic: Determines how good unit is at contesting objectives (see Гўв‚¬ЛњcontrolГўв‚¬). (CoreГ‚ Rules, 4.0) control score: A unitГўв‚¬s control score is determined by adding all the Control characteristics of models in the unit contesting the objective in question. An armyГўв‚¬s control score for that objective is determined by adding together all the control scores of units in the army contesting that objective. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 32.2) Core: Basic ability that can only be used by each unit once per phase, e.g. Move, Shoot, Fight. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.1) Counter-charge: Command, 2CP. Charge in enemy charge phase. (Commands, 5.0) Cover: -1 to hit rolls for attacks that target a unit behind or wholly on this terrain feature, unless unit charged or has Fly. (Terrain, 1.2) Covering Fire: Command, 1CP. Shoot in enemy shooting phase, but -1 to hit rolls and must target nearest enemy unit. (Commands, 4.0) Crit (2 Hits): Weapon ability. UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed hit roll of 6 scores 2 hits on target. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) Crit (Auto-wound): Weapon ability. UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed hit roll of 6 skips wound roll step. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) Crit (Mortal): Weapon ability. UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed hit roll of 6 inГЇВ¬вЂљicts an amount of mortal damage equal to the Damage characteristic of the weapon and the sequence ends. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 20.0) critical hit: UnmodiГЇВ¬ВЃed hit roll of 6. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.0) D3: Roll a D6 and halve the result, rounding up. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 2.2) damaged: A unit is damaged if it has any damage points currently allocated to it (i.e. if the damage points allocated to it were not enough to slay a model in the unit). Some abilities Heal damaged units. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 18.2) Damage characteristic: Determines number of damage points inГЇВ¬вЂљicted by successful attack. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.0) damage points: Damage points are inГЇВ¬вЂљicted by successful attacks and mortal damage. Damage points go into a damage pool ГЇВ¬ВЃrst, then they must be allocated one at a time to the target unit. Each time the number of damage points allocated to a unit equals its Health characteristic, a model in the unit is slain. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.0) damage pool: InГЇВ¬вЂљicted damage points are added to the targetГўв‚¬s damage pool until all damage points inГЇВ¬вЂљicted by the ability have been calculated. Ten, each of those damage points must be allocated one at a time to the target unit. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 17.0) Declare: Te step of every ability afer which players may use reactions but before the eГЇВ¬в‚¬ect is resolved. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 5.2) deployment phase: Phase in which players use Deploy abilities to deploy their armies. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 10.0) destroyed: A unit is destroyed when the last model in the unit is slain. (CoreГ‚ Rules, 18.4) 60
